observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all cash in bank accounts, and maintain the required subsidiary ledgers thereof.
observations.reason: (See related finding in Item 1, Part II of this report) | For CY 2024, the Municipal Accountant did not prepare the BRS for all bank accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Reconcile the amounts of PPE accounts recorded in the FS and lapsing schedule;

Coordinate with the Inventory Committee to investigate the whereabouts, condition, and accountability over the assets; and

Provide an allowance for depreciation on all depreciable assets or request for authority from COA to derecognize non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability.
observations.reason: For CY 2024, not all of the depreciable PPEs were provided with depreciation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following:

The Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain stock cards and supplies ledger cards, respectively, for issued inventories; and

The inventory committee to conduct the semestral physical count of inventories, and prepare a report thereon.
observations.reason: For CY 2024, an updated stock cards were not maintained. Nor was a physical count of inventories conducted within the year.
For CY 2024, RIS and SSMI were not utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the identified persons responsible or liable in the Notices of Suspension and Notices of Disallowances to settle the audit suspensions/ disallowances.
observations.reason: The balance of NS and ND as of December 31, 2024 remained at P8,727,775.98 and P197,801.40, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to optimize the use of its DRRM funds taking into consideration all the thematic areas as mandated under Republic Act No. 10121.
observations.reason: A significant balance of current year LDRRMF was transferred to the STF, and the prior years’ balances were not utilized.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

members of the GAD Focal Point System to ensure the implementation of GAD-related PPAs through proper gender mainstreaming or attribution of GAD fund to the programs, projects and activities of the LGU, to fully address the gender sensitive issues in the locality, and

request from the Sangguniang Bayan to formulate and pass a local GAD code or ordinances with the assistance of the LGU GFPS, to support the localization of Magna Carta of Women and to ensure that GAD is mainstreamed in local policy-making and integrated in local development plans.
observations.managementAction: The municipality already adopted a local GAD code.
observations.reason: The PPAs in the GPB of CY 2024 was either not implemented due to absence of fund, or less appropriation in the annual budget compared to the GPB.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant continue to regularly reconcile their cash records to totally
eliminate the differences.

Likewise, we recommended (a) the preparation and maintenance of complete
BRS and cashbook by the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer,
respectively; and (b) to ensure strict compliance with Section 14 of the MNGAS and
the applicable provision of the GAM for LGUs by preparing separate BRS for each
bank account maintained by the Municipality, regardless of transaction volume.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer supply the
schedule to the Accounting Office for the drawing of JEV and the set-up of RPT and
SET receivables at every beginning of the year based on the updated
RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain SLs for
RPT/SET receivables.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant cleanse all the dormant
accounts observing the guidelines and procedures in its proper disposition laid out in
COA Circular No. 2023-008; and (b) responsible officials submit the lacking
documents.

We recommended that the Head of Agency (HoA)/SDO and Municipal
Accountant, within seven (7) days from the release of the check, submit the DVs for
CA for confidential activities/projects with complete required supporting documents
to the COA ATL concerned in a sealed envelope with a visible label
“CONFIDENTIAL – For the COA ATL Only” and with a covering transmittal letter
enumerating the submissions.

We further recommended that the HoA/SDO continue to observe compliance
with the preparation and submission to ICFAU of liquidation reports supported with documentary
requirements.

We recommended that the (a) Inventory Committee comply with the reporting
in the RPCI of their physical count of inventories and submit it not later than July 31
and January 31 of each year; (b) Accounting Office and GSO keep a record of their
inventories through SLCs and SCs for inventory management; and (c) Accounting
Office base the JEV preparation for the recording of issuance of supplies and
materials from the SSMI prepared by the GSO.

We reiterated our recommendation that the (a) Inventory Committee carry
out complete inventory of PPE, report it on RPCPPE by type, ensuring all the needed
details are included and thereafter, submit a copy to COA within the specified period;
(b) GSO (i) maintain all property records for all PPE; and (ii) renew and update the
LTO registration of motor vehicles, as warranted; and (c) LCE, Municipal
Accountant, Municipal Engineer and GSO adhere with Local Roads Asset
Management System so that all vital data on roads and its road components for every
road network are reflected in pertinent reports and records.

Moreover, we recommended that the (a) LCE, Accounting Office, GSO and
Inventory Committee promptly institute the required courses of action for the proper
recognition and disposition of found at station and non-existing/missing PPEs/items;
and (b) Municipal Accountant (i) observe the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2015-
008 and 2020-006 about the disclosure in the NFS of necessary information on total
road network system and PPEs found at station/non-existing/missing; and (ii) keep a
LC for each of the recorded CIP.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant account for the employee
leave benefits such that a liability is recognized as they are earned; (b) Municipal
Budget Officer establish an Employee Benefits Reserve Fund to fund for all the leave
benefits owed to employees; and (c) Municipal Human Resource Management Officer
(MHRMO) have a collaborative effort with the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Budget Officer for the strict adherence to Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules
Implementing Book V of E.O. No. 292 on the accounting and funding of leave benefits.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i)
closely review the recorded transactions in accordance with the revised chart of
accounts for LGUs prescribed by COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1,
2015; and (ii) rectify the misstatements noted in the accounting records; and (b)
involved office/s to submit the documentation deficiencies to the Audit Team.

We also recommended that, before entering into contract with the winning
bidder, the PE ascertain that the CSHP is approved by the DOLE, as part of the
contract and agreement between the Municipality and the contractor.

We recommended that the LCE (a) ensure that the LDRRMFIP is formulated,
reviewed, updated annually and approved by taking into account all available
funding sources for the execution of their DRRM-related PPAs; (b) stop the granting
of CA unless allowed under COA Circular No. 97-002 and at the same time adhere to
the declared policies of R.A. No. 9184 relative to procurement; and (c) require the
personnel concerned to submit the documents found to be unattached to the DVs, as
noted.

We reiterated our recommendation that the GFPS (a) pursue the
implementation of well-planned targets in the GPB so that the gender issues indicated
therein could be fully addressed; (b) sex-disaggregate/quantify the targets and
outcomes, as necessary; (c) constitute the GFPS Execom and M&E Team in keeping
with Items 4.1.A.2 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; and (d)
submit the GPB within the period set in COA Circular No. 2014-001.

In addition, we recommended that the GFPS formally adjust and
communicate all modifications to the originally endorsed GPB, and subsequently
provide COA with a copy of the revised GPB along with the acknowledgment receipt
from the concerned DILG office.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Mayor consider augmenting the
manpower complement of the Municipal Treasurer; (b) Municipal Treasurer
exercise its authority to yearly examine the books of accounts and other pertinent
records of businesses as this can produce additional revenue to fund essential PPAs
of the LGU; and (c) Business Permit and Licensing Officer (BPLO) (i) ascertain that
business permit applicants duly comply with the submission of applicable
documentary requirements and maintain the documents in their file folders; and (ii)
adopt the Electronic LGU - Integrated Business Permit and Licensing System (eLGU-
iBPLS) being offered by the Department of Information and Communications
Technology.

We recommended that the (a) MHRMO ensure that (i) the DTRs are properly
accomplished; (ii) the number of days to be paid in the payroll are consistent with the
number of days worked in the DTR; and (iii) all JO personnel are issued duly
approved contracts prior to the commencement of services, regardless of when they
are engaged; and (b) Municipal Accountant ensure that salary payments to
previously referred personnel are supported with complete documentation taking into
account the discussed observations.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant book the 20% LGU
share from Fire Code Revenues in the GF; and (b) LCE subject its utilization under
the process of regular appropriation through the approval of local Sanggunian.

We recommended that (a) Management continue the practice of withholding
and remittance of taxes and contributions/other payables due to the BIR, GSIS,
HDMF and PHIC within the prescribed period; and (b) Municipal Accountant and
MHRMO continue their coordination with the involved agencies for the
reconciliation of records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Analyze the Accounts Payable outstanding for more than two years and revert to
the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund those that to be with no actual
claims, administrative or judicial, or which are not covered with perfected
contracts on record; and

b. Conduct periodic review and monitoring of the Accounts Payable so the account
will consist only of valid claims against the Municipal Government.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
verify the validity of payables aged more than two years and send confirmation
letters for balances with identified claimants or creditors.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
reclassify the amount of P1,063,870.61, representing the 20 percent LGU share from
BFP Fire Code fees, from the Due to NGAs account to the appropriate Trust
Liabilities account in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs under
COA Circular 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Human Resource
Management Officer and Municipal Accountant to:

a. Strictly comply with Section 56 of General Provisions of the GAA for FY 2024 on
the mandated NTHP of not less than P5,000.00;

b. Adopt stricter policy and internal controls to monitor loan accommodation and
other payroll deductions to ensure that employees can afford the loan repayment
without falling below the minimum required net pay threshold;

c. Evaluate the necessity of coordination with lending institutions on the possible
implementation of seeking pre-approval first from the MHRMO or the MAO before
accommodating loan applications from the Municipality’s employees; and

d. Establish coordination with lending institutions to ensure that they promptly
inform the MAO upon the approval of loan applications, thereby enabling timely
verification of Net Take-Home Pay compliance before the processing of payroll
deductions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal
Accountant, to strictly enforce the salary deduction on executed promissory notes
to resolve delays in liquidation.

Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to strictly enforce compliance on the grant, utilization and liquidation of
cash advances, in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. Municipal General Services Officer – Designate and the Municipal Engineer
assess the status of projects linked to Guaranty/Security Deposit Payable
accounts that have remained outstanding for over a year from the date of
retention and verify whether the suppliers have fully complied with the terms and
conditions stipulated in their respective contracts; and

b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to require the concerned
suppliers, after the lapse of the warranty period, to secure the necessary
documents needed to process the refund of their guaranty deposit, in compliance
with Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009, Section 62.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR
of RA No. 9184 and Section 6.2 of Annex E of the same Revised IRR, to return the
suppliers and contractors’ retention money.

In addition, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to:

a. Implement a monitoring system to flag retention balances approaching or exceeding
their allowable holding periods to ensure timely actions for retention money release;
and

b. Strictly monitor the retention money and comply with the provisions of Annex B of
COA Circular No. 2015-009, Section 62.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and
Section 6.2 of Annex E of the same Revised IRR.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator, send a written formal follow-up letter to the
DENR to:

a. Confirm receipt and evaluation of the submitted CMRs and SMRs; and

b. Request an update on the status of the CDO, including any further actions
required from the Municipality for its lifting.

Henceforth, we recommended that Management comply with all provisions of all
issued ECC to prevent recurrence of project suspensions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to:

a. coordinate with the concerned landowners, with regard to surveying/titling and
purchase of lot, to facilitate the completion and submission of all required legal
documents. In cases where the landowners are unable or unwilling to provide the
necessary documents within a reasonable period, the Municipality should
consider identifying alternative properties or reprogram the funds to other priority
development projects;

b. follow up and coordinate with the concerned landowners for the Pathway Project to
secure the Deed of Donation necessary for the legal transfer of the land intended for the
pathway project. If the donation cannot be finalized within a reasonable timeframe,
Management should consider identifying an alternative site or reprogramming the
allocated funds;

c. assess the necessity of the repair and maintenance of roads funded under the
continuing appropriation. If found to be no longer necessary or feasible, the
Municipality should initiate the reprogramming of the unused funds; and

d. perform complete/adequate detailed engineering activities to ensure that issues
such as unavailability of electricity for power tools and equipment are properly
addressed during the preliminary engineering study before project
implementation.

Furthermore, we recommended that Management expedite and regularly monitor the
implementation of all PPAs funded under continuing appropriations for 20 percent
DF to ensure timely utilization of the fund and achieve the desirable socio-economic
development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer and Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from
incurring and charging expenses that do not fall within the four thematic areas of
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Chairperson of GFPS to
submit GAD AR for CY 2024 and ensure that GPB and its corresponding
Accomplishment Report are submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed
period, for timely review and audit of GAD fund.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee responsible for conducting the semi-annual physical count of the Municipal Government inventories and preparing RPCI for all inventory accounts at the end of every semester for submission to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year pursuant to the provision of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; b. GSO-Designate (i) maintain stock cards for each type of inventory pursuant to Sections 114 and 119 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; (ii) maintain the required forms and reports for semi-expendable properties pursuant to COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022 and COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024 on the forms and reports in monitoring the receipt and issuance of Semi-Expendable Properties. (iii) regularly reconcile the RPCI to the accounting records to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the Inventory accounts; and c. Municipal Accountant submit the JEV and the corresponding supporting documents for the inventories held for manufacturing, and the signed documents for the two disbursement vouchers noted by the Audit Team, for audit purposes.

We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Mayor (i) create an Inventory Committee responsible for conducting the physical count of the Municipal Government properties and preparing the RPCPPE, RPCLRN and RPCBA, in accordance with the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II; (ii) review the staffing pattern of the Municipal Government to ensure proper segregation of duties, preventing any individual from having simultaneous access to financial transactions and custody of assets, and make appropriate corrective action as necessary; and (iii) consider availing the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances as provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to come up with a reliable PPE balance; (b) General Services Officer – Designate (i) prepare and maintain complete property records and reports for PPE and Biological Asset accounts pursuant to the Sections 114 and 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015; (ii) ensure that the RPCPPE, RPCLRN and RPCBA are accomplished in accordance with the provision of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015; and (iii) prepare and maintain Report of Local Road Network, local road inventory and road map pursuant to Sections VI(1) and VII(1) of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015; (c) General Services Officer- Designate and Municipal Engineer identify the concerned components of the road and road identification number pursuant to the LRAM Manual as prescribed by DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2020-155 dated November 16, 2020; (d) Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer- Designate or Inventory Committee, as the case may be, coordinate with each other and prepare supporting schedules to facilitate the reconciliation of the property accounts and establishment of the actual existence, ownership, and condition of the properties; (e) Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and General Services Office with the complete description and cost segregation, and the total number of kilometers and the corresponding amount of the Municipality's road networks; (f) Municipal Accountant ensure proper disclosure of Road Network System as prescribed in Item VI (2) of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015; and (g) Municipal Agriculturist (i) ensure that the breeding stocks are properly tagged for easy identification; and (ii) submit the required notice of loss and request for relief from accountability for the death of 155 breeding stocks totaling ₱89,410.00 pursuant to Item 1 of COA Memorandum No. 92-751 dated February 24, 1992.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Treasurer submit the Consolidated Report on Accountability for Accountable Forms and its supporting documents within five days of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 51 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II; and b. General Services Officer – Designate and Designated Inspection Officer verify the actual specification of the delivered items that the actual specification of the delivered items were indicated in the delivered items is indicated

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Treasurer (i) devise various strategies and activities, such as widening the issuance of Notice of Delinquencies, assigning more personnel in delivering the notice of delinquencies, with specific instructions to serve notices to actual owner of the property or the actual occupants and institute follow up actions in case of failure to reply, and comprehensive tax information campaign, to facilitate effective revenue collection; (ii) coordinate with the Provincial Treasurer on the implementation of RA No. 12001 and ensure that eligible taxpayers are informed of the amnesty to potentially reduce the level of delinquent accounts; and (iii) furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with a certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year for the establishment of RPT Receivables/SET Receivables based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card; b. OIC-Municipal Assessor conduct field verifications and physical inspections of properties, update the tax maps and assessment rolls accordingly, correct identified errors such as double assessments or tax-exempt properties (e.g., road lots) being assessed and coordinate regularly with the Municipal Treasurer to enhance the reliability and accuracy of real property tax records and support effective revenue collection; c. Municipal Accountant ensure that receivables for the setting-up of RPT and SET are based on the certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year from the Municipal Treasurer; and d. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant transfer the share of the barangays of the Municipality from real property tax collection within five days after the end of each quarter pursuant to Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer identify the person/s responsible for the late payment of the electric bill and cause the refund of the payment of surcharges totaling ₱1,591.94.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer monitor and require the contractor to take corrective actions on the defects noted in the project pursuant to Section 62.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and apprise the Audit Team on the actions taken thereto; otherwise, demand payment of ₱4,437.60, equivalent to the defects noted.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Mayor, Bids and Awards Committee, and other concerned Municipal Officials submit justification with corroboratory documents on the noted observations in the implementation of the Construction of Proposed Two-Storey Isolation Facility with Roof Deck (Design and Build Scheme); b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer demand payment or institute appropriate action to collect the excess contract cost of ₱1.745 million and liquidated damages of ₱1.615 million from the contractor; c. Municipal Accountant, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipal Engineer, and BAC Chairperson submit the lacking documents relative to the planning, procurement, and implementation of the project; and d. Municipal Treasurer cause the refund of the excess payment made to the contractor amounting to ₱2,411.93 over the revised contract cost.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Mayor (i) adhere to the guidelines outlined in DBM-DILG-DOF JC No. 2015-01 dated February 12, 2015, as amended by DBM-DILG-DOF JC No. 1, s. 2023 dated April 17, 2023, by ensuring that the responsibility for identifying, implementing, and utilizing LGU funds remains with the LCE and designated local officials; (ii) comply with the reporting requirements outlined in DBM LBC Nos. 150, 151, and 154 ensuring the timely submission of written notices to the designated officials and committees; (iii) instruct the GSO, as the Property Officer, to accept all deliveries as mandated under Section 114 of Rule 15 and MNGAS for LGUs; and (iv) constitute an Inspection Officer/Committee to oversee the proper inspection and verification of all procured goods; b. MPDC (i) secure formal extension approval from DOST and (ii) coordinate with the relevant offices to facilitate the electrical system upgrade to ensure the timely operation of the shredder and the effective implementation of the project; c. Municipal Budget Officer (i) require offices concerned to align their budget requests with the identified MFOs under the AIP to prevent misclassification of appropriations; and (ii) ensure that all budget proposals are supported by a PPMP; d. OIC-MSWDO (i) ensure that the procurement of food items strictly complies with the provisions of the MOA and relevant procurement laws; and (ii) seek guidance from the DSWD to align procurement practices with applicable regulations while maintaining efficient fund utilization; e. Municipal Accountant ensure compliance with Item 4.8 of DBM LBC Nos. 151 and 154 by properly segregating and recording LGSF-SBDP NTF-ELCAC funds in separate subsidiary ledgers for each beneficiary barangay and its corresponding project(s); f. Municipal Accountant and Treasurer (i) adhere to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and BTr Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009 by limiting cash advances to duly designated and bonded disbursing officers and (ii) implement measures to secure and verify updated tax clearances prior to the final settlement of payments for all government contracts procured through public bidding, in line with BIR Revenue Regulations No. 017-2024; g. BAC Chairperson comply with the notification timeline provided under the relevant provisions of RA No. 9184 as amended; and h. Municipal Accountant, BAC Chairperson and other concerned offices submit all the lacking documents for audit and other purposes.

We recommended that the: a. Local Development Council, Implementing Office concerned and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (i) ensure that PPAs to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready with specific target date of implementation, and (ii) assess and identify issues relevant to timely implementation of PPAs to ensure its completion; and (ii) long outstanding unimplemented PPAs totaling ₱.486 million which is not in conformance with Section 3.2.3 of DILG-DOF-DBM JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020; b. Municipal Budget Officer (i) ensure that 20% of the updated NTA provided by the DBM thru their Local Budget Memoranda be appropriated fully to 20% Development Fund in accordance with Section 287 of RA No. 7160; (ii) classify the payment of amortization and other fees under the appropriate nature of expenditure – capital outlay and financial expenses in the budget; and (iii) align appropriations for loan repayment with the actual repayment schedule issued by the lending institution, allowing for a reasonable buffer to accommodate potential changes in interest rates.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with a certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year for the establishment of Real Property Tax Receivables/ Special Education Tax Receivables based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card;

We recommended that the: a. Supply Officer III (i) maintain stock cards for all inventories carried in stock by the Municipal Government pursuant to Section 114 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015; b. Inventory Committee (i) prepare the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) for all inventory accounts at the end of every semester and submit it to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year. (ii) Moreover, reconcile the RPCI to the accounting records to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the Inventory Accounts; and e. Municipal Accountant (i) submit the JEV and the corresponding supporting documents to the Audit Team for audit purposes.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant and Inventory Committee coordinate with each other and prepare supporting schedules to facilitate the reconciliation of the property accounts and establishment of the actual existence, ownership, and condition of the properties; b. Inventory Committee provide justification on the noted observation on the submitted RPCPPE; and c. Supply Officer III (i) prepare and maintain appropriate property records and reports for PPE accounts in accordance with Sections 114 and 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015; (ii) prepare and maintain Report of Local Road Network, local road inventory and road map pursuant to Sections VI(1) and VII(1) of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015; and (iii) identify the components of the road and road identification number pursuant to the LRAM Manual as prescribed by DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2020-155 dated November 16, 2020; and d. Municipal Accountant ensure proper disclosure of Road Network System as prescribed in Item VI (2) of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant review and examine thoroughly the claims of Municipal officials and employees so that claims for traveling expenses either through cash advances or reimbursement will not result in overpayment in excess of the rates indicated in EO No. 77; b. Municipal Agriculturist submit the monitoring and evaluation of the Rice Productivity Enhancement Program to ascertain the effectiveness of the Fuel Subsidy Program of the Municipal Government; and c. Municipal officials and employees strictly observe the prescribed rules and regulations outlined in EO No. 77 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 and other related guidelines to ensure that government funds are properly and legally spent for its specified purpose.

We recommended that the: a. Supply Officer III ensure that equipment of the Municipal Government are provided with proper storage facilities and to process the repair of the rear side near the tail light of the purchased motor vehicle (SUV) using the vehicle’s comprehensive insurance; b. Municipal Engineer closely monitor the condition of all infrastructure projects and initiate corrective actions as needed to prevent further deterioration; and c. Municipal Accountant to demand payment of the liquidated damages of ₱15,992.00 from the supplier of the Two Units Mini Dump Trucks for the delay incurred in the delivery of equipment procured.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant (i) submit all the lacking documents for audit purposes and (ii) provide justification on the delay in the submission of paid disbursement vouchers and ensure that paid disbursement vouchers are submitted to the Audit Team within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009; b. Municipal Mayor require the concerned Municipal Department Heads to submit justification with supporting documents on the noted observations in the procurement and loan transactions relative to the parcels of land totaling ₱53.319 million; and c. Municipal Officials avoid reimbursement as a mode of payment.

We recommended that the: a. BAC and BAC Secretariat ensure compliance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and related GPPB issuances; and b. Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairperson submit justification on the noted observations in the procurement and implementation of the projects and submit all the lacking supporting documents in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and ensure that for audit purposes all lacking documents are submitted.

We recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees submit justification and corroboratory documents on the noted observations in the procurement and implementation of the projects and the liquidation of funds received in compliance with the pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and the Memoranda of Agreement and relevant guidelines on utilization of funds and other observations.

We recommended that the: (i) prepare the annual progress report for CY 2023 on the strategies implemented, as well as the accomplishments, as required by the NSWMC in its Resolution No. 1103, series of 2018 and submit the same to the NSWMC, and ensure that targets and accomplishments be presented in the said report; (ii) provide the necessary support in urging the remaining barangays in establishing their own MRF and conduct continuous information and education campaign activities and programs/activities to achieve the goals of the SWMP; and (iii) collaborate with the Liga ng mga Barangay to actively mobilize barangay officials in implementing the SWM program at the grassroots level such as educating their constituents on the proper segregation of reusable, recyclable, and compostable waste, promoting the utilization of established MRFs, and reinforcing compliance with the mandatory segregation at source as prescribed under RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the: a. MDRRM Officer (i) indicate the MFOs, PIs and targets for the PPAs to be funded in the budget year; (ii) submit the distribution list of all issued welfare goods during CY 2023 and the document showing the purpose of distribution; (iii) provide explanation on the noted observations in the issuance of welfare goods in CY 2023; (iv) ensure compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of Local Disaster Risk Management Fund; and (v) monitor and facilitate the implementation of the PPAs under the MDRRM Fund. b. Members of the Local Finance Committee evaluate all budget proposals as part of the budget preparation using the criteria provided in the BOM; c. MDRRM Officer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (i) provide justification on the observation on the utilization of the CY 2022 MDRRMF in Special Trust Fund; and d. BAC Chairperson provide justification on the procurement of PPAs under MDRRMF and ensure compliance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the: a. BAC strictly adhere to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and related GPPB issuances; b. Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairperson submit justification on the noted observations in the procurement and implementation of the projects and submit all the lacking supporting documents in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and ensure that lacking documents be submitted for audit purposes.

We recommended that the MENRO-Designate provide proof of distribution of the cavans of rice used for the Establishment of Redemption Centers/ Drop-off Centers and submit supporting documents to support proper classification and recording in the appropriate accounts.

We recommended that the: a. Inventory Committee provide justification on the noted observation on the submitted RPCPPE and ensure that the RPCPPE is properly filled-up with proper data; b. Municipal Accountant provide justification on the difference noted for the disposal of the Construction and Heavy Equipment and Motor Vehicles and make appropriate adjustment if warranted; c. Supply Officer III prepare and maintain appropriate property records and reports for PPE accounts in accordance with Sections 114 and 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015; (ii) prepare and maintain Report of Local Road Network, local road inventory and road map pursuant to Sections VI(1) and VII(1) of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015; and (iii) identify the components of the road and road identification number pursuant to the LRAM Manual as prescribed by DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2020-155 dated November 16, 2020; and d. Municipal Accountant ensure proper disclosure of Road Network System as prescribed in Item VI (2) of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant review and examine thoroughly the claims of Municipal officials and employees so that claims for traveling expenses either through cash advance or reimbursement will be submitted timely and properly, ensuring compliance with Section 107 of PD No. 1445 and Section 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006; and b. MDRRMO ensure compliance with the provision of Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of Local DRRM Fund.

We recommended that the: a. Supply Officer III ensure that equipment of the Municipal Government are provided with proper storage facilities and process necessary repairs; b. Municipal Engineer closely monitor the condition of all infrastructure projects and initiate corrective actions as needed; and c. Municipal Accountant to demand payment of the liquidated damages of ₱15,992.00 from the supplier of the Two Units Mini Dump Trucks for the delay incurred in the delivery of equipment procured.

We recommended that the: a. BAC and BAC Secretariat ensure compliance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and related GPPB issuances; b. Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairperson submit justification on the noted observations in the procurement and implementation of the projects and submit all the lacking supporting documents in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and c. Municipal Mayor instruct concerned Municipal Officials to provide evidence of full compliance with the posting and reporting guidelines provided in DBM LBC No. 131 dated January 8, 2021 relative to the utilization of LGSF for FY 2021.

We recommended that the: (i) prepare the annual progress report for CY 2022 on the strategies implemented and accomplishments and submit to NSWMC as required; (ii) provide the necessary support in urging the remaining barangays in establishing their own MRF and conduct IEC campaigns; and (iii) MSWMB to provide necessary technical support and assignment of personnel to barangays for SWM implementation.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and concerned implementing office provide specific target dates of implementation in the Annual Investment Plan of the Municipality; b. Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Budget Officer, and BAC Chairperson submit justification on the observation in the procurement and implementation of the projects and submit all lacking documents in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001, and pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant ensure that monthly financial transactions are submitted within the period prescribed in Section 107 of PD No. 1445 and Section 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009; and b. MDRRMO ensure compliance with the provisions in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of Local DRRM Fund.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Mayor direct the MPDC to instruct each office of the MGV to submit sex and age-disaggregated data and other relevant data for the completion of the GAD database; b. prioritize the attendance of the GFPS members and TWG to various GAD-related trainings and seminars; c. instruct the GFPS-TWG to ensure submission of GAD AR within the prescribed period under Section V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 and submit the GAD AR supporting documents pursuant to Section 4.C.8 of the JMC; and d. instruct the lead offices to prepare the IRR of the GAD Code, as amended, to effectively implement the provisions contained therein. Additionally, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer should assign separate RC and RC codes for GFPS that will serve as the RC for GAD-related expenses pursuant to COA Circular No. 2021-008 dated September 6, 2021.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant and GSO assess and ensure that inventories are recorded using the perpetual inventory method and maintain stock cards and SSMI; b. GSO reconcile the RPCI to the accounting records; and c. Municipal Accountant submit supporting JEVs and documentation for the recording of distribution and consumption of inventories.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant and GSO submit proof that the NGAs’ PPEs are properly recorded in the books of the recipient agencies and construction materials are used in accordance with the intended purpose; and b. GSO adhere to prescribed inventory accounting and record donated assets accordingly.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant and GSO coordinate to reconcile and prepare complete RPCPPE and RPCLRN; b. Supply Officer III prepare and maintain property records and required reports for PPE accounts; c. Municipal Accountant ensure proper disclosure of the Road Network System; and d. Restate the CY 2023 financial statements in comparison with the CY 2024 balances pursuant to Paragraphs 46 and 47 of IPSAS 3.

We recommended that the: a. BAC strictly adhere to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and related GPPB issuances; b. Municipal Accountant and other concerned employees submit the lacking supporting documents required under Item 9.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001; and c. BAC Secretariat and BAC Chairperson ensure compliance with posting and documentation requirements and provide necessary documents for audit purposes.

We recommended that the: a. MDRRM Officer provide justification for appropriation of MDRRM Fund less than five percent and provide explanation on the non-appropriation of IRA amounts; b. MDRRM Council and MDRRM Officer provide justification for utilization of funds and ensure compliance with procurement guidelines in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; c. Local Finance Committee submit proof of full compliance in reporting and posting requirements for the funds received from the national government relative the DRRM as provided in DBM LBCs; and d. BAC Chairperson and BAC Secretariat provide justifications on procurement out of the LDRRMF and ensure compliance with IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Assessor relative to the unrecorded 44 parcels of land and make appropriate adjustment in the books of accounts and provide the Audit Team with the copy of adjustments, if any; and ensure that all properties owned be recorded in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and Designated General Services Officer to cause the transfer of the title of the 30 parcels of land in the name of the Municipal Government of Victoria.

We recommended that the BAC and BAC Secretariat ensure compliance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and related GPPB issuances; Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairperson submit justification on the noted observations in the procurement and implementation of the projects and submit all the lacking supporting documents in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and Municipal Mayor instruct concerned Municipal Officials to provide evidence of full compliance with the posting and reporting guidelines provided in DBM LBC No. 131 dated January 8, 2021 relative to the utilization of LGSF for FY 2021.

We recommended that the BAC and BAC Secretariat ensure compliance with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and related issuances in the conduct of pre-bid conference and submission of required post-qualification documents; Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairperson submit the necessary justification and lacking documents in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the MENRO-Designate prepare the annual progress reports for CY 2020 and submit the same to NSWMC and ensure implementation of MRFs and compliance with NSWMC requirements including obtaining discharge permit from EMB for the SLF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the GFPS ensure submission of sex-disaggregated data and other GAD requirements; prioritize GFPS training attendance; require GFPS-TWG submission of GAD AR and supporting documents; prepare IRR of GAD Code and assign separate RC codes for GAD-related expenses as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2021-008.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Assessor request for sufficient fund for the reconstruction of updated tax maps, and devise strategies to improve tax collection efficiency including coordinated field operations and information campaigns.

We recommended that the BAC and BAC Secretariat submit the necessary justification and documents for audit purposes; officials and employees concerned provide satisfactory explanation on the noted inconsistencies and ensure compliance with procurement timelines and the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and Municipal Accountant assess and demand the supplier liquidated damages for delayed delivery of the hammer mill single stage.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the Sangguniang Bayan and concerned Municipal officials make appropriate and immediate action to address or resolve the identified issues and/or fast track the implementation of programmed developmental projects and ensure compliance with JMC No. 1 and COA Circular No. 2013-004 in the conduct of information and publicity for PPAs.

We recommended that the BAC submit satisfactory explanation on the noted observations and strictly adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and provide the needed supporting documents for audit purposes.

We recommended that the: a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit satisfactory explanation on the noted observations and strictly adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 for the Concreting of Road at Alialy Street, Poblacion, Victoria, Oriental Mindoro; and b. Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairperson submit the necessary documents for audit purposes and ensure compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the BAC and BAC Secretariat submit satisfactory explanation on the noted observations and strictly adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairperson submit the necessary documents for audit purposes and comply with COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal SWM Board through the MENRO-Designate submit the copy of signed MOU to the Audit Team and ensure compliance with the conditions on use and safekeeping of the mobile shredder and chipper; and ensure strict implementation of RA No. 9003 provisions on segregation, MRF establishment and securing required permits for SLF operation.

We recommended that the MSB evaluate the PPAs in the SEF Budget and ensure compliance with the guidelines relative to SEF utilization; submit the lacking supporting documents and billing statements; and avoid advance payments and ensure SEF funds are used for priority education-related expenses.

We recommended that the concerned officials and employees provide satisfactory explanation on the noted inconsistencies with the purpose indicated in the SEF Budget and ensure compliance with the procurement provisions of RA No. 9184; and submit required supporting documents for audit.

We recommended that the BAC and BAC Secretariat ensure compliance with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and related issuances; the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairperson submit the necessary justification and lacking documents in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; and provide explanations on all noted observations for audit purposes.

We recommended that the BAC and BAC Secretariat submit satisfactory explanation on the noted observations and strictly adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 for the Concreting of Road at Alialy Street, Poblacion, Victoria, and that the Municipal Accountant submit the necessary documents for audit purposes.

We recommended that the: a. BAC and BAC Secretariat submit satisfactory explanation on the noted observations and strictly adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and b. Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairperson submit the necessary documents for audit purposes.

We recommended that an updated project report covering the whole period of operation be rendered by the concerned Municipal and MinSCAT officials/employees. Records, particularly subsidiary ledgers, showing the construction costs of these capital assets, a clear financial report on the results of the project operation and inventory reports should be produced for further audit and any balance of cash should be turned over to the Municipality while the fixed assets should be transferred to MinSCAT where they have been erected; and appropriate accounting entries should be made to close the erroneous Investment account. Formal communication will be made to the President of MinSCAT regarding this matter.

1.16 We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to:

a. File a complaint ticket and upload the backup database system
currently being used by the City, together with the List of PPEs showing
differences in the accumulated depreciation computation, as advised by
COA GAS-ASDOSO; and

b. Closely monitor the accumulated depreciation computations of the PPE
accounts with identified differences, and verify whether corrections or
adjustments have been made.

2.12 We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to:

a. Review the list of PPE items with no system-generated depreciation and
file a complaint ticket with the COA GAS – ASDOSO for proper
solution; and

b. Closely monitor the affected PPE accounts and make adjusting entries,
as appropriate, for fair presentation in the financial statements.

3.13 We recommended and the Management agreed to require the:

a. City Engineer to regularly furnish the City Accountant with the
periodic monitoring or status/accomplishment report of the ongoing
projects of the City for recording in the CIP accounts;

b. City Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to update the balances of
the CIP accounts based on the actual status or physical
accomplishments, as indicated in the periodic monitoring or
status/accomplishment report of the ongoing projects of the City
Engineer; and

c. City Engineer and City Accountant to henceforth have adequate
coordination and regular monitoring of all ongoing projects of the City,
to ensure correct and complete CIP balances in the books.

4.11 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to:

a. Direct the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical count
on all inventory items and to reconcile any variances noted with the
Accounting Office; and

b. Consider the augmentation of additional personnel to the Inventory
Committee to assist in the conduct of the physical inventory and
reconciliation of its results with the appropriate records.

5.10 We recommended and the City Accountant, City Mayor and City HRMO
agreed that:

The City Accountant shall:

a. Prepare adjusting entries to correct the balances of the Due to Officers
and Employees and Government Equity accounts in the amount of
₱11.000 million;

b. Review and analyze the Unreconciled ledger balances of the Due to GSIS
and Due to Officers and Employees accounts, and reconcile the same;

c. Review the current processes and procedures for recording the dues and
remittances to GSIS and assess whether changes or system enhancements
are needed;
The City Mayor will:

d. If warranted, create a team/task force to hasten the reconciliation of
unreconciled balances with individual ledger accounts to ensure the
correctness of the accounts and avoid the accumulation of unreconciled
balances that may affect the members’ retirement/life claims, loan
proceeds and other benefits;
The City HRMO shall:

e. Immediately provide the required or lacking documentation and other
pertinent information to GSIS, and ensure close monitoring to facilitate
the prompt updating of individual records to avoid delays in the
remittance of the withheld amounts to GSIS; and
The City Accountant and City HRMO will:

f. Henceforth, ensure that all amounts withheld from employees’ salaries
and wages for GSIS contributions are remitted in full within the first ten
(10) days of the calendar month following the month to which the
contributions apply, pursuant to Section 6 of RA No. 8291.

6.11 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to require the:

a. City Accountant to employ monitoring mechanisms that will facilitate the
timely determination of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF as of year-
end; and

b. City Accountant and City Treasurer to henceforth ensure that the
unexpended balance of LDRRMF for QRF and MOOE will be
immediately transferred to the TF.

7.18 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to direct the:

a. City Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to correct the erroneous
recording of Rent Income at ₱3.328 million from Hotel Operations in
the EEF instead of the General Fund, for an accurate depiction of the
results of operations and account balances;

b. City Accountant and City Treasurer to coordinate and reconcile their
respective records regarding the total difference of ₱8,500.00 noted in
the EEF books, to ensure correctness and reliability of their respective
records;

c. Officer-in-Charge of CEED to review and assess the operating
performance of the existing LEEs for appropriate action, to avoid the
recurrence of net losses and to achieve the goals of the economic
enterprises as financially self-sufficient and profitable entities; and

d. City Accountant and City Treasurer to coordinate regarding the
inclusion of the operations of the Victorias Hotel under the General
Fund, until it is established as a local economic enterprise thru an
appropriate ordinance.

8.14 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to direct the:

a. City Treasurer to generate/prepare and submit the RPT and SET due
and collectible for the year, based on the RPTARs/Taxpayer’s Index
Cards to the City Accountant;

b. City Accountant and concerned offices to provide assistance in the
preparation of the subsidiary ledgers for the RPT and SET Receivables
and Aging Schedule for taxpayers; and

c. City Treasurer to take appropriate actions to ensure the timely release
of RPT shares to barangays to finance programs, projects and
activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly and year-end Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts to arrive at the correct cash in bank balance as at December 31, 2024.  Also, we recommend that investigation be conducted on the two (2) accounts still carried in the books of the LGU but not confirmed by the depository bank.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, General Services Officer, and Municipal Engineer to comply with the Accounting and Reporting Procedures on Local Road Asset Management System pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to prepare a systematic and well-planned physical inventory count, which could be done in a year-round basis, until all the Property, Plant and Equipment of the municipality have been accounted for, and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Reconcile the amounts of PPE accounts recorded in the FS and lapsing schedule;
Coordinate with the Inventory Committee to identify and investigate the whereabouts, condition and accountability of these assets; and
Provide an allowance for depreciation on all depreciable assets identified and
request for authority from COA to derecognize non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability if any, pursuant to existing COA rules and regulations.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for all taxes withheld, and remit regularly to the BIR within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to review and reconcile the members’ data with GSIS in order to arrive at the correct amount of premium contributions to GSIS and be remitted accordingly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
The Municipal Engineer to submit to the Audit Team the necessary duly approved time extensions, and strictly monitor the timely implementation of the said projects; and
The Municipal Accountant to impose liquidated damages if the duly approved time extensions were not prepared and submitted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Assessor to closely monitor the site preparation for the immediate implementation of the Access Road in Brgy. Zone 1. Henceforth, ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following:
The Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal Budget Officer to transfer the current year unexpended QRF and MF- MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under Trust Liabilities-DRRM account; and
The LDRRMO to incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the balance of prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF with updated programs or activities, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following:
Formulate a gender-responsive budgeting framework to ensure that gender considerations are incorporated at LGU implemented projects.
Establish coordination between the GAD Focal person/Office and other departments concerned to ensure GAD integration into LGU funded PPAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Secretary to submit copies of approved contracts within five (5) days after their execution as provided for in COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2009-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following:
The Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursements thru e-Payments are based on a duly approved DVs/Payrolls; and
The Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit Daily Report of e-Payments from the Agency Account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Enjoin the Office of the Accounting and Treasury to reconcile their records regularly to avoid accumulation of variance; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to draw journal vouchers for valid reconciling items.
We recommended that the municipal accountant draw correcting entries for the reclassification.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to retrospectively recompute the Road Network Account, ensuring the exclusion of residual value in the depreciation expense calculation; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary correcting entries to update the Road Network Account balances, including Depreciation Expense, Prior Period Adjustments, and Accumulated Depreciation Accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to provide adequate disclosures regarding the nature and details of the prior period errors and the corresponding adjustments to net revenue, as reflected in the Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Equity for the two financial periods.

We hereby reiterate the prior year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the HRMO to promptly update the salary details of employees in the GSIS portal, ensuring that no delays occur during periods of salary increments;
Advise the Municipal Accountant and the HRMO to coordinate with GSIS for the reconciliation of unpaid premiums per individual account, and to secure the necessary supporting documents such as payroll records and service records in preparation for a face-to-face reconciliation; and
Confer with the GSIS management and concerned LGU employees to determine the most appropriate resolution for the unsettled premium balances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive make representations with the appropriate government agency requesting for an extension for the use of the unutilized funds; otherwise, consider the cancellation of the contract agreement with the contractor and return the undisbursed fund if both the extension and Environmental Compliance Certificate are not secured. Henceforth, refrain from implementing projects without first complying with the requirements prescribed by appropriate government agencies.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Program of Works (POW) on infrastructure projects prior to the approval of the Annual Investment Plan or in the 1st quarter of the budget year, to improve implementation efficiency;
Evaluate the need to augment manpower in the Municipal Engineer’s Office to assist in the preparation of Program of Works and other detailed engineering activities; and
Reprioritize balances of completed PPAs through vision-setting and consultation with various sectors.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive make proper charges out of the LDRRM fund, taking into consideration the guidelines regarding the allocation and utilization of the fund provided for in DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and other pertinent guidelines for LDRRMF for a more developed and improved disaster preparedness and response capabilities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Revise the existing contingency plans for landslides and armed conflict insurgency to incorporate mitigating measures in the anatomy of hazards; and
Enjoin the LDRRM Council to incorporate the resource gaps identified in the contingency plan into the LDRRMIP, thereby maximizing the local government unit’s disaster preparedness.

In light of the foregoing, we reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct all department heads to coordinate and aid the GFPS in providing the lacking information to generate a well-equipped GAD database; and
Require the GFPS to issue a compliance certificate confirming that the concerned department heads/participants have coordinated and provided the missing data for a well-equipped GAD database before attending a GAD planning session.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive as co-chairperson of the Local School Board:
Instruct the LSB Secretary to furnish, with proof of receipt, the Local Budget Officer, the Local Accountant, and the Local Treasurer a copy of the duly approved SEF Budget not later than seven (7) days after its approval;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to furnish a copy of the SEF Utilization Report to the LSB; and
Direct the LSB Secretary to submit quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B) on SEF utilization to the DepEd CO, through appropriate channels, copy furnished the local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG.
