observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management that before entering into a contract agreement, see to it
that all expenditures are covered by an appropriation ordinance both as to amount and
purpose for each of the departments/offices for which the appropriation was intended.

We also recommended that Management be directed to complete the documentary
requirements provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the garbage collection,
hauling and disposal services rendered to the Municipality amounting to
P4,626,676.00.

We recommended that Management:
a. observe Zonal/BIR Value and Market Value per Tax Declaration in the
acquisition of lot;
b. see to it that when acquiring an untitled lot, the landowner has posted an
indemnity or property bond whose amount is fixed by the Municipal Treasurer
concerned on the basis of the market value as determined by the Municipal
Assessor and as appearing on the current Tax Declaration of the property; and
c. initiate the enactment of a Municipal Ordinance providing procedures and
guidelines for the expeditious acquisition by the Municipality of private real
property, registered or unregistered, for development projects which include,
among others, the source of funds for the purpose.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure that detailed engineering activities are
sufficiently carried out on the basis of feasibility or preliminary engineering
study made which establishes the technical viability of the project and
conformance to land use and zoning guidelines before the awarding of the
contract to immediately address issues that will hamper their timely
completion;
b. allocate local funds equivalent to the reverted amount for the remaining works
of the unfinished project and submit a Project Completion Report within 30
calendar days to DILG-RO through the DILG Provincial Office after the issuance
of the Certificate of Completion, in compliance with the NAU; and
c. create a Project Monitoring Committee/Inspection Team responsible for the
review, inspection, and evaluation of the extent of implementation of programs
and projects to intensify monitoring of the projects, determine the causes of
delay and immediately address them.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Sangguniang Bayan to initiate the enactment of a separate Market
Code providing updated fees and rates considering the current needs of the
Municipality;
b. reinforce the existence of a Market Management Committee and assign a
Market Administrator who will perform the duties and responsibilities relative
to the overall management of the Municipalitys public market;
c. require the Municipal Treasurer to utilize only regular and bonded employees
to undertake the market collection; and
d. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strengthen the existing control system for
the issuances of accountable forms, collections and remittances by requiring the
assigned collector to prepare a separate report of daily cash tickets collection.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator to:
a. conduct a thorough study/investigation and survey of areas/locations of the
proposed projects, in coordination with the barangays and other concerned
officials, prior to inclusion of the PPAs in the Municipalitys AIP under the 20
percent DF to guarantee the full implementation of the PPAs in the
projected/scheduled year/date to be completed;
b. see to it that the PPAs included in the AIP have complete descriptions by
indicating, among others, the specific period of implementation;
c. coordinate with the Local Development Council to evaluate and reassess the
PPAs under the continuing appropriations if these PPAs are still responsive to
the existing needs of their constituents, otherwise, initiate reprogramming of
PPAs to more viable and significant PPAs to meet the socio-economic needs
of the Municipality; and
d. request the Municipal Engineer to fast track the ongoing development projects
and closely monitor the implementation of the same so that any problems in
the course of implementation can be immediately addressed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk and
Reduction Management Council to review and update the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan by including all the
Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) of the previous years' unexpended balances
and any supplemental budget, in compliance with Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular
No. 2012-002, and henceforth, instruct the MDRRM Officer to closely monitor the
implementation of the proposed PPAs.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant and GSO (i) implement the perpetual inventory method for accounting the supplies and inventories, (ii) maintain subsidiary records for inventory in the form of supplies ledger cards and stock cards, respectively, and (iii) ensure regular reconciliation of the accounting and property records;
b. Municipal Accountant (i) make appropriate adjustments in the Other Supplies and Materials Inventory account for the property and equipment and provide the Audit Team with a copy of such adjustment, and ensure that the procurement and issuance of supplies and materials be recorded in the appropriate asset and expense accounts pursuant to the asset method of accounting; (ii) reconcile with the inventory report of MHO on the rapid antigen test kits and MDRRMO on the welfare goods and provide this Office of the result of the reconciliation, and make an appropriate adjusting entry, if warranted; (iii) submit justification on the observations noted in the accounting treatment used in recording the expenditures for inventories held for distribution and consumption; and (iv) ensure that the JEVs in the recording of consumed and distributed inventories are supported by RIS and SSMI; and
c. GSO submit justification on the inclusion of issued inventories in the RPCI and ensure that only the actual physical count of all types of inventories will be reported in the RPCI and submit the RPCI to the Audit Team, and see to it that all issuances of inventories are covered by an approved RIS and summarized in the SSMI, to be forwarded to the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that the:
d. Municipal Accountant and GSO implement the perpetual inventory method for accounting various assets held for distribution and maintain subsidiary records for inventory in the form of supplies ledger cards and stock cards, respectively, and ensure regular reconciliation of the two records in accordance with the regulation, and submit proof that the distributed PPEs are properly recorded in the books of the recipient agencies and construction materials are used in accordance with the intended purpose; and
e. Municipal Accountant ensure proper classification of the transactions in accordance with the provision of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant and GSO coordinate with each other and prepare supporting schedules to facilitate the reconciliation of the PPE and Biological Assets accounts and establishment of the actual existence, ownership, and condition of the properties, and provide justification on the noted observation relative the PPE and biological assets of the Municipal Government;
b. Inventory Committee and GSO see to it that the required reports on Local Road Networks are in the prescribed format and supported with complete details pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015, and coordinate with the Municipal Engineer relative to the details of the Road Networks to facilitate submission of the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network; and
c. GSO-Designate ensure that the motor vehicles of Municipal Government be marked with “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” and bear government plate in accordance with the provision of Section V(2) and V(5) of COA Circular 75-6 dated November 7, 1975.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant (i) make appropriate adjustment in the misclassified obligations to officers and employees and receipt of fund from various LGUs and GOCCs to the Due to Officers and Employees, Due to LGUs and Due to GOCCs account pursuant to Annex B of COA Circular 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015, prescribing the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs, (ii) coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the transfer of the receipt of fund from PHIC in the General Fund books to Trust Fund books and maintain subsidiary ledger to monitor the receipt and disbursement of the fund; and (iii) analyze and conduct a thorough review of the Accounts Payable to determine valid claims and ensure that all recorded payables are duly supported with required documents under the GF and SEF with payables outstanding for more than two years to facilitate the reversion of the unsettled obligations aged more than two years in the General and Special Education Funds to the unappropriated surplus as provided in Section 98 of PD No. 1445; and
b. Municipal Treasurer and other concerned offices coordinate with the suppliers and employees to inform them of the unclaimed payables, otherwise provide certification to the Municipal Accounting Office that there are no valid claimants for the long outstanding payables to facilitate the reversion of funds.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant (a) make appropriate adjustments in the General and Special Education Fund books for the unrecorded collection of receivables in January and February 2021 of ₱1,896,935.58 and ₱1,896,935.58 of RPT and SET, collection from CY 2020 of ₱137,662.33 RPT and ₱212,729.80 SET, and advance payments of 2,577,667.72 for RPT and ₱2,577,667.72 for SET, gross of discounts; (b) coordinate with the Provincial Accountant relative the share of the Municipality for the collection of RPT and SET for proper recognition of income in the books of the Municipal Government; and (c) henceforth, ensure that the (i) receivables for RPT and SET be recognized at the beginning of the year; (ii) receivables for the setting-up of RPT and SET shall be based on the certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year from the Municipal Treasurer; (iii) JEVs be properly drawn in the collection of tax payments and recognition of income; and (iv) income and receivables be properly recognized in the year they were earned and not collected;
b. Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year for the establishment of Real Property Tax Receivables/Special Education Tax Receivables based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card; and
c. Municipal Accountant and Treasurer analyze and determine the cause of the deficiencies between their records, prepare reconciliation and make the necessary adjustments, and submit to this Office any actions taken to establish the correct balances of the subject receivables.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) make appropriate adjustments in books of accounts relative to the observations noted and provide this Office of the JEV drawn; and (ii) ensure proper recording of transactions related to the utilization of LDRRM Fund and fund transfers from National Government Agencies and on the recognition of income and closing entries, and the preparation of Statement of Financial Performance in the Trust Fund books pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Treasurer (i) ensure that the collections of the collectors are within the maximum cash accountability allowed in their fidelity bond, otherwise, increase the amount of fidelity bonds in order to avoid such cases in compliance with Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009; (ii) install a more sound internal control on the collection and remittance by requiring the revenue collectors to remit their collections daily regardless of the amount of collections on hand pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992; (iii) refund to the Municipal Fund the ₱14,290.00 or the amount equivalent to the unreconciled 1,429 pieces of Accountable Form No. 55, and the ₱18,000.00 used for the requisition of check without appropriation; and (iv) stop the practice of using directly the interest income without appropriating the same;
b. Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the availability of appropriation for the requisition of checkbooks of the Office of the Municipal Treasurer; and
c. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer provide justification on the noted observations in the cash collection and accountable form management, where applicable.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Engineer (i) monitor and require the contractors to make corrective actions on the defects noted in the projects pursuant to Section 62.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and apprise the Audit Team of the actions taken thereto, otherwise demand payment of the equivalent of ₱2,505,666.65 due to the defects noted; (ii) ensure compliance with DPWH DO No. 197 dated October 7, 2016, COA Resolution No. 2015-014 dated April 6, 2015 and other applicable laws, rules and regulation in the preparation of ABC for its future projects; and (iii) ensure that projects are completed based on approved plans and specifications; and
b. Municipal Accountant demand payment or institute appropriate action for the collection of the excess contract cost of ₱18,921.20 from Sialdang Construction and Enterprises.

We recommended that the:
a. BAC Secretariat observe proper consolidation of Project Procurement Management Plan and preparation of indicative Annual Procurement Plan pursuant to GPPB Circular No. 07-2015 dated July 16, 2015;
b. BAC Chairman abide with the provision of Section 12.1 of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184 by implementing only the staff of the BAC Office in the conduct of procurement activities of the Municipality; and
c. BAC and GSO-Designate consider the procurement of common-use supplies in the Procurement Service of the Department of Budget and Management (PS-DBM) or bulk purchases to avail of volume discount and lesser paperwork.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a. Designated MENRO prepare the annual progress report for CY 2021 on the strategies implemented, as well as the accomplishments, as required by the NSWMC in its Resolution No. 1103, series of 2018 and submit the same to the NSWMC, and ensure that targets and accomplishments be presented in the said report; (ii) conduct continuous information education campaign (IEC) activities and programs/activities to achieve the goals of the SWMP; and (iii) in coordination with other Municipal Officials concerned, fast track the improvement and processing of discharge permit of the Sanitary Landfill for it to be fully operationalized;
b. Municipal Mayor, through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes and ensure that all Barangays of the Municipality has operational material recovery facilities to fully implement mandatory segregation at source; and
c. Municipal Officials avoid reimbursement as a mode of payment.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer ensure regular reconciliation of their records to provide accurate financial reports intended for different users;
b. Municipal Budget Officer (i) ensure that reversion of funds is properly presented in the budget documents of the Municipal Government; (ii) submit the documents proving that the projects for the reverted fund of ₱1,640,660.44 in the continuing appropriation pertain to balances of completed projects and/or discontinued or abandoned projects; (iii) ensure that the indicative IRA (now National Tax Allotment – NTA) provided by the DBM thru their Local Budget Memoranda be used in the preparation of the Annual Budget of the Municipality; and (iv) provide justification on the non-appropriation of the ₱1,563,335.55 IRA received during CY 2021; and
c. Municipal Engineer (i) provide justification for the delay incurred in the implementation of the developmental projects. Henceforth, ensure that developmental projects be implemented in the period to which they were planned to achieve optimal utilization of the fund; and (ii) ensure that the date of preparation of the POW was indicated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the GAD Focal Point System:
a. Instruct the MPDC to maintain GAD database with sex-disaggregated data and other relevant data that will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation, and aid in identifying gender issues focused amongst clients and organization;
b. Prioritize the attendance of the GFPS Executive Committee, TWG and MET to various GAD-related trainings and seminars in order for them to serve as a catalyst in gender mainstreaming in the Municipality and identify gender issues in their GPB; and
c. Instruct the GFPS-TWG and/or the Designated GAD Focal Person, as the case may be, to submit the GAD Agenda and the DILG-received GAD AR and its supporting documents within the prescribed period to facilitate the evaluation of the success of the GPB.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following:
a. The Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all properties
owned by the municipality and prepare and submit the RPCPPE in
prescribed format not later than January 31 of each year; and
b. The Property Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain Property
Cards and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. The LDRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit a
monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF;
b. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the
current year unexpended QRF and DRRMF- MOOE to the Special Trust
Fund under Trust Liabilities-DRRM account at year-end; and,
c. The LDRRM Officer to ensure that the proposed programs, projects and
activities are incorporated in the LDRRM Plan prior to the release and
use of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor immediately issue the
Certificate of Completion, and make representations to the end-user and
beneficiaries of the Small Water Impounding Project for them to
immediately issue a Certificate of Acceptance to support the liquidation of
the fund transfer of P5,715,000.00 in the books of the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following officials
to do the following courses of action:
a) The Municipal Assessor to prepare and maintain an updated
Assessment Roll and furnish the Municipal Treasurer a copy thereof on
or before December 31 of the current year;
b) The Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list of taxpayers
and their respective tax due and furnish a copy to the Municipal
Account; and
c) The Municipal Accountant to set up RPT and SET Receivable at the
beginning of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer to comply with the prescribed procedures for disbursements
regarding the stamping/marking “PAID” on the documents once payment is
undertaken/consummated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to ensure that liquidation of cash advances for payroll be
supported with approved DTRs, Certificates of Service, or accomplishment
reports.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to submit the certification attesting to the truthfulness of the
contents of the report in Item b of DBM and DOH Joint Circular Nos. 1 and
2, both dated Novembers 25, 2020, to confirm compliance with the circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the Annual SEF Utilization Report and submit the
necessary document for the issuance of procured assets to end-user.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MPDC, MENRO
and Municipal Engineer to immediately prepare the papers/documents
needed for the implementation of the remaining development programs and
projects.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action:
a. Direct the concerned department heads of the municipality to timely
implement the programs, projects and activities funded by various
National Government Agencies in accordance with pertinent rules and
regulations and/or Memorandum of Agreement;
b. Make representations to the head of the Department of Social Welfare
and Development in pursuing the immediate implementation of
Pineapple Processing Program if still feasible, or in the return of the
fund to it; and

1.12	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) order the concerned officials to (i) adhere to the posting requirements set out in the procurement law; (ii) accomplish completely and properly the PO in every transaction; and (iii) submit the lacking supporting document as discussed.
3.2	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant instruct the submission of the lacking documents to the Audit Team.

4.13	We recommended that (a) the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant regularly reconcile the cashbook balance with the accounting records so that errors committed could be identified and appropriate adjustments in the records could be effected; and (b) the Municipal Accountant take up necessary adjustments to revert the cash for the identified stale check worth ₱33,562.67.

5.12	We recommended that the LCE direct the Inventory Committee to facilitate a one-time cleansing of PPE account balances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
5.13	We also recommended the (i) institution of required course of action for missing properties and reflect the required disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements; (ii) application of provisions with regard to the valuation of items found at station and include it in the PPE account balance when evidence warrants; and (iii) maintenance of complete property records by the Property Officer and the reconciliation of the same with the accounting records and the result of the physical inventory count.

6.5	We recommended that the LCE observe the requirements expressed in the said Circular by enjoining the GSO, Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to maintain conformant pertinent reports and property records such that all of the local roads, together with their related components, are reported detailing the roads’ complete description and cost segregation.

7.8	We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to make appropriate adjustments so that correction could be effected arising from the erroneous recording of fund transfers and, thereafter, see to it that the same are recorded under respective proper account.

8.7	We recommended that the (i) concerned officials find ways to promptly collect the required documents to facilitate timely recognition of loan payments so that accurate year-end balances of the Loans Payable account could be reflected; and (ii) Municipal Accountant adjust the Loans Payable and Cash in Bank accounts to make the balance reconcile with the bank records.

9.14	We reiterated our recommendation that Management continue the practice of ensuring optimal use of DF so that socio-economic projects that are beneficial to the agency’s constituents are timely implemented.
9.15	We also recommended that the (i) Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), together with the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO), prepare the AIP/Annual Budget clearly specifying the PPAs aimed at the attainment of socio-economic and environmental targets of the LGU; (ii) Municipal Accountant properly classify transactions in the accounting ledgers based on applicable standards and check the completeness of supporting documents before effecting payment; and (iii) concerned offices submit the supporting documents as required by COA Circular No. 2012-001 and pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184.

10.19	We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) MDRRMO to (i) properly plan, review and update  the LDRRMFIP specially those pertaining to PPAs charged to the Special Trust Fund and maximize/monitor their utilization for disaster preparedness, mitigation, and rehabilitation objectives; and (ii) ensure the compliance of the Municipality with respect to minimum standards on disaster preparedness by procuring the lacking equipage as prescribed in the Disaster Preparedness Manual; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) maintain the required registries and (ii) instruct the submission of lacking documents to the Audit Team.

11.18	We recommended that the LCE enjoin the GFPS to (i) monitor the GAD PPAs and coordinate with respective offices for the proper implementation of GAD Plan; (ii) secure an acknowledgement receipt of adjusted GPB from the DILG to ensure compliance of their adjustments to the requirements of the JMC on the localization of the MCW; (iii) enhance the GPB and GAD AR by completely filling out the needed qualitative/quantitative aspects of the activities implemented including data on sex-disaggregation pursuant to MCW, as necessary; (iv) fully comply with the provision of the JMC with respect to constitution of GFPS through the designation of TWG Chair and members in accordance with Item 4.1.A of the said law; and (v) observe the deadlines in the submission of GPB and GAD AR to the Audit Team.

12.7	We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer to delegate the collection function to regular and permanent employees of the agency in compliance with existing rules and regulations.

13.7	We, on the other hand, recommended that the LCE direct the Accounting, Treasury and Human Resource departments to (i) reconcile their records periodically to ensure that HDMF deductions are remitted intact; and (ii) implement a system or adopt a solution that will facilitate the timely recording and reporting of the HDMF remittances.

a. Municipal General
Services Officer - Designate to:
a.1 maintain property cards for each Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) of the Municipality and reconcile them with the ledger cards of the Accounting Office; and
see to it that all property cards are complete and updated including Property tagging or assigning inventory stickers/labels to all the property owned by the Municipality.
a.2 disclose all necessary information required in the RPCPPE to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the report; and
a.3 include in the RPCPPE the condition of each property and if found unserviceable, prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and other necessary documents for proper disposal of these property.
b. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the property ledger cards with the property cards of the MGSO and effect the corrections/adjustments, if warranted.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
The designation of the Municipal General Services Officer was transferred from the Municipal Treasurer to the Municipal Assessor during the year, thereby the partial implementation.

All necessary information were disclosed in the submitted RPCPPE for CY 2021.

Partially Implemented
The newly designated GSO was unable to prepare the IIRUP due to the volume and urgency of other transactions in the Municipality, however, she promised to prepare and submit the same to the COA upon completion of documents for proper disposal of the unserviceable property.

No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason:
The designation of the Municipal General Services Officer was transferred from the Municipal Treasurer to the Municipal Assessor during the year, thereby the partial implementation.

IIRUP was not prepared due to the volume and urgency of other transactions in the Municipality.

The Municipal Accountant could not undertake reconciliation due to the absence of complete Property Cards of the MGSO.

observations.recommendation:
a. Municipal General Services Officer to:
a.1 maintain a complete Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
a.2 keep a Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPC) for all roads and their components; and
a.3 render a complete Report on Local Road Networks at the end of the year.

b. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for every road network.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineer has prepared and submitted the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network as of December 31, 2021 to the Audit Team.

The Municipal Engineer has provided the complete descriptions of road components for all road projects of the Municipality in CY 2021.

The Accounting Office properly computed the depreciation for each road and component.

Subsidiary records were maintained for each road network.
observations.reason:
The Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Network was submitted to the Audit Team.

The responsibility of maintaining the Road Networks of the Municipality was transferred to another personnel designation of the Municipal Assessor to the MGSO which contributed to the delayed implementation of the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
a. Municipal Assessor to:
a.1 conduct a continuous ocular inspection of the real properties at the barangay level to validate the actual use of these properties;
a.2 prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer, on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December each year, an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties.

b. Municipal Treasurer to update their records based on the assessment roll of the Municipal Assessor showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year and furnish the Municipal Accountant as a basis in setting up the Real Property Tax (RPT)/Special Education Tax (SET) Receivables and corresponding Deferred RPT/SET Income at the beginning of the year.

c. Municipal Accountant to:
c.1 prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) setting up the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the updated and certified list of Real Property Tax records of the Municipal Assessor; and
c.2 reconcile the RPT/SET Receivable accounts and total collections for CY 2020 including penalties, recorded in the general ledger, with the Municipal Treasurer’s list of delinquencies and Monthly Report on the Collections of Real Property Tax as of December 31, 2020 and make necessary adjustments in the books, if any.
observations.managementAction:
An updated assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties was being prepared and submitted to the Municipal Treasurer monthly.

Records of the Municipal Treasurer regarding the tax collections and taxpayers were continuously being updated based on the monthly assessment roll.
observations.reason:
Reconciliation of the RPT/SET receivables of the Municipality at the beginning of the year were properly set up by the Municipal Accountant based on the updated records of the Municipal Assessor.

The Municipal Accountant properly undertook adjustments and recording based on the updated assessment roll.

observations.managementAction:
All receipts of supplies and materials were recorded as inventory.

The preparation of the Stock Cards (SC) for each inventory item is ongoing. The recording of receipts and issuances of receipts and issuances were undertaken for some inventory items such as the drugs and medicines.

The RIS and SSMI were submitted to the Accounting Office for proper recording of the expense of supplies and materials.

No action was taken by the Management for reconciliation and formal physical count pending completion of updated stock cards.
observations.reason:
The lack of manpower and volume of transactions contributed to the partial implementation of the recommendations.

Reconciliation will be undertaken upon completion of the updated Stock Cards and physical count.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineer has prepared and submitted the Quarterly GPPA reports with complete and correct details to the Audit Team.

The Municipal Accountant was continuously performing reconciliation of the ledger balance of CIP accounts with the Quarterly Report on Government PPAs submitted by the Engineering Office and recorded the necessary adjustments.
observations.reason: Quarterly GPPA reports with complete and correct details were submitted to the Audit Team and reconciliation of the ledger balances was continuously being performed by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.managementAction:
The Annual Operating Budget of the MRF was prepared for CY 2022.

An update on the 10-year SWMP is for submission to the Environmental Management Bureau (EMB). 

The Municipal Engineer and MPDC prepared a project feasibility study for the Sanitary Landfill pursuant to relevant guidelines.

The income and expense accounts pertaining to the operations of the MRF were properly presented and reflected in the separate Statement of Financial Performance for the MRF.
observations.reason: The Annual Operating Budget was prepared for CY 2022 and necessary steps were undertaken to update the SWMP and prepare feasibility studies and financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
a. Municipal Budget Officer to:
   a.1 provide written explanations for the discrepancy of P5,130,402.00;
   a.2 see to it that loan amortization is taken up under the correct Economic program classification; and
   a.3 prepare AIP for all unimplemented projects cited above, otherwise, revert the amounts for re-appropriation and prepare the necessary supplemental appropriations.

b. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to prepare the Utilization Report for the 20% Development Fund for close monitoring of its utilization and to ensure the AIP is the basis for preparation of Annual and Supplemental Budgets.
observations.managementAction:
The discrepancy of P5,130,402.00 pertains to the supplemental projects with inclusion of five projects with worth P3,134,572.29 in the budget for CY 2020 but not included in the Annual Investment Program (AIP). The supplemental AIP was submitted to the Audit Team.

No action was taken by the Management to prepare AIP for all unimplemented projects cited above, however, Management agreed to evaluate and assess the unimplemented project under continuing appropriations for re-appropriation.
observations.reason:
The discrepancy pertains to supplemental projects included in the budget for CY 2020 but not included in the AIP.

Utilization Reports were not submitted to the Audit Team. The MPDC agreed to prepare the reports in the succeeding periods.

observations.recommendation:
a. Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC) to:
   a.1 identify the needs of the Municipality in times of calamities and disasters as a guide in the preparation of an accurate LDRRMFIP and ensure the maximum utilization of the LDRRMF;
   a.2 review and update annually the LDRRMF Management Plan to incorporate the PPAs to be funded by the unexpended balance of LDRRMF from previous years; and
   a.3 institutionalize measures to strengthen disaster resilience including climate change impacts.

b. MDRRM Officer to closely monitor the implementation of the proposed PPAs.
observations.managementAction:
The MDRRMO agreed to comply with the recommendations and submit a revised LDRRMFIP for CY 2022.

The concerned offices conducted continuous monitoring and meetings to implement measures to strengthen disaster preparedness and the Municipality’s overall resilience to disasters.

The MDRRM Officer was doing close monitoring; however, due to unresolved issues delays in the completion of projects were incurred.
observations.reason:
The utilization of the current year’s LDRRMF was not fully maximized.

No action was undertaken to fully incorporate previous years’ unexpended balances in the CY 2021 LDRRMFIP as required; the Municipal MDRRMO agreed to submit a revised plan for CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GAD Focal Person – Designate to reconcile the total amount utilized for CY 2020 and prepare corrected reports and/or adjusting entries, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has reconciled the discrepancy and adjusted the same.

observations.recommendation:
a. submit supplemental budgets to cover the total contract costs of the two GAD Fund activities and prevent the contracts from being declared null and void, thus cause for disallowance in audit; and
b. ensure that all obligations/disbursements from the GAD Fund are in accordance with the GAD Plan and Budget.
observations.managementAction:
Supplemental Budgets were submitted to the Audit Team.

GAD Plan and Budget was prepared for CY 2021 and Management ensured that all GAD disbursements were in accordance with the aforementioned budget.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan to enact ordinances providing guidelines for the grant of financial assistance and that the Information Systems Office to create a data bank of all indigents of the Municipality for ready reference in processing financial assistance and other related transactions.
observations.managementAction: Kautusang Pambayan Bilang 81, T-2021, was enacted to provide guidelines for granting financial assistance to qualified beneficiaries.

The necessary documents were submitted to the Audit Team.

Official Receipts were not issued for the return of cash due to the urgency of the transaction, instead a picture was taken as proof of receipt of cash assistance returned.

No JEV was necessary for the refund due to the absence of OR, however, proper recording relative to the distribution of the assistance was undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments in the books to correct certain transactions relative to Property, Plant and Equipment accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding in the books No. 1 of 2020 The necessary adjustments in the books were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant: a. To provide an Allowance for depreciation on all depreciable assets; b. To prepare journal entries to correct the accounting errors in the financial statements, and furnish copies of the JEVs to the Audit Team for purposes of verification.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding in the books 2020 The depreciable allowance for some of these assets cannot yet be provided due to unavailability of supporting documents. The allowance for depreciation on some items were booked-up and supporting JEVs were submitted.
observations.reason: Other PPE items were not provided depreciation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare journal entries to correct the accounting errors in the financial statements, and copies of the JEVs be furnished to the Audit Team for purposes of verification.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding in No. 3 of 2020 The necessary adjustments in the books were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended/unobligated QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under Trust Liabilities-DRRM account at year-end.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 4 The necessary accounting adjustment to transfer the unexpended LDRRMF as of December 31, 2021 was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to adopt perpetual inventory method in accounting for purchases of inventories.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding in 2020 Perpetual inventory method was adopted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to adopt the following: a) The Municipal Ledger cards to monitor the inventories; b) The General Stock cards, and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI), together with the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS), as basis for crediting the non-existing balance from the books; and c) The Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of inventories every semester and submit the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 6 The necessary accounting adjustment to restore undocumented inventory adjustments was made; perpetual inventory method was adopted and physical count of inventories was provided and a report thereon was submitted.
observations.reason: Not all of the inventory items were provided with stock cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) create an Inventory Committee in accordance with Section 156 of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management prescribed by COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, and direct said committee to conduct properly the required physical count of all PPEs of the LGU; and b) direct the Supply/Property Officer to maintain Property Cards for each type of PPE.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 7 An inventory committee was constituted and conducted the required physical count of PPEs. Property Cards were maintained for some PPE items.
observations.reason: Not all of the PPE items were provided with property cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee Chairperson, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to submit the lacking documents and contracts and progress payments of the three (3) infrastructure projects funded under the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 8 The lacking documents and contracts and progress payments were submitted.
observations.reason: The lacking documents were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to require the contractors concerned to post the subject warranty securities.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 9 Henceforth, the Municipality should strictly enforce compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR in the implementation of infrastructure projects, particularly on the posting of warranty securities before final payment.
observations.reason: Thereafter, contractors posted the warranty securities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to do the following: a) Submit pictures taken during the receipt of the SAP by the beneficiaries; b) Provide in a document the determination of the vulnerable sector to which the beneficiaries belong, to determine the documentary requirements needed in the pay-out of assistance, in the future, if any.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 10 The lacking pictures were submitted. The necessary determination was complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairperson, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, submit the lacking supporting documents; rectify the deficiencies noted; refrain from having transactions with suppliers where pecuniary interest exist; require the contractors/suppliers to issue official receipt in gross amount; and implement its projects within the specified contract time. Henceforth, refrain from payment and undergoing procurement process without complete documentations, and strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 11 The lacking documentary requirements were submitted in compliance to both GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and GPPB Resolution No. 18-2020.
observations.reason: The lacking documentary requirements were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure all amounts obligated shall have an approved appropriation.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 12 The MBO ensures that amounts obligated have an approved appropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor take the following courses of actions: a. require the implementing offices/departments to submit reports of status of implementations of COVID-19 related PPAs to the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator; b. require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to monitor and evaluate the implementation of COVID-19 related PPAs to reflect it in the fund utilization report; c. post the accumulated fund utilization report in at least three (3) conspicuous public places of the Municipality at the end of every month; d. expedite the utilization of the unutilized funds from Bayanihan Grant before the lifting of the State of Calamity; and e. return the unutilized funds from the Bayanihan Grant to the Bureau of Treasury after the lifting of the State of Calamity.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 13 The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator submitted status of implementation of COVID-19 related PPAs. The accumulated fund utilization report was posted. The Municipality prioritized utilization of the Bayanihan Grant to address the pandemic. Unutilized fund was returned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to facilitate the implementation of PPAs funded under the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 14 Implementation of PPAs under the LDRRMF was facilitated, although there were factors beyond the municipality's control which hindered implementation of some PPAs.
observations.reason: Factors beyond the control of the municipality hindered the implementation of some PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the District Supervisor to fully utilize the Special Education Fund Budget in conformity to DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 15 The SEF budget was fully utilized. For CY 2021, there is a supplemental budget under SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a) verify the composition of the “unreconciled balances” of the Cash in Bank – LCCA account per BRS; b) regularly prepare bank reconciliation statements for each bank account; and c) prepare the necessary journal entries to take up reconciling items pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 and Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 1 2019 Efforts were exerted to identify the unreconciled balances and the BRS were prepared. Reconciling entries were booked-up.
observations.reason: There were still unreconciled balances in the BRS. BRS for other funds were not prepared due to lack of bank statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the JEV to correct the erroneous entry by recording Interest Expense and Road Network accounts; b) Cease capitalizing interest expense of loans for completed projects pursuant to IPSAS 5 - Borrowing Costs.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 2 Correcting entry was effected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference of ₱411,096.74 of Loans Payable – Domestic account between the accounting records of the Municipality and of the Municipal Development Fund Office. Also, furnish the audit team of the JEV to record the adjusting entries for purposes of verification.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 3 The necessary reconciliation was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to verify the composition of the beginning balances of the Due to BIR account of all funds and prepare the necessary adjusting and correcting entries for the errors identified.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 5 The Municipal Accountant exerted effort to verify the composition of the beginning balances and identified the composition of the Due to BIR account.
observations.reason: The composition of the balance was identified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: 1) Exert effort in identifying the details/breakdown of the beginning balance of the Due to GSIS account; 2) Reconcile amounts of GSIS contributions in payrolls/DVs and in subsidiary ledgers; 3) Prepare necessary adjusting entries on the unreconciled balances for the unrequired GSIS contributions for Extra Hazard Premium (EHP) and correcting entries for the errors identified. Furnish copy of the JEVs to record the adjusting entries for purposes of verification.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 6 Efforts were made. The necessary reconciliation and adjustments were effected in the books.
observations.reason: The necessary reconciliation was made and the adjustment was effected in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to stop including EHP as part of GSIS contributions in the payroll of regular employees, reconcile GSIS billings and payrolls/DVs to ensure that amounts billed were correctly and properly withheld, submit the Agency Remittance Advice and Electronic Remittance File on the first week of every month, and remit directly to the GSIS the employees’ and employers’ contributions within the first ten (10) days of the calendar month following the month to which the contributions apply.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 7 EHP was not included as part of GSIS contributions in the payroll of regular employees. The necessary reconciliation of amounts in GSIS billings and payrolls was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. direct the concerned department heads of the municipality to timely implement the programs, projects and activities funded by various National Government Agencies in accordance with pertinent rules and regulations and/or Memorandum of Agreement; b. make representations to the head of the Department of Social Welfare and Development in pursuing the immediate implementation of Pineapple Processing Program if still feasible, or in the return of the fund; and c. instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate to effect the immediate return of the unexpended/excess balances from completed projects pursuant to Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 11 Efforts were effected and there were observations on the implementation and return of funds where applicable.
observations.reason: Return of unexpended funds was effected where applicable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to ensure that LDRRMF is utilized solely for disaster risk reduction and management activities. Moreover, require: a) the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate registries (RAOs); b) the LDRRMO to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF certified correct by the Municipal Accountant within the reglementary period; c) the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for the LDRRMF and provide adequate disclosures thereof in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and d) the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund pursuant to NDRRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC Joint Memorandum Circular and COA Circular.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 11 The required subsidiary records are maintained; disclosures are reported in the Notes to Financial Statements; the CY 2019 unexpended LDRRMF was transferred.
observations.reason: The CY 2019 unexpended LDRRMF was transferred and the subsidiary records and disclosures were prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the members of the MSWMB to review and update the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan with timeline for implementation to determine the PPAs to be prioritized during the budget year and intensify efforts to have the Plan approved by the NSWMC.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 12 The MSWM Plan was updated and bears the approval of the NSWMC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify the collection efforts of her office and coordinate with other LGU officials to devise collection strategies and avail all legal remedies provided under RA to collect all taxes due to the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 13 Collection strategies were intensified.
observations.reason: Collection strategies were intensified and proper remedies were exhausted to collect the taxes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct: a) Municipal Treasurer to intensify the collection to recover government funds invested in the program and collect the tax dues; b) the Municipal Accountant to institute action for the collection of the accounts or other appropriate remedies to clear the amount in the books; and if warranted, request/apply for the write-off of the accounts following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 1 The Municipal Treasurer intensified collection. The Municipal Accountant and Local Chief Executive implemented measures to liquidate inter and intra agency accounts.
observations.reason: Efforts were exercised to liquidate the inter and intra agency transfers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) Create and convene the Inventory and Disposal Committee that shall conduct the validation of the PPEs physical existence and status, and submit the necessary report in accordance with the prescribed procedures and timeline; b) Require the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian to maintain and update PPE ledger cards and property cards respectively for each class of property and reconcile their records and coordinate with each other for the provision of depreciation and determination of impairment losses of these assets.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 2 The inventory committee was convened and the required validation of PPEs were undertaken. PPE ledger cards and property cards were maintained; however, they are not updated and reconciled.
observations.reason: PPE ledger cards and property cards were maintained but not updated and reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) the Municipal Mayor direct the Property Custodian to update, prepare and submit all documents and reports such as duly approved RIS to evidence the issuance of the materials from stock and Report of the Supplies and Materials Issued; b) the Municipal Accountant to comply with the provisions of the Manual of the NGAS, Volume I on the proper recording of purchases and issuances of supplies and materials; restore to inventory accounts the undocumented P569,213.49 adjustments; maintain the Subsidiary Ledger Card for inventories; and c) Municipal Mayor require Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of inventory items under perpetual inventory method and the Municipal Social Welfare Officer and Treasurer to furnish the copy of delivery receipt/acceptance and inspection report of all received inventory items.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 5 The Municipal Property Custodian prepared the required documents; perpetual inventory method was adopted; the necessary adjustments were made and the perpetual inventory records maintained though some subsidiary ledger cards remain not updated.
observations.reason: Not all supporting documents (RIS, SSMI) were filed; some subsidiary ledger cards are not updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal management submit the lacking supporting documents; rectify the deficiencies noted; refrain from having transactions with suppliers where pecuniary interest exists; require the contractors/suppliers to issue official receipt in gross amount; and implement its projects within the specified contract time. Also, ensure compliance with RA 9184 and its IRR.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 3 The lacking supporting documents were submitted; justifications for early completion and other explanations were provided to COA. The audit team received the documents.
observations.reason: The justifications and supporting documents were submitted to the audit team; the proofs of delivery were furnished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002, PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 and require all officials and employees concerned to immediately settle/liquidate their outstanding cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 3 2015 communication to the accountable employees to liquidate their outstanding cash advances was done. Partial enforcement resulted in liquidations.
observations.reason: Partial liquidations were enforced; some balances were cleared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU refrain from hiring private legal consultant without first securing the required documents stated in COA Circular No. 86-255 as amended by COA Circular No. 95-011, Section 9.1.2 of COA Circular 2012-001 and Section 10 of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 5 The hiring of private legal consultant without the proper procurement process was stopped.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prepare Monthly Report of Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption using the form prescribed in COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 and ensure proper supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 4 Monthly report of official travels and report of fuel consumption were prepared and regularly submitted.
observations.reason: The monthly reports were prepared and regularly submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the recipient officials and employees be required to refund the amount in excess of the authorized amount as prescribed in E.O. No. 80 and DBM BC No. 2014-3.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 7 There was partial refund of the disallowed amount.
observations.reason: Only partial refund was made as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officers and employees to strictly comply with the provisions of E.O. No. 298 and refund the amount received in excess of the authorized travel rates as well as the unnecessary travel registration/seminar fees; stop the practice of issuing travel authority to persons not affiliated to the concerned organization, and strictly observe the proper classification of accounts as provided in the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 3 There was partial refund of the disallowed amount and the prohibition on issuance of travel authority to persons not affiliated to the concerned organization was observed. Proper classification of accounts was reiterated.
observations.reason: Only partial refund was made as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations and the Municipal Mayor agreed to: (a) instruct the Treasury Office to hasten the updating of RPTAR/Taxpayer’s Index Cards and furnish the Accounting Office the list required under Section 20, Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; and (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to base the establishment of RPT and SET receivables on the list submitted by the Treasury Office for an accurate recognition of the said receivables.
observations.reason: Verification made by the Audit Team revealed that the list provided by the Treasury Office was a document entitled Certified List of Property Tax Delinquencies which contain incomplete/partial data.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer and collectors to (i) deposit/remit intact and on a daily basis the collections with/to the authorized government depository bank/Municipal Treasurer; (ii) stop the practice of retaining accumulated collections in their custody; (iii) prepare a complete RCD at the close of each business day; and (iv) always affix their signature in the pertinent section of the RCD.
observations.reason: Review of RCDs disclosed that there were still instances of collectors retaining accumulated collections in their custody; beginning balances were not always supplied in the RCD; and signatures were sometimes not affixed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the (a) Management agreed to (i) stop the charging of inappropriate/improper expenditures to the 20% Development Fund and strictly adhere to the relevant provisions of the JMC; (ii) continue the practice of ensuring optimal use of development funds so that socio-economic projects that are beneficial to the agency’s constituents are timely implemented; (b) BAC agreed to adhere with the GPPB Circular 2020-01 concerning the emergency procurement of goods and the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 for the conduct of public bidding; and (c) Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure the completeness of supporting documents provided by COA Circular 2012-001 before effecting payment of contracted projects. We further recommended the submission of the lacking documents as discussed.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the concerned Municipal officers to (i) prepare the necessary LDRRMFIP in accordance with the prescribed format inclusive of PPAs charged to unexpended LDRRMF of prior years; (ii) adhere to the applicable guidelines in the timely submission and complete reporting of donations during quarantine; and (iii) strictly comply with the abovementioned regulations with regards to the LDRRMF.
observations.reason:
(a) Reiterated in this Report

(b) Timely submission and complete reporting of donations were still not observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the: a) Municipal Accountant to record future support funds received from national government agencies to the correct payable account as a matter of sound accounting practice and for proper monitoring and control; and b) MSWD Officer to (i) inform the DSWD Regional Office (RO) III of the names of the individuals who returned the SAP 1st tranche for delisting in the SACEAP and in the list of beneficiaries eligible to receive the 2nd tranche subsidy; (ii) include in the SACEAP the names and other needed information of the 15 beneficiaries that were not previously  encoded or submit to DSWD for their review for possible inclusion in future government subsidies;  (iii) delete or request for deletion the names of the beneficiaries who were encoded twice in the SACEAP; (iv) strictly comply with the applicable regulations on the documentation of SAP-ESP; and (v) submit the required lacking documents to support the propriety of the subsidy payments.
observations.reason: (b) Those with SAC form were requested for encoding in the SACEAP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to (i) comply with the posting and reporting requirements in the utilization of the Bayanihan Grant to promote transparency and accountability of public funds; and (ii) submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team and observe compliance with the above-cited provisions of GPPB Circular in the procurement of goods.
We recommended and all concerned agency officials and personnel agreed to submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team and comply with the provisions of the abovementioned regulations.
observations.reason: Review of the submitted documents revealed that there were still lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Human Resources and Management Officer (HRMO), the Accounting Office and other concerned offices to submit the supporting documents as required by COA Circular No. 2012-001 to prevent the issuance of audit suspension or disallowance for failure to support the legality or regularity of the payment of CNA incentive.

We recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to use the correct account classification in recording personnel benefits and avoid accumulating similar transactions in Other MOOE account to maintain fair representation of the accounts.
We recommended and the Management agreed to continue to adhere to applicable tax laws and regulations in the withholding and remittance of taxes.
We recommended and the Management agreed to strictly observe the applicable rules and regulations in the withholding and remittance of contributions and/or loan amortizations.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the a) Treasury Office to hasten the updating of Real Property Tax Assessment Register/Taxpayer’s Index Cards and furnish the Accounting Office the list required under Section 20, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and

b) Municipal Accountant to base the establishment of RPT and SET receivables on the list submitted by the Treasury Office for an accurate recognition of the said receivables.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Management agreed to instruct the: a) Municipal Accountant (i) to maintain complete PPELC for each class of PPE to record the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof; (ii) to review or cause the review of prior years’ disbursement vouchers and supporting documents in the custody of the Accounting Office for purposes of tracing back procurement of Work/Zoo Animals  as well as PPEs using the Special Education Fund to facilitate the preparation of PPELCs; (iii) in case the Accounting Office does not maintain copy of prior years’ disbursement vouchers and supporting documents, request from the COA Regional Office, through the Audit Team Leader, for copy of said documents already submitted to the Audit Team in previous years, for the same purpose of completion of PPELCs pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-006 dated September 18, 2013; and (iv) to prepare journal entry voucher to record adjusting entries recognizing the proper PPE account for transactions that were erroneously recorded as outright expense and submit copy of said duly approved journal entry voucher to the Audit Team for monitoring purposes; b) General Services Officer (i) to designate staff who will be responsible in preparing the required PCs of all PPEs including properties acquired from previous years to avoid the recurrence of the audit finding in the next reporting period; and (ii) to coordinate with the Accounting Office for benchmarking and reconciliation of data; and c) Inventory Committee to complete the physical verification of all properties of the Municipality acquired until December 31 and render required reports thereon within the timeline of January 31 of each year.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: a) require the Municipal Treasurer and collectors to (i) deposit/remit intact and on a daily basis the collections with/to the authorized government depository bank/Municipal Treasurer; (ii) stop the practice of retaining accumulated collections in their custody; (iii) prepare a complete RCD at the close of business day; and (iv) affix their signature in the pertinent section of the RCD; and b) designate a disbursing officer who shall exclusively perform the disbursing functions.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to i) direct the Market Administrator to strictly implement the provisions of the Market Code specifically on the execution of the contracts and enforcement of the provisions stipulated therein; and ii) make representation to the Sangguniang Bayan for the amendment of the Market Code to include a penalty provision that would enjoin the stallholders to pay their obligations within the designated period and duration of the lease contracts if they intend to allow lease contracts exceeding one year.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the: a) Human Resources and Management Officer i) to comply with the relevant provisions of CSC Resolution No. 020790 in drafting the Contracts of service/job order for all JO workers; and ii) to ensure the correctness and completeness of details in the DTRs of JOs; and b) Municipal Treasury and Municipal Accountant to comply with documentary requirements provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 before the grant of cash advances for the payment of wages of JOs and Casuals and in the liquidation of the same.
We recommended and the Management agreed to continuously observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) – Designate to maintain property cards for each Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) of the Municipality and reconcile with the ledger cards of the Municipal Accounting office.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of PPE accounts with Accounting Office

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
instruct the Treasury Office to hasten the updating of RPTAR/Taxpayer’s Index Cards and furnish the Accounting Office the list required under Section 20, Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to base the establishment of RPT and SET receivables on the list submitted by the Treasury Office for an accurate recognition of the said receivables.
