We recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to use the correct account classification in recording personnel benefits and avoid accumulating similar transactions in Other MOOE account to maintain fair representation of the accounts.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
instruct:
a. Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) - Designate to:
a.1 maintain property cards for each Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) of the
Municipality and reconcile with the ledger cards of the Municipal
Accounting Office;
a.2 disclose all necessary information required in the Report on the Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to ascertain the
accuracy and reliability of the report; and
a.3 include in the RPCPPE the condition of all property of the City and if found
unserviceable, prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and other necessary documents for proper
disposal of these property.
b. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the property ledger cards with the property
cards of the MGSO and effect corrections/adjustments, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal General Services Officer to:
a.1 maintain a complete Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
a.2 keep a Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPC) for all roads and their
components; and
a.3 render a complete Report on Local Road Networks at the end of the year.
b. Inventory Committee, in coordination with the Municipal Engineering Office, to
conduct physical inventory of all existing local road networks in the Municipaity,
prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network
to be reconciled in the accounting records and LRNPC, and furnish a copy of
the inventory report to the Audit Team for verification and audit;
c. Municipal Accountant to:
c.1 identify the road lots recorded under the Land account and effect their
reclassification to the Road Networks account;
c.2 reconcile the list of inventory per local road inventory report and road map
with those recorded in the Registry in Public Infrastructure;
c.3 keep and maintain subsidiary records for road and road components for
every road network;
c.4 segregate and recognize the cost of each component of the local roads with
identified cost;
c.5 prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each
component at the end of the year; and
c.6 disclose the total road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements
in the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-008.
d. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of
road components for road projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Assessor to:
a.1 conduct continuous ocular inspection of the real properties at the
barangay level to validate the actual use of these properties;
a.2 prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer, on or before the thirty-first
(31st) day of December each year, an assessment roll containing a list of
all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed
and the values of such properties.
b. Municipal Treasurer to update their records based on the assessment roll of the
Municipal Assessor showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and
collectible for the year and furnish the Municipal Accountant as basis in
setting up the Real Property Tax (RPT)/Special Education Tax (SET)
Receivables and corresponding Deferred RPT/SET Income at the beginning of
the year.
c. Municipal Accountant to:
c.1 prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) setting up the RPT/SET Receivables
at the beginning of the year based on the updated and certified list of Real
Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card; and
c.2 reconcile the RPT/SET Receivable accounts and total collections for CY
2020 including penalties, recorded in the general ledger, with the Municipal
Treasurer’s list of delinquencies and Monthly Report on the Collections of
Real Property Tax as of December 31, 2020 and make necessary
adjustments in the books, if any.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor to:
a. instruct the Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies and
materials as inventory items and record as expenses only those items actually
issued/consumed during the accounting period.
b. direct the MGSO to:
b.1 properly maintain and observe correct recording in the SC for each type
of inventory;
b.2 record all receipts and issuances of supplies in the stock cards;
b.3 prepare and consolidate all Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for which
supplies and materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and
Materials Issued (SSMI) with complete details and submit the same to the
Municipal Accounting Office (MAO); and
b.4 reconcile the Stock Cards periodically with the ledger cards of the MAO;
b.5 conduct physical count of inventories semestrally, generate / prepare and
submit the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories to the Auditor not
later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.
c. require the Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer to:
c.1 reconcile the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories with the GL
controlling accounts; and
c.2 draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the adjustments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Engineer to submit to COA the Quarterly Reports on Government
PPAs with complete and correct details such as target completion date,
percentage of completion, total cost incurred to date and project status, copy
furnish the Municipal Accountant as basis for recording/adjustment in the
books of the completed projects to its appropriate account; and
b. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the ledger balance of the CIP accounts with
the Quarterly Report on Government PPAs submitted by the Engineering Office
and record the necessary adjustments, if any, pursuant to Paragraph 4, Section
50 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the Annual Operating Budget for each Local
Economic Enterprise as required by the Budget Operations Manual for LGUs;
b. MSWMB to review and update the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan of the
Municipality and subsequently, every two years or as the need arises, in
compliance with Section 4 (g), Rule VI of the IRR of RA No. 9003;
c. MPDC to revisit the project feasibility study on the operation of the MRF to re-
establish its medium-term and long-term viability and formulate and recommend
fiscal plans and policies for its improvement pursuant to Section 476 (6) of RA
No. 7160; and
d. Municipal Accountant to prepare a realistic/definite Statement of Financial
Statements for MRF as required under the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume 1, to
properly determine the financial status of the MRF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. Municipal Budget Officer to:
a.1 provide written explanations for the discrepancy totaling P5,130,402.00 and
inclusion of five projects worth P3,134,572.29 in the budget for CY 2020 but
not included in the Annual Investment Program (AIP);
a.2 see to it that loan amortization is taken up under the correct program
classification;
a.3 prepare AIP for all unimplemented projects cited above, otherwise, revert
the amounts for re-appropriation to other projects and subsequent
preparation of CY 2021 Investment Programs, and henceforth use the AIP
as basis for the preparation of the Annual and Supplemental Budgets to
ensure the validity and legality of disbursements.
b. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to prepare the
Utilization Report for the 20% Development Fund for close monitoring of its
utilization.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (MDRRMC) to:
a.1 identify the needs of the Municipality in time of calamities and disasters as
guide in the preparation of an accurate LDRRMFIP and ensure the maximum
utilization of the LDRRMF;
a.2 review and update annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan to incorporate the
PPAs to be funded by the unexpended balance of LDRRMF from previous
years; and
a.3 institutionalize measures to strengthen disaster preparedness and response
capabilities at all levels to answer the needs of its constituents in time of
calamities and disasters.
b. MDRRM Officer to closely monitor the implementation of the proposed PPAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal GAD Focal Person – Designate to reconcile the total amount utilized
for CY 2020 and succeeding years from the Municipality’s GAD Fund, and prepare
corrected reports and/or adjusting entries, if necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer and
Municipal GAD Focal Person – Designate to:
a. submit supplemental budgets to cover the total contract costs of the two GAD
Fund activities and prevent the contracts to be declared null and void, thus,
cause for disallowance in audit; and
b. ensure that all obligations/disbursements from the GAD Fund are in
accordance with the GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan to
formulate policies and guidelines to govern the grant of financial assistance which
includes but not limited to the eligibility of the beneficiaries, limitation on the
amount to be given in a particular care, the case/s to be given priority and the all
requirements needed.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Management
Information Systems Office to create a data bank of all indigents of the Municipality
for ready reference in processing financial assistance and other related
transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. MSDO to submit written justifications on the noted deficiencies in the
implementation of the Social Amelioration Program of the DSWD and submit all
necessary documents as required by Memorandum Circulars of the DSWD to
support the receipt of the subsidy and the qualification of the beneficiaries;
b. Municipal Treasurer to submit the Official Receipts as proof of return of cash
assistance, otherwise, submit written explanations relative thereto; and
c. Municipal Accountant to submit records and/or Journal Entry Voucher (JEV)
relative to the refund of cash assistance.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Designated GSO implement the perpetual
inventory method for accounting the supplies and inventories and maintain
subsidiary records for inventory in the form of supplies ledger cards and
stock cards, respectively, and ensure regular reconciliation of the two records
in accordance with the same regulation;
b. Municipal Accountant make appropriate adjustment in the Other Supplies
and Materials Inventory account and provide the Audit Team with a copy of
such adjustment, and ensure that the procurement and issuance of supplies
and materials be recorded in the appropriate asset and expense accounts
pursuant to the asset method of accounting; and
c. Designated GSO submit justification on the inclusion of issued inventories in
the RPCI and ensure that only the actual physical count of all type of
inventories will be reported in the RPCI, and submit the RPCI to the Audit
Team.

We recommended that the:
a. Inventory Committee, Designated GSO and Municipal Accountant ensure
full compliance with the provisions of Sections 5, 6, 7 and 8 of COA
Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 relative the one-time cleansing
of PPE to come-up with a reliable PPE balance;
b. Designated GSO coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer relative the
transfer of custody of the property cards, and prepare and maintain
prescribed format of property cards pursuant to Section 45 and Annex 37,
Volume II of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs;
c. Municipal Accountant (i) coordinate with the Municipal Assessor relative the
unrecorded 44 parcels of land acquired through purchase and/or donation,
and make appropriate adjustment in the books of accounts and provide the
Audit Team with the copy of adjustments, if any. Henceforth, ensure that all
properties owned by the Municipal Government be recorded in the books of
accounts; (ii) ensure regular encoding of the previous years’ PPE recorded
in the manual PPE ledger to the eNGAS; and (iii) analyze the nature of the
transactions recorded in the Power Supply System account and make
appropriate adjustment, if warranted. Henceforth, ensure proper
classification of accounts in accordance to the Revised Chart of Accounts for
LGUs as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015;
d. Municipal Accountant and Designated GSO coordinate with each other and
prepare supporting schedules to facilitate the reconciliation of the property
accounts and establishment of actual existence, ownership, and condition of
the properties;
e. Inventory Committee prepare and accomplish the RPCPPE and RPCLRN
pursuant to Annex 49 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II and
Annex C of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015, and
ensure that only the result of the actual physical count of PPE will be
reported in the RPCPPE and RPCLRN; and
f. Municipal Engineer prepare a schedule of the Local Roads indicating therein
the cost of each Road Network Component such as (i) road lot, (ii) road
pavement, (iii) drainage and slope protection structures, and (iv) other
miscellaneous structures to be submitted to the GSO and Accounting Office.
Moreover, subsequent road projects of the Municipality should clearly
indicate the separate cost of each road component for easy reference. The
Engineering Office is also vested to determine the life of the Local Roads
taking into consideration the design, type of users, and volume of users of the
roads. Such detail should also be included in the schedule of Local Roads as
basis for the depreciation of the road components to be applied by the
Accounting Office.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Agriculturist collate all the documents relative the swine totaling
₱98,033.20 and livestock from DA RFO MIMAROPA totaling ₱777,455.66
and provide the same to the Municipal Accountant for appropriate recording
in the books of accounts, and provide acceptable justification on the noted
observation relative the livestock from DA RFO MIMAROPA; and
b. Municipal Accountant (i) ensure that the sale of swine totaling ₱98,033.20
has valid authority for disposal before recording in the books of accounts,
otherwise, cause the responsible Office to provide acceptable justification on
the sale of these assets without valid authority and submit the same to the
Audit Team for evaluation, and cause the refund of the difference in proceed
and cost of the sold animals; and (ii) record the livestock from the DA RFO
MIMAROPA taking into consideration the changes in fair value, physical
change and the offspring they generated pursuant to Paragraph 13 and 16 of
IPSAS 27, Agriculture.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that assets
charged against Trust Fund be recognized first in the appropriate asset accounts
in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 before
transferring to the General Fund books, and ensure proper recording of
transferred asset in the General Fund books in accordance with the provision of
the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer monitor the collections of
the collectors to ensure that it is within the maximum cash accountability
allowed in their fidelity bond, and if possible, coordinate with the Municipal
Mayor and Municipal Budget Officer for the increase in the appropriation of
fidelity bond premium commensurate to the average cash accountabilities of
collectors; and install sound internal control on the collection and remittance by
requiring the municipal revenue collectors to remit their collections intact and
daily before the close of office hours and barangay deputized collector to remit
their collection regularly and avoid keeping large amounts of money in their
possession.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant provide acceptable
justification on the incurrence of the ₱850,923.19 appropriation and/or
obligation not backed-up by actual cash in the 20% Development Fund.
Henceforth, ensure compliance with the provision of Sections 337 of RA No.
7160; and
b. Municipal Accountant review the outstanding/stale checks, and require the
Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the
Accountant with the original DVs and its supporting documents for the
preparation of the JEV following the procedures prescribed under the
Manual on NGAS for LGUs to ensure recording of such cancellation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Assessor and Designated General Services Officer to cause the transfer of the
title of the 30 parcels of land in the name of the Municipal Government of
Victoria.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant ensure that excess amount from the fully completed
projects be immediately refunded to the source agencies pursuant to Section
4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994;
b. Municipal Treasurer remit the 5% share from income from building permit
fees of the DPWH-NBCDO, and ensure compliance with provisions of
Sections 2.3 and 2.5 of DPWH-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 001
dated July 4, 2013; and
c. Municipal Engineer as the Building Official ensure the utilization of the 15%
share from income from building permit fees taking into consideration the
provisions of Sections 2.6, 2.7 and 3.0 of the same circular.

We recommended that the:
a. Local Finance Committee stop the practice of estimating the share from IRA
for the budget year but instead be guided by the Local Budget Memorandum
to be issued by the DBM to maximize the government resources in the
preparation of the Annual Budget of the Municipality;
b. Municipal Budget Officer, Planning and Development Coordinator,
Treasurer and Accountant provide the document/s or proof that balances of
the projects declared as savings from the balances of completed projects,
and/or discontinued or abandoned projects totaling ₱6.06 million in the 20%
Development Fund as per Articles 418 and 454(b) of the IRR RA No. 7160;
c. Municipal Accountant review the paid payrolls for hazard pay as compared
with the attached daily time records and accomplishment report and cause
the refund of the excess payments made, and ensure that attachments in the
paid disbursement vouchers are free from alterations, or if it cannot be
avoided, the person who make the alteration should sign in the portion of the
altered documents;
d. BAC Secretariat submit the copy of Annual Procurement Plan for the
procurement in response to COVID-19 and its proof of posting pursuant to
GPPB Circular No. 03-2020 dated April 6, 2020;
e. Acting Municipal Treasurer provide acceptable justification on the
reimbursement made for the procurement of welfare goods totaling
₱525,156.00;
f. MDRRM Office and MSWD Officer provide the distribution list of the
welfare goods and medicines and other supplies distributed;
g. Local Finance Committee submit the copy of the report of utilization of the
BGCM Fund pursuant to Annex C of DBM LBC No. 125 and its proof of
posting and the written notice to the DBM, House of Representatives, Senate
of the Philippines, House Committee on Appropriations, Senate Committee
on Finance, and other offices; and
h. Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer provide acceptable
justification on the double payments of the three beneficiaries of the SAP-
ESP and make appropriate action thereto.

We recommended that the:
a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and BAC Secretariat provide acceptable
justification on the noted procedural lapses in the procurement out of fund
from National Government Agencies and 20% Development Fund.
Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No.
9184 particularly on the (i) requirement of valid PCAB license and
registration as an eligibility requirement, (ii) posting of performance security
within the prescribed period, (iii) inclusion CSHP duly approved by the
DOLE and other contract documents in the Bidding Documents as required
under Section 37.2.3 of the law, and (iv) posting of NOA and NTP and
approved contract in the PhilGEPs within the prescribed period;
b. Municipal Engineer provide acceptable justification on the issuance of
extension of contract, otherwise, compute the liquidated damages to be
imposed to the contractor for the delays incurred in the implementation of
the projects due to invalid reason for extension of contract time, and provide
the Audit Team with the computation. Henceforth, ensure compliance with
the provision of Item 10.2 of Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184
relative the issuance of extension of contract time;
c. Municipal Accountant ensure compliance with the provision of Section 86 of
PD 1445 relative the issuance of certificate of availability of funds; and
d. Municipal Accountant, BAC Chairperson, Municipal Engineer and other
concerned employees submit the lacking supporting documents in
compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated
June 14, 2012 and pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit all the
lacking documents to the Audit Team for audit purposes.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, MDRRM Council and
MDRRM Officer provide specific, measurable, achievable, realistic and time-
bound (SMART) performance indicators in each PPAs appropriated for
LDRRMF to facilitate monitoring of the attainment of the planned PPAs,
and provide justification on the appropriation of Quick Response Fund less
than the required 30% of the total 5% LDRRMF. Henceforth, ensure
compliance with the provision of Section 4.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013;
b. MDRRM Officer monitor and facilitate the implementation of the PPAs
under the LDRRMF and ensure that all projects appropriated be
implemented during the year;
c. BAC Chairperson and BAC Secretariat provide justification on the noted
observations on the procurement out of LDRRMF of the Municipality.
Henceforth, ensure compliance with relevant provision of the 2016 Revised
IRR of RA No. 9184 on the posting of the Notice of Award in PhilGEPS
website and inclusion CSHP duly approved by the DOLE and other contract
documents in the Bidding Documents;
d. Municipal Engineer provide acceptable justification on the issuance of
extension of contract time for reasons under Item 10.2 of Annex E of the
Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and
e. Municipal Accountant submit all the lacking supporting documents in
compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated
June 14, 2012 and pertinent provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No.
9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the Designated MENRO to (i) prepare the annual progress report on
the strategies implemented, as well as the accomplishments, as required by
the NSWMC in Resolution No. 1103, series of 2018 and submit the same to
the NSWMC; (ii) conduct continuous information education campaign (IEC)
activities and programs/activities to achieve the goals of the SWMP; and (iii)
coordinate with the DENR in the transition for the use of the Sanitary
Landfill; and
b. through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of
barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the
government by educating their respective constituents on the proper
segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes, and
ensure that all Barangays of the Municipality has operation material
recovery facilities to fully implement mandatory segregation at source.

We recommended that the:
a. Designated Market Supervisor and Acting Municipal Treasurer (i) seek the
assistance of the Executive and Legislative Departments to revisit the
contract of lease and enforce the provisions of the Market Code, and (ii)
instititute appropriate action against the delinquent market stall holders for
collection of their unpaid bills to boost the Municipal Government’s revenue
and eliminate delinquencies or be kept to a minimal amount; and
a. Municipal Accountant adopt accrual basis of accounting based on the
concrete record of the receivables pursuant to Paragraph 7mof IPSAS 1,
Presentation of Financial Statements, and institute a system of control
relative to the recording of collectibles from Public Market stallholders using
the appropriate account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the GAD Focal
Point System:
a. Prioritize the attendance of the GFPS Executive Committee, TWG and MET
to various GAD-related trainings and seminars in order for them to serve as
a catalyst in gender mainstreaming in the Municipality and identify gender
issues in their GPB;
b. Instruct the MPDC to maintain GAD database with sex-disaggregated data
and other relevant data that will serve as basis for gender-responsive
planning, programming and policy formulation, and aid in identifying
gender issues focused amongst clients and organization; and
c. Instruct the GFPS-TWG and/or the Designated GAD Focal Person, as the
case may be, to submit the DILG-received GAD AR and its supporting
documents within the prescribed period to facilitate evaluation of the success
of the GPB.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer consider the enrolment of
all accounts of the Municipal Government with BIR-eFPS, otherwise ensure
that funds for payment of the tax from the other four bank accounts be
transferred on or before the date of remittance to BIR to avoid possible use
of cash in the GF-Proper; and
b. Municipal Accountant ensure the proper recognition of intra-agency
receivables and payables accounts in the books of accounts of General and
Trust Funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to make necessary adjustments in the books to correct all
erroneous recording or classification of items in PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant:
a. To provide an allowance for depreciation on all depreciable assets; and
b. To prepare journal entries to correct the accounting errors in the
financial statements, and copies of the JEVs be furnished to the Audit
Team for purposes of verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare journal entries to correct the accounting errors in the
financial statements, and copies of the JEVs be furnished to the Audit Team
for purposes of verification.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the
unexpended/unobligated QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust
Fund under Trust Liabilities-DRRM account at year-end.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to adopt perpetual inventory method in accounting for
purchases of inventories.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following:
a) The Municipal Accountant to maintain supplies ledger cards to monitor the
issuances of the inventories;
b) The General Services Officer to maintain stock cards, and submit the
Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI), together with the
Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS), to the Accounting Office for recognition of
expense of issued/distributed inventories; and
c) The Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of inventories every
semester and submit the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI)
within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) create an Inventory Committee in accordance with Section 156 of the Rules
and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in the Local
Governments prescribed by COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992,
and direct said committee to conduct properly the required physical count of
all PPEs of the LGU; and
b) direct the Supply/Property Officer to maintain Property Cards for each type
of PPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards
Committee Chairperson, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to
submit the lacking documents in the contracts and progress payments of the
three (3) infrastructure projects funded under the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer
to require the contractors concerned to post the subject warranty securities.
Henceforth, the Municipality should strictly enforce compliance with the
pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR in the implementation of
infrastructure projects, particularly on the posting of warranty securities
before final payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to do the
following:
a) Submit pictures taken during the receipt of the SAP by the beneficiaries;
and
b) Provide in a document the determination of vulnerable sector to which the
beneficiaries belong, to determine the documentary requirements needed in
the pay-out of like assistance, in the future, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairperson,
in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to submit the lacking
documentary requirements, in compliance to both GPPB Circular No. 01-2020
and GPPB Resolution No. 18-2020.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Budget Officer to ensure all amounts obligated shall have an approved
appropriation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor take the following courses of
actions:
a. require the implementing offices/departments to submit reports of status of
implementations of COVID-19 related PPAs to the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator;
b. require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to monitor
and evaluate the implementation of COVID-19 related PPAs to reflect it in
the fund utilization report;
c. post the accumulated fund utilization report in at least three (3)
conspicuous public places of the Municipality at the end of every month;
d. expedite the spending of the unutilized funds from Bayanihan Grant before
the lifting of the State of Calamity; and
e. return the unutilized funds from the Bayanihan Grant to the Bureau of
Treasury after the lifting of the State of Calamity.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to facilitate the
implementation of PPAs funded and reported under LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the District Supervisor
to fully utilize the Special Education Fund Budget in conformity to DepEd-
DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017, DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint
Circular No. 1, series of 2020, and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 2,
series of 2020 in order to provide all the schools within the district the
necessary equipage in response to the BE-LCP program of the DepEd.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to promptly request the copies of debit advices issued by the LBP-Tarlac for the timely recognition of payments to reflect accurate year-end balances of the Loans Payable account.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to (i) prepare the necessary LDRRMFIP in accordance with the prescribed format; (ii) refrain from using the LDRRMF for programs/projects/activities which are not directly related to disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations; and (iii) ensure the compliance of the Municipality with respect to minimum standards on disaster preparedness; and (b)  Municipal Accountant (i) to adhere to the applicable guidelines on accounting and reporting of the LDRRMF by recognizing asset or expense account instead of Subsidy to Other Funds, where appropriate and by recording payables only for rendered or delivered services or goods; and (ii) coordinate with the BAC Secretariat for purposes of submitting the lacking supporting documents identified in this report.

We recommended that the Management (i) utilize the 20% development funds in accordance with the parameters set forth in the Joint Memorandum Circular and, in relation thereto, desist from charging inappropriate expenditures against the development fund such as administrative expenses which can be charged against the regular budget of the agency; (ii) comply with the documentary requirements and relevant provisions of R.A. No. 9184 specifically those relating to the submission of performance securities and results of eligibility check/screening; and (iii) submit the lacking documents mentioned in the report in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

Management Comment:
The Municipality conveyed that some documents that were noted were already submitted to the Audit Team and the other necessary actions and recommendations given by the COA will be implemented immediately.

Auditor’s Rejoinder: 
We validated the Management’s reply claiming that all supporting documents were submitted.  In our validation, we determined that there were still lacking documents which are required to support the disbursements. Hence, we reiterate our audit recommendation for the Management to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents.
observations.managementAction:
Management Comment:

Auditor’s Rejoinder: 
We validated the Management’s reply claiming that all supporting documents were submitted.  In our validation, we determined that there were still lacking documents which are required to support the disbursements. Hence, we reiterate our audit recommendation for the Management to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct: (a) the persons determined to be responsible for the lacking documents to submit the same to the Audit Team; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to (i) check the completeness of supporting documents before granting the cash advance for CF and (ii) observe the three months requirement in granting cash advance for CF purposes.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct: a) the MENRO, in coordination with the SWMB, to fully implement and monitor effectively the Solid Waste Management Plan and activities which focus on preserving and protecting the environment, and b) the MENRO and support offices to strictly implement the provisions of Municipal Ordinance No. 2007-81, specifically the proper segregation of wastes in order to instill discipline among constituents and attain the objectives R.A. No. 9003.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: (a) require all GAD officials concerned to develop and maintain a GAD Database as source of information for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and (b) instruct the GAD Focal Person to (i) ensure that the GAD AR is accompanied by a brief summary of the reported program/project and copies of reported policy issuances and (ii) submit in writing the changes in the DILG-approved GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and secure an acknowledgement receipt of adjusted GPB from the DILG to ensure the compliance of their adjustments to the requirements of the joint memorandum circular on the localization of the Magna Carta of Women.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the ME and the Bids and Awards Committee to (i) consider the project specific provisions of DILG MC 2019-71 in the implementation of AM projects (ii) furnish the Auditor advance copies of contracts of similar transactions and their supporting documents within five (5) days from the execution thereof; and (iii) adhere to the above-cited provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 and RA No. 9184 and its IRR on the procurement and documentation process.

Management Comment:
The Municipality conveyed that some documents that were noted were already submitted to the Audit Team and the other necessary actions and recommendations given by the COA will be implemented immediately.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
We validated Management’s reply claiming that all supporting documents were submitted.  In our validation, we determined that there were still lacking documents which are required to support the disbursements. Hence, we reiterate our audit recommendation for the Management to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents.
