Auditor’s Rejoinder:
We validated Management’s reply claiming that all supporting documents were submitted.  In our validation, we determined that there were still lacking documents which are required to support the disbursements. Hence, we reiterate our audit recommendation for the Management to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to continuously remit the GSIS premiums within the prescribed period.
We recommended and the Management agreed to continuously remit the Pag-IBIG premiums within the prescribed period provided for under the aforementioned law.
We recommended and the Management agreed to continuously remit the PHIC premiums within the prescribed period.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to drop in the PPE account the small tangible items in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office prepared a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to drop in the small value items in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) – Designate to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable property thru the submission of a request for disposal to the Office of the Auditor and the duly accomplished Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP);
b. the Municipal Disposal Committee to prepare and submit to the Office of the Auditor the Appraisal Report and other required documents for evaluation and inspection and initiate the immediate disposal of these unserviceable properties in accordance with the rules and procedures prescribed in COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and
c. the Municipal Accountant, upon disposal, to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to drop from the books of accounts by debiting the related Accumulated Depreciation account, crediting the unserviceable Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) and record any gain or loss in the sale of the assets.
observations.managementAction: The GSO conducted disposal of PPE except for request for the motor inspection of unserviceable motor vehicles. The MGSO submitted the annual disposal of PPE except for request for the motor inspection of unserviceable motor vehicles. The MGSO submitted a letter request for the inspection of unserviceable motor vehicles. The MSWDO in connection with collection on SLP coordinated with concerned agencies for remaining unutilized funds.
observations.reason: The MGSO submitted a letter request for the inspection of unserviceable motor vehicles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) – Designate to disclose all necessary information required in the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the report.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer / General Services Officer – Designate requested all the necessary details for the maintenance of property cards of each PPE account from the Accounting Office and promised to submit a complete and detailed RPCPPE and IIRUP for proper disposal of unserviceable property by the end of the year.
observations.reason: The GSO was unable to fully implement the recommendation due to COVID-19 restrictions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. analyze the outstanding claims against the Municipality and ascertain the validity of the same; b. if found that the projects contracted for are completed and finally accepted, immediately process the release of the retention money to the concerned suppliers/contractors; and c. if the obligation to pay is already extinguished and is no longer valid, revert the amount to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared a JEV to set-up the Allowance for Impairment. The Municipal Accountant analyzed the outstanding claims against the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant processed and released P156,384.31 to suppliers. The remaining balance of the retention money is for further notification to the suppliers.
observations.reason: No action was taken by some suppliers; remaining balance of the retention money is for further notification to the suppliers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. the Municipal Social and Welfare Development Officer (MSWDO) to: a.1 send out demand letters to concerned beneficiaries to help enforce collection of the outstanding loans receivable; and a.2 ascertain the financial and physical condition of the loan borrowers and submit report to the Audit Team for verification; and b. the Municipal Accountant to set-up the corresponding allowance for impairment in the books of accounts due to the high-risk of non-collectability of the Loans Receivable – Others account of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management sent out demand letters to concerned beneficiaries to help enforce collection of the outstanding loans receivable. 38 beneficiaries and loan borrowers were visited and reminded of their balances. The amount of P162,000.00 will be requested for write-off because the loan borrowers were either not located, documents such as death certificates and other certification as proof of incapacity to pay off the loan borrowers. Once the documents were collected, it will be submitted to the Commission on Audit with a request to write-off the outstanding balances from the noted loan borrowers. The Municipal Accountant prepared JEV to set-up the Allowance for Impairment.
observations.reason: With the total amount of P405,229.80, only P309,679.80 is under the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office for collection; P162,000.00 requested for write-off pending supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the accounts in the financial statements and reclassify erroneous entries to the most appropriate account for proper presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared JEV to reclassify the account used for payment to Job Order personnel assigned in different departments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. reclassify the recording of disaster related equipment by debiting the account Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment and crediting the account Other Machineries and Equipment; b. adopt the appropriate estimated useful life for these Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) as provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2003-007 in computing the depreciation expense; and c. take-up necessary adjustment in the recorded Accumulated Depreciation as Prior Period Adjustment to be closed to Government Equity for the adjustment arising from the revision on the useful life of PPE.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer mentioned that the Municipal Engineer has submitted partial inventory reports of Municipal and Barangay roads and agreed to maintain Local Road Networks Property Card and submit a complete inventory report of the local road networks. The Municipal Accountant took-up necessary adjustment in the Accumulated Depreciation account as Prior Period Adjustment. The Municipal Accountant prepared JEV to reclassify the recording of disaster related equipment by debiting the account Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment and crediting the account Other Machineries and Equipment. The Municipal Office adopted the appropriated estimated useful life for these PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the erroneous entry in the recording the receipt of the fund in the Trust Fund books by debiting Due to National Government Agencies (NGAs) and crediting Due to Government Owned and Controlled Corporations (GOCCs).
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the correcting JEV to record the receipt of the fund in the Trust Fund books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. instruct the Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies and materials as inventory items and record as expenses only those items actually issued/consumed during the accounting period; b. direct the MGSO to: b.1 properly maintain and observe correct recording in the SC for each type of inventory; b.2 record all receipts and issuances of supplies in the stock cards; b.3 prepare and consolidate all Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for which supplies and materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) with complete details and submit the same to the Municipal Accounting Office (MAO); and b.4 reconcile the Stock Cards periodically with the ledger cards of the MAO; b.5 conduct physical count of inventories semestrally, generate / prepare and submit the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year; and c. require the Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer to: c.1 reconcile the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories with the GL controlling accounts; and c.2 draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant mentioned that the discrepancy of P5,085,750.34 in the balances of Supplies and Materials Expenses as presented in the Statement of Financial Performance pertains to all procurement that are directly recorded as expense instead of inventory. The basis for recording the purchases was the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) attached to the Disbursement Vouchers. Moreover, since the Municipality has no provision for stock room, supplies were immediately issued to the end users/departments. The Municipal Accountant prepared the SSMI only but with incomplete data. The Municipal Accountant prepared the annual physical report only instead of semestral reports due to volume and urgency of other transactions. The Accounting Office ensured that no JEV will be processed without complete supporting documents.
observations.reason: Due to volume and urgency of other transactions, Management was unable to fully implement the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to include in the report on Government Projects, Programs and Activities (GPPAs) all on-going Government Projects, Programs and Activities (GPPAs) of all on-going projects and provide all information required under Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 in the quarterly report on GPPAs as basis for appropriate management decisions and audit actions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer has submitted a revised Quarterly Report on GPPAs for the quarter ending December 31, 2020. The Municipal Accountant stated that the discrepancy of P1,327,580.75 pertains to completed project, "Farm to Market Road (Phase II) at Sitio Pinac, Barangay Masapang which was recorded in the appropriate asset account on January 29, 2021.
observations.reason: The Audit Team will further verify the revised Quarterly GPPA and will monitor the Municipality’s implementation of the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Assessor to fast-track and exhaust all possible means to secure the Original Certificate of Title (OCT) or Transfer Certificate Titles (TCT) of all untitled land as soon as possible, especially those recorded for over 70 years, pursuant to the provisions of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The Management coordinated with the agencies for land titling with regard to those untitled land which have no available documents. The Municipal Assessor and MPDO were requested to fast-track titling.
observations.reason: 6 parcels of land for school used were already donated to DepEd, hence, they are responsible for land titling. Some untitled land acquired in 1949 have no supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. assign a focal person or technical working group who will closely monitor the pre-implementation and implementation of the programs or projects; and b. direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) to coordinate with the focal person, in case other person was assigned, in order to expedite the implementation of the projects under the Sustainable Livelihood Program (SLP) in accordance with the Specific Implementation Agreement (SIA).
observations.managementAction: With regards to the remaining unutilized fund, the MSWDO coordinated with the DSWD Provincial Coordinator of the DSWD-SLP and the Project Development Officer (PDO) and implemented the remaining projects and utilized the fund accordingly. The MSWDO submitted documentation of coordination and implementation.
observations.reason: The remaining unutilized SLP funds were delayed because assignment of PDO was transferred and there was no coordination initially.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Health Officer to efficiently and effectively manage PhilHealth’s Per Family Payment Rate (PFPR) Fund and Capitation Fund (PCF) by: a. allocating the fund based on the priority needs and necessity of the priority projects through careful evaluation and planning; and b. abiding to the provisions set forth under PhilHealth Circular Nos. 010 series of 2012 and 16 series of 2004 on the proper disposition and utilization of the funds to ensure the attainment of the purpose for which the funds were created.
observations.managementAction: Funds were allocated based on the priority needs and necessity of the Municipality. PFPR and PCF funds were used for payment of installation of new electric meter base. Victoria has no hospital and part of Banca-Banca and Masapang National Roads are frequent venues for road accidents. The funds were used for Basic Life Support Training aimed to train and equip our RHU staff and BFP members to prepare them for emergencies and calamities to better serve constituents. Rehabilitation of the birthing facility was intended to repair and improve the facility in order to pass the standards set by the DOH Licensing Office and to be able to secure a License to operate.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to closely monitor the timely implementation of the PPAs under the Municipality’s 20 per cent Development Fund in order to deliver the maximum benefits to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: On the 8th of February 2020, the Municipality launched and kick started the "Zero Waste Campaign". Monthly meetings were held with MDC and issuance of citation ticket is for approval of the Sangguniang Bayan. The remaining 3 projects are still on-going as of year-end. The Acting MPDO monitored on-going projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Mitigation Officer (MDRRMO) to optimize the utilization of the Mitigation Fund by preparing a comprehensive Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Investment Plan (LDRRMIP) and incorporating therein only feasible and implementable PPAs within the calendar year and monitor its strict implementation.
observations.managementAction: Management optimized the utilization of the Mitigation Fund by preparing a comprehensive Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Investment Plan (LDRRMIP). The MDRRMO ensured updated plans and coordinated for re-evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal GAD Focal Person to prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report (AR) with all the required attachments as enumerated in C.8 Item 5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working days from end of January of the preceding year.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person prepared the GAD Accomplishment Report (AR) and submitted on March 20, 2020 thru DILG GAD Plan and Budget Monitoring System.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the MSWMB review and update the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality every two years or as the need arises in compliance with Section 4 (g), Rule VI of the IRR of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: On the 8th of February 2020, the Municipality launched and kick started the "Zero Waste Campaign". The LDRRM plan is scheduled to be re-evaluated and updated this year, CY 2021, for another three-year planning period (2022-2024). Flyers and monthly meetings were conducted. The MDRRMC will ensure the next LDRRMFIP will incorporate PPAs to be funded by unexpended balances.
observations.reason: The planned projects and activities on Solid Waste Management were hindered by the onset of COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) and the members of the Municipal Solid Waste and Management Board (MSWMB) to: a. enforce strict implementation of the provisions of Sections 21 and 22 of RA No. 9003 and Municipal Ordinances Nos. 2018-108 and 2018-109 on the mandatory segregation of solid wastes and carry out the following: a.1 Strengthen information and education campaign activities like continuous information dissemination relative to solid waste management; a.2 Establish close coordination with barangay officials to ensure that efforts to improve the Solid Waste Management will reach the constituents at the barangay level; a.3 Develop a strategic scheme, like providing incentives, for the purpose of encouraging constituents or enterprises to develop or undertake responsible and effective solid waste management; and a.4 Impose fines and penalties for any offense made or violation of Section 48 of RA No. 9003 and pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the Municipal Ordinance Nos. 2018-108 and 109; and b. Implement the closure of the open dump and expedite the construction and operation of a sanitary landfill as a final disposal site for solid wastes to ensure protection of public health and the environment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Government enforced strict implementation of mandatory segregation of solid wastes and ensured that unsegregated solid wastes were not collected. Flyers were distributed to all barangays and schedule of garbage collection were posted. It was proposed to give a reward to the barangay with Best Solid Waste Management. The Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan (SCRP) of the municipal dumpsite will be formulated to ensure closure of the dumpsite; coordination with DENR-EMB is ongoing.
observations.reason: Planned projects and activities on Solid Waste Management were impeded by COVID-19; citation ticket is for approval of Sangguniang Bayan and SCRP formulation coordinated with DENR-EMB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the JEV to correct the capitalized entry by recording Interest Expense and Road Network accounts; and b) Cease capitalizing interest expense of completed projects pursuant to IPSAS 5 -Borrowing Costs.
observations.reason:
Not Implemented
No JEVs were prepared to correct the misstatements. Further, interest payments in CY 2020 were still capitalized to Road Network account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference of ₱411,096.74; and may we be furnished of the JEV to record the adjusting entries for purposes of verification.
observations.reason:
Not Implemented
The CY 2019 Statement of Loan Amortization computed the loans payable to be ₱4,548,672.15 at the end of same year, still leaving a discrepancy of ₱411,096.74. Thus, no reconciliation was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to identify the breakdown of unlocated difference of ₱394,047.57; communicate with the Department of Agriculture on the status of the unlocated amount of ₱394,047.57 if this shall be considered liquidated and to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers of Due to NGA account by Project in order to regularly monitor the remaining balances of funds received from NGAs.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented
No. 4 maintained and unlocated difference of ₱394,047.57 remained identified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to verify the composition of the beginning balances of the Due to BIR account of all funds and prepare and furnish the list required under Section 20 Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; and the Municipal Treasurer to update the RPTARs/Taxpayers’ Index Card and subsequently, prepare and furnish the list required under Section 20 Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented
The composition of the beginning balances of Due to BIR account was not yet verified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 1) Exert effort in identifying the details/breakdown of the beginning balance of the Due to GSIS account; 2) Reconcile amount of GSIS contributions in payrolls/DVs and in subsidiary ledgers; 3) Prepare necessary adjusting entries on the unreconciled balances for the unrequired GSIS contributions for Extra Hazard Premium (EHP) and correcting entries for the errors identified. Furnish copy of the JEVs to record the adjusting entries for purposes of verification.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented
Details of breakdown of CY 2019 beginning balance of Due to GSIS account was not yet provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to stop including EHP as part of GSIS contributions in the payroll of regular employees, reconcile the amounts in GSIS billings and payrolls/DVs to ensure that amounts billed were correctly and properly withheld, submit the Agency Remittance Advice and Electronic Remittance File on the first week of every month, and remit directly to the GSIS the employees’ and employers’ contributions within the first ten (10) days of the calendar month following the month to which the contributions apply.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented
No reconciliation schedule was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the required reconciliations and to disclose either on the face of the Statement of Cash Flows/ SCBAA or in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with the provisions of IPSAS 2 and IPSAS 24 to achieve transparency in financial reporting.
observations.reason:
Not Implemented
The two reconciliations were still not prepared and submitted by the Municipal Accountant to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: require the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to ensure timely implementation of projects and supervise the completion thereof without delays so that the Municipality may no longer be required for the full refund/return of the funds received and for the enjoyment of the benefits of the targeted beneficiaries; and instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate to effect the immediate return of the unexpended/excess balances from completed projects pursuant to Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented
There were already returned/refunded AAR projects in CY 2019 from CY 2018 and prior years, respectively, but there were still AAR projects in CY 2019, 2016 and prior years where funds remained recorded as “Due to NGA” in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, and others concerned implement, without any further delay, the programs/projects under the 20% Development Fund and direct the Bids and Awards Committee to start the procurement activities immediately to attain completion of projects at the end of fiscal year. Henceforth, ensure that the 20% Development Fund is promptly and fully utilized pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented
There were CY 2019 20% DF projects that were completed only in CY 2020. Utilization rate of CY 2020 20% DF with final appropriation (after realignments) of P19,826,690.80 reached 29.52% or P5,853,674.56, leaving unutilized balance of P13,973,016.24 or 70.48%.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) ensure that the LDRRMF is utilized solely for disaster risk reduction and management activities. Moreover, the LCE should require: a) The Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate registries (RAOs); b) The LDRRMO to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, and submit the same within the reglementary period; c) The Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for the LDRRMF and provide adequate disclosures thereof in the Notes to Financial Statements; and d) The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended CY 2019 LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund, pursuant to NDRRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented
The LDRRMO failed to submit the Monthly Report on Sources & Utilization of LDRRMF while the Municipal Accountant failed to transfer the unexpended balance of CY 2019 to the Special Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal management require the members of MSWMB to review and update the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan with timeline for implementation to determine the PPAs to be prioritized during the budget year; intensify efforts to have the Solid Waste Management Plan approved by the NSWMC as required under Section 16 of RA 9003.
observations.reason:
Not Implemented
The LGU failed to submit to the Audit Team a Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan that is approved by the NSWMC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify the collection to recover government funds invested in the program and institute action for the collection of the accounts or appropriate remedial action to clear the balances and if warranted, request/apply for the write-off of the accounts following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented
Of the total RPT and SET collectible in CY 2020 amounting to P7,128,854.67, only P885,229.34 was credited, leaving RPT & SEF Receivable balance of P6,243,625.33 as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to stop the payment of honoraria and other allowances to job order workers pursuant to Section 2 of CSC Memorandum Circular No. 40 and Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations, and submit immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI form to the Audit Team; copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee on Appropriations and the Senate Committee on Finance, as mandated by Section 93 of RA 10964.
observations.reason:
Not Implemented
AAPSI for CY 2020 was not submitted by the management to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal management submit within the deadline a list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year at the beginning of the year; submit project billboard pictures on all projects implemented in CY 2019; maintain updated project status based on the data sourced from the monthly monitoring report prepared by Municipality; and ensure that appropriate project signboards and/or notices have been posted within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented
PPAs for CY 2019 were included in the Annual Budget for implementation but some documentation and timely submissions remained outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify the collection to recover past-due loans and government funds, and the Municipal Accountant to determine the reason why each fund transfer account balance were not liquidated and undertake immediate appropriate action and if warranted, request/apply for write-off of accounts following COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented
There were movements observed in five (5) receivable accounts from CY 2017-2018 up to CY 2020; however as of December 31, 2020, several receivables remain in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Mayor: a) Create and convene the Inventory and Disposal Committee that shall conduct the validation of the PPEs physical existence and submit the necessary report in accordance with the prescribed procedures and timeline; b) Require the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian to maintain a complete and updated PPELC and Property Cards for each class of property and regularly reconcile their records and coordinate with each other for the provision of depreciation and determination of impairment losses of these assets.
observations.reason:
Not Implemented
The inventory committee was not created and the required Property Cards were not prepared and maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Treasurer to update the RPTARs/Taxpayers’ Index Card and subsequently, prepare and furnish the list required under Section 20 Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; and the Municipal Accountant to establish RPT and SET receivables sourcing from the list duly certified by the Treasury Office.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented
In CY 2020, RPT and SET reconciliation and set-up were based on amounts determined in the CY 2019 4th Quarter certified by the Municipal Assessor and not on the full certified list showing taxpayers and amounts due.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a) the Property Custodian to update, prepare and submit all documents and reports such as duly approved RIS to evidence the issuance of the materials from the books; b) the Municipal Accountant to comply with the provisions of Sections 43 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I on the proper recording of purchases and issuances of supplies and materials and to restore to inventory accounts the undocumented P569,213.49 adjustments and maintain the Subsidiary Ledger Card for inventories; c) the Municipal Mayor require Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of inventory items under the perpetual inventory method and the Municipal Social Welfare Officer and Treasurer to furnish copy of delivery receipt/acceptance and inspection report of all received inventory items.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented
In CY 2020, adjustments to inventory accounts were still not supported with SSMIs, some inventories were charged as outright expense, and both Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards were not maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Intra-Agency Accounts and Due to Other Funds to promptly identify any discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and effect the necessary adjustments, if appropriate, to bring the affected accounts into an agreement.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented
In CY 2020, Due from Other Funds was reported at P70,096,185.24 while Due to Other Funds amounted to P72,708,381.14, leaving a discrepancy of P2,612,195.90.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal management enforce proper segregation of solid waste at source and conduct information and dissemination campaign on proper solid waste management so that the constituents will be fully aware of the benefits derived from the program.
observations.reason:
Fully Implemented
PPAs were included in the Annual Budget for implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to render her accounts and submit the required financial transactions together with supporting documents to the Accounting Division and submit the accounts and reports to COA on or before the 10th day of the succeeding month. Likewise, remind accountable officers of sanctions for failure to render accounts.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented
Submission timeliness improved; however, some delays remained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the supporting documents for some entries in the General Journal and schedules, prepare Subsidiary Ledgers correctly and rectify deficiencies; include data for the Special Education Fund in SCBAA and justify noted differences.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented
Some supporting schedules and subsidiary ledgers were prepared and submitted; SCBAA updated to include SEF data.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the lacking supporting documents and rectify deficiencies; refrain from transactions with suppliers where pecuniary interest exists; require contractors/suppliers to issue official receipts in gross amount; and adhere to RA 9184 and its IRR.
observations.reason: Non-submission of some lacking documents. Some justifications and supporting documents remain outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management furnish the audit team with the advice on the review by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan and the LFC of the Province of Northern Samar on the appropriation ordinances; and henceforth strictly adhere to the provisions of Sec. 327 of RA 7160.
observations.reason: The audit team was furnished the advice and the matter was addressed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with COA Circular No. 97-002, PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001, and require employees to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: Some advances remained unliquidated. As of December 31, 2020, Advances to Special Disbursing Officer and Advances to Officers and Employees have balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management refrain from disbursing government funds without complete documentations in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD 1445.
We recommended that management adhere to the required procurement documents and secure required documents prior to hiring private legal consultancy and avoid payments without legal basis.
observations.reason: The LGU hired a private lawyer as special consultant; however, some documentary requirements and proper procurement mode were lacking.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prepare Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Driver’s Trip Tickets using prescribed forms and submit them to COA.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were later provided; however, not all required reports were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require recipient officials and employees to refund the amount in excess of the authorized amount as prescribed in E.O. No. 80 and DBM BC No. 2014-3.
observations.reason: Some refunds were made; unsettled disallowances remained in the Suspensions/Disallowances/Charges (SASDC).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee be required to submit copies of approved contracts/purchase orders within five (5) days after issuance and copies of delivery documents within 24 hours after acceptance of delivered goods and services.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require concerned officers and employees to strictly comply with provisions of E.O. Nos. 248 and 298 and COA Circulars; refund excess amounts and stop issuance of travel authorities for attendance to conventions where not affiliated.
observations.reason: Some employees were required to refund excess amounts. Some procurement of fuel and related documents were later supported; however, several questionable travel expenditures remained.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to
 
a) direct the Market Administrator to strictly implement the provisions of the Market Code specifically on the execution of the contracts and enforcement of the provisions stipulated therein; and
b) make representation to the Sangguniang Bayan for the amendment of the Market Code to include a penalty provision that would enjoin the stallholders to pay their obligations within the designated period.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation:
We reiterate our prior year’s recommendations and the
 
(a) Municipal LSB agreed to follow and perform the activities in the preparation and approval of the SEF Budget with indicative timelines as prescribed by DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 or the Revised Guidelines on the Use of Special Education Fund (SEF); and
 
(b) Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the Report of SEF Utilization quarterly using the prescribed form for submission to appropriate offices.

We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the BAC and its Secretariat and the Municipal Accountant, to ensure that all required supporting documents are properly accomplished and attached to the disbursement vouchers
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018
observations.reason:
Approval of the SEF Budget was still not within the prescribed timeline.

There are still vouchers with incomplete and/or improperly accomplished supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the a) Treasury Office to hasten the updating of Real Property Tax Assessment Register/Taxpayer’s Index Cards and furnish the Accounting Office the list required under Section 20, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and b) Municipal Accountant to base the establishment of RPT and SET receivables on the list submitted by the Treasury Office for an accurate recognition of the said receivables.

2.4	We reiterated our recommendations and the Management agreed to instruct the: a) Municipal Accountant (i) to maintain complete PPELC for each class of PPE to record the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof; (ii) to review or cause the review of prior years’ disbursement vouchers and supporting documents in the custody of the Accounting Office for purposes of tracing back procurement of Work/Zoo Animals  as well as PPEs using the Special Education Fund to facilitate the preparation of PPELCs; (iii) in case the Accounting Office does not maintain copy of prior years’ disbursement vouchers and supporting documents, request from the COA Regional Office, through the Audit Team Leader, for copy of said documents already submitted to the Audit Team in previous years, for the same purpose of completion of PPELCs pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-006 dated September 18, 2013; and (iv) to prepare journal entry voucher to record adjusting entries recognizing the proper PPE account for transactions that were erroneously recorded as outright expense and submit copy of said duly approved journal entry voucher to the Audit Team for monitoring purposes; b) General Services Officer (i) to designate staff who will be responsible in preparing the required PCs of all PPEs including properties acquired from previous years to avoid the recurrence of the audit finding in the next reporting period; and (ii) to coordinate with the Accounting Office for benchmarking and reconciliation of data; and c) Inventory Committee to complete the physical verification of all properties of the Municipality acquired until December 31 and render required reports thereon within the timeline of January 31 of each year.

3.6 	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to promptly request the copies of debit advices issued by the LBP-Tarlac for the timely recognition of payments to reflect accurate year-end balances of the Loans Payable account.

4.7	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: a) require the Municipal Treasurer and collectors to (i) deposit/remit intact and on a daily basis the collections with/to the authorized government depository bank/Municipal Treasurer; (ii) stop the practice of retaining accumulated collections in their custody; (iii) prepare a complete RCD at the close of business day; and (iv) affix their signature in the pertinent section of the RCD; and b) designate a disbursing officer who shall exclusively perform the disbursing functions.

5.4   We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to i) direct the Market Administrator to strictly implement the provisions of the Market Code specifically on the execution of the contracts and enforcement of the provisions stipulated therein; and ii) make representation to the Sangguniang Bayan for the amendment of the Market Code to include a penalty provision that would enjoin the stallholders to pay their obligations within the designated period and duration of the lease contracts if they intend to allow lease contracts exceeding one year.

6.4   We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to (i) prepare the necessary LDRRMFIP in accordance with the prescribed format; (ii) refrain from using the LDRRMF for programs/projects/activities which are not directly related to disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations; and (iii) ensure the compliance of the Municipality with respect to minimum standards on disaster preparedness; and (b)  Municipal Accountant (i) to adhere to the applicable guidelines on accounting and reporting of the LDRRMF by recognizing asset or expense account instead of Subsidy to Other Funds, where appropriate and by recording payables only for rendered or delivered services or goods; and (ii) coordinate with the BAC Secretariat for purposes of submitting the lacking supporting documents identified in this report.

7.15	We recommended that the Management (i) utilize the 20% development funds in accordance with the parameters set forth in the Joint Memorandum Circular and, in relation thereto, desist from charging inappropriate expenditures against the development fund such as administrative expenses which can be charged against the regular budget of the agency; (ii) comply with the documentary requirements and relevant provisions of R.A. No. 9184 specifically those relating to the submission of performance securities and results of eligibility check/screening; and (iii) submit the lacking documents mentioned in the report in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
7.16	Management Comment: The Municipality conveyed that some documents that were noted were already submitted to the Audit Team and the other necessary actions and recommendations given by the COA will be implemented immediately.
7.17	Auditor’s Rejoinder: We validated the Management’s reply claiming that all supporting documents were submitted.  In our validation, we determined that there were still lacking documents which are required to support the disbursements. Hence, we reiterate our audit recommendation for the Management to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents.

9.8   We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct: a) the MENRO, in coordination with the SWMB, to fully implement and monitor effectively the Solid Waste Management Plan and activities which focus on preserving and protecting the environment, and b) the MENRO and support offices to strictly implement the provisions of Municipal Ordinance No. 2007-81, specifically the proper segregation of wastes in order to instill discipline among constituents and attain the objectives R.A. No. 9003.

10.3	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: (a) require all GAD officials concerned to develop and maintain a GAD Database as source of information for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and (b) instruct the GAD Focal Person to (i) ensure that the GAD AR is accompanied by a brief summary of the reported program/project and copies of reported policy issuances and (ii) submit in writing the changes in the DILG-approved GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and secure an acknowledgement receipt of adjusted GPB from the DILG to ensure the compliance of their adjustments to the requirements of the joint memorandum circular on the localization of the Magna Carta of Women.

11.11	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the ME and the Bids and Awards Committee to (i) consider the project specific provisions of DILG MC 2019-71 in the implementation of AM projects (ii) furnish the Auditor advance copies of contracts of similar transactions and their supporting documents within five (5) days from the execution thereof; and (iii) adhere to the above-cited provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 and RA No. 9184 and its IRR on the procurement and documentation process.
11.12	Management Comment: The Municipality conveyed that some documents that were noted were already submitted to the Audit Team and the other necessary actions and recommendations given by the COA will be implemented immediately.
11.13	Auditor’s Rejoinder: We validated Management’s reply claiming that all supporting documents were submitted.  In our validation, we determined that there were still lacking documents which are required to support the disbursements. Hence, we reiterate our audit recommendation for the Management to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents.

12.5	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the: a) Human Resources and Management Officer i) to comply with the relevant provisions of CSC Resolution No. 020790 in drafting the Contracts of service/job order for all JO workers; and ii) to ensure the correctness and completeness of details in the DTRs of Jos; and b) Municipal Treasury and Municipal Accountant to comply with documentary requirements provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 before the grant of cash advances for the payment of wages of JOs and Casuals and in the liquidation of the same.
13.5	We recommended and the Management agreed to continuously observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR.
14.3   We recommended and the Management agreed to continuously remit the GSIS premiums within the prescribed period.
15.2	We recommended and the Management agreed to continuously remit the Pag-IBIG premiums within the prescribed period provided for under the aforementioned law.
16.3   We recommended and the Management agreed to continuously remit the PHIC premiums within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services
Officer (MGSO) – Designate to maintain property cards for each Property, Plant
and Equipment (PPE) of the Municipality and reconcile with the ledger cards of the
Municipal Accounting office.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to drop in the PPE account the small value
items by debiting Prior Period Adjustment and Accumulated Depreciation of the
respective PPE, and crediting the respective PPE accounts in the amount of
P1,290,301.60, P1,178,390.37 and P2,468,691.97, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
 the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) – Designate to facilitate the disposal of
the unserviceable property thru the submission of a request for disposal to the Office of
the Auditor and the duly accomplished Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable
Property (IIRUP);
 the Municipal Disposal Committee to prepare and submit to the Office of the Auditor the
Appraisal Report and other required documents for evaluation and inspection and initiate
the immediate disposal of these unserviceable properties in accordance with the rules and
procedures prescribed in COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992;
 the Municipal Accountant, upon disposal, to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to drop
from the books of accounts by debiting the related Accumulated Depreciation account,
crediting the unserviceable Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) and record any gain or
loss in the sale of the assets.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General Services
Officer (MGSO) – Designate to disclose all necessary information required in the Report on
the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to ascertain the accuracy
and reliability of the report.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. analyze the outstanding claims against the Municipality and ascertain the
validity of the same;
b. if found that the projects contracted for are completed and finally accepted,
immediately process the release of the retention money to the concerned
suppliers/contractors; and
c. if the obligation to pay is already extinguished and is no longer valid, revert
the amount to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. the Municipal Social and Welfare Development Officer (MSWDO) to:
 send out demand letters to concerned beneficiaries to help enforce collection of the
outstanding loans receivable; and
 ascertain the financial and physical condition of the loan borrowers and submit report
to the Audit Team for verification.
b. the Municipal Accountant to set-up the corresponding allowance for impairment in the
books of accounts due to the high-risk of non-collectability of the Loans Receivable –
Others account of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the
accounts in the financial statements and reclassify erroneous entries to the most appropriate
account for proper presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. reclassify the recording of disaster related equipment by debiting the account
Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment and crediting the account Other
Machineries and Equipment;
b. adopt the appropriate estimated useful life for these Property, Plant and
Equipment (PPE) as provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2003-007 in
computing the depreciation expense; and
c. take-up necessary adjustment in the recorded Accumulated Depreciation as
Prior Period Adjustment to be closed to Government Equity for the
adjustment arising from the revision on the useful life of PPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the erroneous entry in recordings the
receipt of the fund in the Trust Fund books by debiting Due to National
Government Agencies (NGAs) and crediting Due to Government Owned and
Controlled Corporations (GOCCs) in the amount of P311,461.97 for fair
presentation in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
 record all purchases of supplies and materials as inventory items and record as
expenses only those items actually expended/consumed during the accounting period;
and
 refrain from approving Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) without complete documents to
support the entries made.
b. direct the Municipal General Services Officer – Designate to:
 consolidate weekly the Requisition and Issuance Slips (RIS) for which supplies and
materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI)
and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant; and
 conduct a complete physical count of inventories and submit the duly accomplished
reports thereon to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on
the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units
(LGUs), Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to
include in the report on Government Projects, Programs and Activities (GPPAs) all
on-going project and provide all information required under Section 2.1 of COA
Circular No. 2013-004 in the quarterly report on GPPAs as basis for appropriate
management decisions and audit actions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and
Development Officer and the Municipal Assessor to fast-track and exhaust all
possible means to secure the Original Certificate of Title (OCT) or Transfer
Certificate Titles (TCT) of all untitled land as soon as possible, especially those
recorded for over 70 years, pursuant to the provisions of PD No. 1445 and COA
Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. assign a focal person or a technical working group who will closely monitor the
pre-implementation and implementation of the programs or projects; and
b. direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) to
coordinate with the focal person, in case other person was assigned, in order to
expedite the implementation of the projects under the Sustainable Livelihood
Program (SLP) in accordance with the covering Specific Implementation
Agreement (SIA).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Health Officer to
efficiently and effectively manage PhilHealth’s Per Family Payment Rate (PFPR)
Fund and Capitation Fund (PCF) by:
a. allocating the fund based on the priority needs and necessity of the projects
through careful evaluation and planning; and
b. abiding to the provisions set forth under PhilHealth Circular Nos. 010 series of
2012 and 16 series of 2004 on the proper disposition and utilization of the funds
to ensure the attainment of the purpose for which the funds were created.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a) verify the composition of the “unreconciled balances” of the Cash in Bank
– LCCA account per books and per bank records;
b) regularly prepare bank reconciliation statements for each bank account;
and
c) prepare the necessary journal entries to take up reconciling items pursuant
to Section 74 of PD 1445 and Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the JEV to correct the
erroneous entry by recording Interest Expense and Road Network accounts
of ₱715,097.79 and ₱754,341.49, respectively and the Loans Payable –
Domestic account of ₱39,243.70;
b) Cease capitalizing interest expense of completed projects pursuant to IPSAS
5 -Borrowing Costs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to reconcile the difference of ₱411,096.74 of Loans Payable – Domestic account
between the accounting records of the Municipality and of the Municipal
Development Fund Office. Also, may we be furnished of the JEV to record the
adjusting entries for purposes of verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to identify the breakdown of unlocated difference of ₱394,047.57; communicate
with the Department of Agriculture on the status of the utilized amount of
₱5,512,598.38 for Erenas Small Water Impounding Project if this shall be
considered as liquidated and to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers of Due
to NGA account by Project in order to regularly monitor the remaining balances
of funds received from NGAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to exert effort to verify the composition of the beginning balances of the Due to
BIR account of all funds and prepare the necessary adjusting and correcting
entries for the errors identified.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to:
1) Exert effort in identifying the details/breakdown of the beginning balance of
the Due to GSIS account;
2) Reconcile amount of GSIS contributions in payrolls/DVs and in subsidiary
ledgers;
3) Prepare necessary adjusting entries on the unreconciled balances for the
unrequired GSIS contributions for Extra Hazard Premium (EHP) and
correcting entries for the errors identified. Furnished copy of the JEVs to
record the adjusting entries for purposes of verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to stop including EHP as part of GSIS contributions in the payroll of regular
employees, reconcile the amounts in GSIS billings and payrolls/DVs to ensure
that amounts billed were correctly and properly withheld, submit the Agency
Remittance Advice and Electronic Remittance File on the first week of every
month, and remit directly to the GSIS the employees’ and employers’
contributions within the first ten (10) days of the calendar month following the
month to which the contributions apply.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to prepare the required
reconciliations and to disclose either on the face of the Statement of Cash Flows/
SCBAA or in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with the
provisions of IPSAS 2 and IPSAS 24 to achieve transparency in financial
reporting.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require
 
all Collecting Officers to turn over their collections intact daily at the close of business day to the Municipal Treasurer; and
 
b) the latter to deposit all the turned over collections to authorized depository bank daily to avoid the possible misuse of cash in their possession.

We also recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to 
a) closely monitor the issuance of Accountable Forms to the Collecting Officers and instruct them to i) issue ORs in strict numerical sequence and indicate details of check payments in the AFs; and i) stop tsfer/borrowing of AFs;
 
b) avoid the delegation of collection function to Job Orders; and
 
c) indicate the name of the collector remitting collections and the corresponding report no. and amount in the collection portion of her RCDs.

Furthermore, we recommended and Municipal Treasurer agreed to verify and count, not only the cash remitted but also the unissued accountable forms presented and ensure that all collections are remitted intact by all collectors concerned to prevent loss or misuse of government funds.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018
