Lack of manpower to cover all collection function.

There are instances when collections are not remitted intact.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct:
 
(a) the Treasury Office to update the RPTARs/ Taxpayers’ Index Card and subsequently, prepare and furnish the Accounting Office the list required under Section 20 Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; and
 
(b) the Municipal Accountant to establish RPT and SET receivables sourcing from the list duly certified by the Treasury Office at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed to instruct the:

a.	Municipal Accountant (i) to maintain complete PPELC for each class of property to record the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof; and (ii) to make necessary adjustments and recognize financial transactions properly to avoid misstatement of affected accounts;
b.	Municipal Treasurer to prepare PC of all PPE including properties acquired from previous years; and

c. 	Inventory Committee to complete the physical verification of all properties of the Municipality and render required reports thereon.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018
observations.reason: Submitted required reports but the physical count conducted was still incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to present only the portion of the loan that is due to be settled within twelve months as current liability as prescribed in paragraph 80 of PPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to
 
a) recognize the revenue in the year it is earned and accrue the current year’s uncollected lease rental at year-end; 

b) recognize utility expense in the year it is incurred; and 

c) make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the errors committed in the recognition of financial transactions and events for CY 2018.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat and the Accounting Office to
 
a) exercise due diligence in preparing documents and processing disbursements and to ensure that required supporting documents are properly accomplished and attached to the disbursement vouchers; and 

b) submit or settle immediately the deficiencies noted in Annex B.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018
observations.reason: There are still vouchers with incomplete and/or improperly accomplished supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that
 
a) Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat Office and the Municipal Engineer to submit the lacking documents and to observe the instructions on contract submission prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-01; and 

b) Municipal Accountant check the completeness of supporting documents before effecting the payments of obligations in view of the contracts’ deliverables.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018
observations.reason: There are still vouchers with incomplete and/or improperly accomplished supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Human Resource and Management Office to ensure compliance on setting the minimum limit of monthly take home pay of Municipal employees in accordance with prevailing statute.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018
observations.reason: A few employees still have take home pay of below P5000.00.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the OIC-MENRO to conduct the Information and Education Campaigns the soonest possible time to remind the residents of the existing environmental laws and policies highlighting fines and penalties when violations are committed. 

We also reiterated our recommendation and the LCE agreed to instruct the solid waste management board to continue to monitor the effective implementation of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018
observations.reason: Partially Implemented | Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. 	Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat, Accounting Office and the Engineering Office submit the lacking documents;
 
b. 	Management consider the timelines of project implementation and improve its monitoring functions or supervision for projects being implemented to avoid the incurrence of delays; and
 
c. 	Management strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR and the DILG Memorandum Circular in the implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipality agreed to 

(a) hasten the transfer of documents between departments to ensure the timely recording of transactions; and 

(b) gather all pertinent documents and analyze the subject accounts to establish the accuracy of their balances especially those accounts with negative balances. 

Furthermore, we reiterate our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Human Resource and Management Officer to 

(a) remit all the due taxes and premiums within the prescribed period; and
 
(b) reconcile the withheld amounts and actual remittances periodically to ensure that all deductions are remitted intact and on time.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal GSO – Designate to: a. conduct a complete physical count of the property of the Municipality, and prepare and submit a properly accomplished RPCPPE not later than January 31 of the following year; b. prepare and maintain Property Cards for each category of PPE to support the balances appearing in the PPE Ledger Cards of the Accounting Office; and c. reconcile the RPCPPE with the Property Cards of the GSO and PPE Ledger Cards of the Accounting Office.
observations.managementAction: Conducted a complete physical count of all the properties of the Municipality, and prepare and submit a properly accomplished RPCPPE later than January 31 of the following year; Property Cards were still not maintained by the Municipal GSO.
observations.reason: Not Implemented Because the Municipal GSO believes it will suffice the requirement for maintaining Property Cards.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal GSO – Designate to: a. maintain and update Stock Cards for each supplies/property to support the balances reported in the Supplies Ledger Cards; and b. conduct physical count of all its inventories to establish the actual and existing inventory items and prepare and submit to the Audit Team every six months, the duly certified and approved RPCI for each type of inventory.
observations.managementAction: A copy of the RPCI was submitted to the Office of the Auditor on August 20, 2020, upon request of the Audit Team.
observations.reason: No action was taken by the Management to take stocks were left in the custody of the Municipal GSO – Designate.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to analyze the accounts that are dormant and long outstanding and consider the applicability of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) I, and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor; request for write-off of the amount due from those who were deceased, sickly, elderly, and those who have no capacity to pay for evaluation.
observations.managementAction: A request for write-off of the amount due from those who were deceased, sickly, elderly and those who have no capacity to pay for the loan was forwarded to the Office of the Auditor for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials to coordinate with the Land Bank of the Philippines, Santa Cruz Branch as an initial step to shift the regular payroll system from manual to automated payroll system to address the risk of loss of funds and ensure the timely delivery of salaries to all employees of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: An ATM Machine was constructed and is now operational. The Municipality started to adopt the automated payroll system on July 1, 2020 for the shift of the regular employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to see to it that the 20 per cent Development Fund is optimally utilized as required under Section 5.0 of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 to ensure that the desired socio-economic and environmental outcomes are achieved.
observations.managementAction: Of the total appropriation, a total amount of P7,236,559.23 was utilized from the CY 2018 continuing appropriation of 20 per cent.
observations.reason: Due to the concurrent position of the Municipal Administrator, strict monitoring of the implementation of the projects was not facilitated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality optimize the utilization of the Mitigation Fund focusing on programs, projects and activities that will strengthen its capacity to prevent and mitigate disaster risks.
observations.managementAction: The Mitigation Fund calendar year 2018 appropriation, a total amount of P549,915.80 was used during CY 2019.
observations.reason: Due to the immediate concerns and limited monitoring, the implementation of the projects was not facilitated.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor appoint permanent employees as Head and Staff of the MDRRMO to satisfy the requirements of Sections 4.2 and 5.3 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-01, and effectively discharge the attached duties and responsibilities.
observations.managementAction: A permanent employee was appointed as MDRRMO on November, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MSWMB to review and update the Ten-Year MSWMP every two years or as the need arises to ensure the sustainability, viability and effectiveness of the solid waste management strategy of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The MSWMB is in the process of finalizing the revised MSWMP and will submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Due to global emergencies like the eruption of Taal volcano and the COVID-19 pandemic the updating of the plan was put on hold.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Barangay Officials to look for conducive locations for the establishment of MRFs so that all barangays have MRFs in compliance with Section 1, Rule XI of the IRR of RA No. 9003 and DILG MC No. 2009-168 and include a composting operation in the MRFs and ensure that the MRFs are fully operating and functioning.
observations.managementAction: MRFs were established in nine barangays of the Municipality. Established MRFs in six barangays were not fully operating and functioning as required; the Municipality is taking steps to make them functional.
observations.reason: Difficulty finding open spaces suitable for establishment of MRFs; some established MRFs are not fully operating.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person and other concerned officials to submit the Annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and GAD Accomplishment Report (GAR) to the Commission on Audit and to oversight government bodies in compliance with Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 and Item V of Joint Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA for timely and comprehensive evaluation of the effectiveness of GAD programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: The Annual GAD Budget and Accomplishment Report were submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Designated General Services Officer (GSO), Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer continue to work hand in hand in tracing/inves-tiga-ting the discrepancies between the RPCPPE and accounting records and adjust them; GSO prepare Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN) and maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and Local Road Network Property Ledger Card in accordance with the COA Circular No. 2015-008; and Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliatio n is on-going with minimal amount of discrepancy for further validation. Adjustments were made as to the Local Road Network as a result of joint efforts of the offices of the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer. Due to mandatory retirement of the Assistant Municipal Treasurer, who is handling GSO functions, the Municipality appointed a new GSO on October 16, 2019. Plantilla position for permanent GSO will be created under CY 2020 annual plan and budget.
observations.reason: The Municipality failed to conduct a complete physical count of their entire properties and prepare RPCPPE. As a result, no reconciliation was done between property and accounting records. Also, no RPCLRN were prepared and no Report on Local Road Network was maintained. The Accounting Office was able to prepare the Local Road Network Ledger Card and reported 31 local road networks within the LGU’s locality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairman submit the necessary documents for audit purpose; and BAC (i) submit the satisfactory explanation on the noted observations; and (ii) strictly adheres to the provisions of the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 for the above-mentioned infrastructure projects and procurement of goods.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Chairman partially submitted the lacking documents for various projects on September 27, 2019. Additional documents were also submitted to the Audit Team on February 7, 2020.
observations.reason: Some of the lacking documents were not yet submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Special Disbursing Officer (SDO) also maintain her own cashbook; and (b) both SDO and DO accomplish the cashbooks in accordance with Section 28, Volume II of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) to establish proper accountability.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Treasurer (AMT) who is acting as DO/SDO is now accomplishing the cashbooks in accordance with Section 28, Volume II of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for proper establishment of accountability.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials and staff through the Municipal Accountant submit the supporting documents required by Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 for audit purposes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal officials and employees concerned submitted to the Audit Team on March 5, 2019 the original Certificate of Appearance of all attendees and the copy of Official Receipt (OR) No. 9692539 dated May 31, 2018 amounting to ₱8,000.00 for the refund of registration fee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC and designated General Services Officer (GSO) to comply with the period of submission of executed contracts and their supporting documents under Sections 3.1.1 and 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: With the mandatory retirement of Assistant Municipal Treasurer who handles the GSO functions, the Municipal Mayor designated personnel under the Office of the Municipal Mayor to prioritize GSO related activities especially the submission of POs and contracts within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: For CY 2019, out of 24 contracts submitted, only four contracts were submitted within five working days from the execution (infrastructure) or issuance (goods) of the contract. The Municipality still failed to submit the executed contracts and POs within the prescribed period. The late submission precluded timely review by COA TAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the: a. Municipal Mayor designate Special Disbursing Officer (SDO) to handle the disbursing functions for the Supplemental Feeding Program (SFP); and b. Officer-in-Charge (OIC) - Municipal Treasurer (i) monitor the fidelity bond of every AO for public funds and request for an increase of bond to cover the maximum cash accountability provided under BTr Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009; and (ii) require the DO and SDO to maintain cashbook and properly accomplish it pursuant to Section 28 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGU).
observations.managementAction: The AMT was designated as the DO. Although, the funds for Supplemental Feeding and Social Pension programs of DSWD will no longer be downloaded to LGUs. Moreover, the Municipality appropriated sufficient fidelity bond for the AMT to cover her various CAs accountability. Her fidelity bond was amounted to ₱1,500,000.00 under risk no. 27-20-0314N effective from January 22, 2020 to January 22, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) (i) submit satisfactory explanation on the observation noted and (ii) strictly adhere to the provisions of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 for the Concreting of Road at Alialy Street, Poblacion, Victoria, Oriental Mindoro; and b. Municipal Accountant, Engineer and BAC submit the necessary documents for audit purposes. Moreover, the Technical Evaluation and Inspection Reports by the COA TAS recommended that the Municipal Accountant deduct from the amount due to King’s Builders and Development Corporation the cost deficiency of ₱10,899.12 or to demand payment for such amounts, as the case may be.
observations.managementAction: The BAC, Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer adhered to the recommendation of COA and assured to strictly follow the provisions of 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184. King’s Builder and Development Corporation refunded the amount of ₱10,899.12 under Accountable Form (AF) No. 51 with serial no. 9691038 dated July 6, 2018. Furthermore, additional documents were submitted on February 7, 2020.
observations.reason: The following documents remained unsubmitted to the Audit Team: Copy of the OR for the purchase of bid documents by the bidder; Warranty security; and Clearance from the Provincial Treasurer that the corresponding sand and gravel fees have been paid. Further, the submitted minutes of pre-bid conference and notice of post-qualification were dated March 12, 2018 and August 2, 2016, respectively, which were not consistent with the schedule of the said procurement activities indicated in the invitation to observers, to wit: Pre-bid: August 18, 2016 Post-Qualification Activity: September 2, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) (i) submit satisfactory explanation on the observation noted and (ii) strictly adhere to the provisions of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 for Construction of Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office and OPCEN; b. Municipal Accountant, Engineer and BAC submit the necessary documents for audit purposes; and c. Municipal Accountant deduct from the amount due to B5 Builders and Supplies the cost deficiency of ₱43,900.15 or to demand payment for such amounts, as the case may be. Henceforth, the Technical Evaluation and Inspection Reports by the COA TAS dated March 8, 2018 was reiterated.
observations.managementAction: The BAC, Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer adhered to the recommendation of COA and will strictly follow the provisions of 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184. B5 Builders and Supplies refunded the amount of ₱43,900.15 under AF No. 51 with serial no. 9691037 dated July 6, 2018. Furthermore, additional documents were submitted on February 7, 2020.
observations.reason: The following documents remained unsubmitted to the Audit Team: Warranty Security; and Clearance from the Provincial Treasurer that the corresponding sand and gravel fees have been paid. Further, the submitted minutes of pre-bid conference and notice of post-qualification were dated March 12, 2018 and August 2, 2016, respectively, which were not consistent with the schedule of the said procurement activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Mayor and the concerned officials see to it that the criteria cited in Republic Act (RA) No. 9003 for establishment of controlled dumpsites and/or sanitary landfill are complied to mitigate the present conditions in the Municipality, see to it that the central disposal facility is established and that the barangays burying and dumping their waste in the backyard are informed of proper waste disposal.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted proof of the successful online application for ECC for Environment Monitoring Board (EMB). Moreover, they are still waiting for the result of further screening to fully utilize the structure for waste disposal activity of the LGU. The evaluation report from proper authorities as of January 20, 2020 was also submitted.
observations.reason: During CY 2019, the Municipality submitted the requirements online regarding their ECC application. However, in the Evaluation Report (ER) from DENR-EMB, there were still lacking documents and information which needs to be submitted. It was also stated in the ER that necessary penalty will be imposed because of their violation to Section 4 of P.D 1586. Furthermore, the MENRO-Designate discussed that the lacking requirements were ready for submission and they are just waiting for the evaluation report on the site observation which was done by the DENR staff last December 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees: a. explain why there was excessive claim of traveling expenses, otherwise, refund the amount of ₱192,159.92 for overpayment; b. submit the necessary documents for the payment of travelling expenses; and c. Henceforth, ensure the compliance with the provisions set forth in Section 4 of EO No. 298 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Aside from prior years' submission, additional documents for further evaluation and appreciation were submitted to the Audit Team on February 7, 2020 as follows: Copy of ORs as proof for the refund of the excess claims by Imelda Callo (now former Vice Mayor (VM)) (₱2,200.00); Tanie Malitao Jr. (₱2,200.00); Julieta Janeo (₱1,540.00) and Mark Mahlon Parial (₱1,800.00). Copy of the OR for the payment of the membership fee in the Vice Mayors League of the Philippines (VMLP), Copies of OR and Certificate of Appearance of Imelda Callo (former VM) for the 4th Qtr 2016 Regular National Executive Board Meeting (RNEBM) of the VMLP, and a certification for the registration fee and Certificate of Appearance of Mr. Luis Castillet Sr. for 2nd Qtr 2016 RNEBM of the VMLP, and Certificate of Appearance of eight (8) Municipal officials for the Training Workshop on the PFM Assessment Tool, Public Financial Management Assessment Tool (PFMAT) Software and PFM Improvement Plan for Local Government Units (LGUs) on August 15 to 16, 2016.
observations.reason: The following documents are still for submission: Certification/s issued by the LCE that the claims of the municipal officials and employees for hotel accommodation in excess of the allowable hotel room/lodging rate of ₱400.00 is absolutely necessary in accordance with EO No. 298 for Hon. Michael Malaluan and Hon. Rowell Ganad; Certified true copy of the OR for the payment of term membership for VMLP and the OR for the 4th Qtr 2016 RNEBM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Designated General Services Officer (GSO) prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for the unserviceable properties to be approved by proper authorities; b. Municipal Mayor create Committee on Appraisal of Property to appraise all the unserviceable properties and request the disposal of the same pursuant to Section 379 of RA No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 92-386; and c. Municipal Accountant explain the non-recording of two motor vehicles.
observations.managementAction: The Management already submitted the necessary documents to COA thru the Municipal Administrator. Explanation of the Municipal Accountant was already submitted to COA in CY 2018. Recording was already made and motor vehicles subsidiary ledgers are reconciled with the records of previous GSO designate.
observations.reason: The Municipal officials and employees concerned is still waiting for the action of the COA TAS, IV-B relative the technical evaluation and inspection of the unserviceable properties for the subsequent disposal of the said properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the rest of the members of the Joint Committee of the CNA submit valid justification s for items (a) and (b) and the required document in item (c) as required in the afore-cited circulars. In case of non-compliance to the above mentioned recommendation, the Municipal Mayor, Accountant and Treasurer enforce the refund of the CNA Incentive received by the 107 municipal officials and employees totaling ₱2,096,000.
observations.managementAction: Additional documents for further evaluation and appreciation were submitted to the Audit Team including justification and cost cutting measures practiced/implemented by LGU as proof of entitlement of the employees for the CNA benefits.
observations.reason: The cost-cutting measures and systems improvement measures undertaken by the Municipality were identified in the submitted CNA between the Municipal Government of Victoria and VICMEA dated February 13, 2015. However, as of October 31, 2015, the proof that the LGU have accomplished at least 70% of its targeted PPAs was still not submitted by the Municipality. Instead, they submitted the SAAOB as of December 31, 2015 and documents relative to the Establishment of Manpower Development Center (Phase I) which has an original expiry date of October 12, 2015 but was not completed on December 11, 2015 which was not also included in the submitted SAAOB. The SWA for the said project as of October 31, 2015 was not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board (MSWMB) in coordination with the Municipal Health Officer and other concerned agencies provide necessary training for the SWM staff to ensure that the solid wastes are handled properly and to minimize the risk of hazards to the SWM personnel and the public. We also recommended that the MSWMB observe the standards and requirements for the collection, transportation and handling of solid wastes required by the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (RA) No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: Copy of proof of the conduct of formal training and seminar of SWM staff for the collection, handling and transporting of wastes certified by the Municipal Health Officer was submitted to the Audit Team on February 2, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality encourage the barangay to establish MRF that will receive the mixed wastes for final sorting, segregation, composting or recycling before transferring the residual wastes to sanitary landfill. Moreover, we suggested that MSWMB strictly implement and monitor the collection and segregation of wastes at the barangay level as required by the provisions of RA No. 9003 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR).
observations.managementAction: Currently, there are ten (10) barangays with MRF as certified by the newly hired Special Assistant on Environment and Natural Resources.
observations.reason: Despite the addition of the five (5) new MRFs, the 22 barangays have not yet established their own MRFs. Interview with the MENRO-Designate also disclosed that waste segregation was not consistently maintained because there are times that the collectors place all types of wastes in one dump truck/compactor and residents putting their garbage out of schedule.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that an updated project reports covering the whole period of operation be rendered by the concerned Municipal and MinSCAT officials/employees. Records, particularly subsidiary ledgers, showing the construction costs of these capital assets, a clear financial report on the results of the project operation and inventory reports should be produced for further audit. Thereafter, any balance of cash should be turned over to the Municipality while the fixed assets should be transferred to MinSCAT where they have been erected. The corresponding accounting entries as these procedures are complied with would logically close the erroneous Investment account.
observations.managementAction: Copy of proof of the conduct of formal training and seminar of SWM staff for the collection, handling and transporting of wastes certified by the Municipal Health Officer was submitted to the Audit Team on February 2, 2020. Meeting with the Accountant of MinSCAT-Main campus was already made together with the GSO informing them of the content of the Audit Team findings/recommendations specifically on remaining cash if there is and structures erected on MinSCAT Campus. The GSO is tasked on making inventory if there are existing structures in the books of MinSCAT under ACBS program. It was also agreed that formal letter will be made addressed to the President of MinSCAT regarding this matter.
observations.reason: Continuous coordination between the Municipality and MinSCAT is being undertaken. Further actions including turnover and transfer of fixed assets to MinSCAT and formalization through correspondence are in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the: a) Local Chief Executive direct Municipal Treasurer to intensify the collection of funds invested in the program and instruct the Municipal Accountant to institute action for the collection of the accounts or other appropriate remedies to clear the amount in the books; and b) Municipal Mayor instruct Municipal Accountant to determine the reason on why each fund transfer account balance were not liquidated and undertake immediate appropriate action and if warranted, request/apply for the write-off of the accounts following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The LGU was not able to trace back these accounts for it existed long ago and no documents were available to support it.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the Municipal Mayor: (a) Create and convene the Inventory and Disposal Committee that shall conduct the validation of the PPEs physical existence and status, and submit the necessary reports such as duly approved RIS to evidence the issuance of the materials from stock and Report of Supplies and Materials Issued etc. as basis for crediting the non-existing balance from the books; and (b) Require the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian to maintain a compete and updated PPELC and PCs respectively for each class of property and regularly reconcile their records and coordinate with each other for the provision of depreciation and determination of impairment losses of these assets.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The inventory committee was not required reports maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the: a) Local Chief Executive remit and deposit collections daily to the authorized depository bank to properly safeguard the government funds; b) prepare Daily Statement of Collections and Accountable Forms (DSCAF) as required by pertinent provisions; and c) transfer the funds to LBP-Allen Branch in order to minimize the delay of deposit.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: The LGU was able to facilitate on remittance and deposit of collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the Local Chief Executive to instruct the: a) Treasury Office to update the RPTARs/Taxpayers’ Index Card and subsequently, prepare and furnish the Accounting Office the list required under Section 20 Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; and b) The Municipal Accountant to establish RPT and SET receivables sourcing from the list duly certified by the Treasury Office.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: The LGU was not able to submit the list.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that: a) Property Custodian update, prepare and submit all documents and reports such as duly approved RIS to evidence the issuance of the materials from stock and Report of Supplies and Materials Issued etc. as basis for crediting the non-existing balance from the books; b) Municipal Accountant to: • Comply with the provisions of Sections 43 of the Manual of the NGAS, Volume I on the proper recording of purchases and issuances of supplies and materials; • Restore to inventory accounts the undocumented P569,213.49 adjustments; and • Maintain the Subsidiary Ledger Card for inventories; c) Municipal Mayor require Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of inventory items under the perpetual inventory method in its related inventory account and also the Municipal Social Welfare Officer and Treasurer to furnish the copy of delivery receipt/acceptance and inspection report of all received inventory items.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: The inventory committee was not required reports maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Intra-Agency Accounts, “Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds” to promptly identify any discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and effect the necessary adjustments, if appropriate, to bring the affected accounts into an agreement.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that: a) Local School Board (LSB) develop a more comprehensive budget priorities based on logical framework of program to ensure that all components and activities were performed in line with the Programmed design and that it is efficient achieving its objectives; b) Municipal Mayor as the Chairman of the Local School Board to: • Give priority for the repair of class of the Acedillo Elementary School to provide students of the facilities that are conducive to learning; c) Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the expenditures based on the appropriated amount as mandated on the approved LSB Resolution For the strict observance of limitations on the use of the Special Education Fund as provided for in Section 4 of DEPED/ DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 01, series of 2017 in the utilization of SEF.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: AAR 2016 Finding No. 1

observations.recommendation: AAR 2018
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: AAR 2017 Finding No. 4; AAR 2016 Finding No. 6; AAR 2015 Finding No. 2

observations.recommendation: We recommend that: a) LCE enforce proper segregation of solid waste at source and information and dissemination campaign on proper solid waste management be conducted so that the constituents will be fully aware of the benefits derived from the program.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: A similar observation is incorporated in Finding No. 11 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to prudently monitor and evaluate the implementation of the development projects to ensure that the 20% DF is optimally utilized to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: AAR 2016 Finding No. 5

observations.recommendation: We recommend that: a) Local Chief Executive direct the department heads of the implementing offices received from various national agencies for the timely implementation and completion of remaining projects without further delay for the enjoyment of benefits of the targeted beneficiaries therefrom; and b) Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate to effect the immediate return of the unexpended/excess balances of the same to the concerned source agencies after the implementation of each project.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: AAR 2016 Finding No. 4

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and require the GAD Focal Point Person to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget as well as GAD Accomplishment Report and submit to DILG for review and endorsement.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: AAR 2016 Finding No. 12

observations.recommendation: Since fiscal responsibility rests upon the Head of the Agency, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to render her accounts and submit the required financial transactions together with supporting documents to the Accounting Division, and the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts and reports to COA on or before the 10th day of the succeeding month. Likewise, the accountable officer and/or other officials concerned are reminded of the sanctions imposed under Sec. 2 of PD 1455 in relation to Art. 218 of the Revised Penal Code on the automatic suspension of payment of salaries for failure to render accounts in a period of two months.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: There were delays in the submission of some financial accounts and reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations, and submit immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI form to the audit team; copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee on Appropriation and the Senate Committee on Finance, as mandated by Section 93 of RA 10964.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: A similar observation is incorporated in Finding No. 15 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor furnish the audit team with the advice on the review by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan and the Local Finance Committee (LFC) of the Province of Northern Samar on the appropriation ordinances. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of Sec. 327 of RA 7160 or the Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: There were delays in submission and some accounts were not reconciled; some entries in the General Journal lacked supporting documents; schedules or lists of General Ledgers were submitted for some accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management rectify the deficiencies noted; refrain from having transactions with pecuniary interest where exist; require the contractors/suppliers to issue official receipt in gross amount; and implement its projects within the specified contract time. Henceforth, refrain from payment and undergoing procurement process without complete documentations, and strictly comply the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR. We also recommend that management submit justification for the early period of action on procurement activities; for the early completion of the Construction of San Lazaro Seawall (Phase 4); for the hiring of a legal consultant without the proper procurement process; and why COA was not informed of the deliveries within 24 hours from acceptance. Also require the official responsible for accepting deliveries of procured items furnish COA with delivery documents within twenty-four (24) hours after such acceptance.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Noting lacking supporting documents; rectify deficiencies; procurements without proper procedures.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management furnish the audit team with the advice on the review by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan and the Local Finance Committee (LFC) of the Province of Northern Samar on the appropriation ordinances. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of Sec. 327 of RA 7160 or the Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The audit team was not furnished with the advice on the review by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan and the LFC of the Province.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002, PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, and require all officials and employees concerned to immediately settle/liquidate their outstanding cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management refrain from disbursing government funds without complete documentations and strictly observe the requirements under PD 1445, RA 9184 and COA Circulars.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU refrain from hiring private legal consultant without first securing the required documents stated in COA Circular No. 86-255 as amended by COA Circular No. 95-011 and Section 10 of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Documents are incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prepare Monthly Report of Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption and to prepare the Driver’s Trip Tickets using the form prescribed in COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension and/or Disallowance as a result of the post-audit of transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management adhere strictly with the provisions of E.O. No. 80 specifically on the payment of Productivity Enhancement Incentive. We also recommend that the recipient officials and employees be required to refund the amount in excess of the authorized amount as prescribed in E.O. No. 80 and DBM BC No. 2014-3.

observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officers and employees to strictly comply with the provisions of E.O. 298, and refund the amount received in excess of the authorized travel rates as well as the unnecessary travel expenses. Stop the practice of issuing travel authority for attendance to conventions of organizations/leagues to which the personnel is not affiliated in order to preclude unnecessary travel and training expenses. Likewise, strictly observe the proper classification of accounts as provided for in the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Acting
   Municipal Treasurer agreed to  trace  the  variance  and  reconcile  the
   balances of the Cash in Bank under the GF,  SEF  and  TF  in  the  books
   maintained  by  the  Accounting  and  Treasury  Offices.  Moreover,  the
   Municipal officials and employees concerned agreed to take the necessary
   action to address  the  issue  of  the  long-standing  reconciling  item
   affecting the cash account of the Municipality under the GF.

We recommended that the:


      a. Municipal Treasurer and Assessor request for  sufficient  fund  for
         the (i) acquisition of motor vehicles for  field  personnel  during
         the discharge of their functions such as distribution of notice  of
         delinquencies, collections, regular assessment,  reassessment,  and
         tax mapping of real properties and (ii) reconstruction  of  updated
         tax maps; and


      b. Municipal Treasurer request for additional  plantilla  position  to
         facilitate effective property tax collection  and  assessment,  and
         devise  tax  campaign  strategies  which  may  include  conduct  of
         barangay symposiums and posting of information or advertisements to
         bulletin boards  at  the  vicinity  of  the  Municipality  and  the
         respective barangay halls to raise public awareness of the pros and
         cons of  early  and  late  payment  of  taxes  and  the  effect  of
         delinquencies in the ownership of real property.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:


     a. Municipal Accountant and Treasurer cause the refund  of  the  excess
        claim of DTE totaling ₱45,910.00. In case of non-compliance  by  the
        Accountable Officers (AOs), cause the withholding  of  their  salary
        until full settlement of their accountability;


     b. Concerned officials and employees (i) make the necessary refund  for
        the claimed  inland  transportation  amounting  to  ₱3,581.00;  (ii)
        refrain from  using  RER  in  claiming  transportation  expense  and
        instead support their claims with ORs or Certificate of Expenses not
        Requiring Receipts as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2017-001; (iii)
        submit the lacking documents  needed  to  support  their  claim  for
        travelling expenses and ensure that dates  of  liquidation  will  be
        indicated in the liquidation reports to  evaluate  properly  whether
        the CAs for travel were liquidated within the prescribed period; and
        (iv) refund the  erroneous  claims  totaling  ₱2,987.75  and  submit
        justification relative the noted observations on  the  said  claims;
        and


     c.  Municipal  Mayor  and  officials  observe  strictly  the  austerity
        measures set by the DILG and appropriate funds  programs,  projects,
        or activities that  will  bring  immediate  socio-economic  benefits
        among its local constituents and submit  explanation  regarding  the
        noted observations.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:


      a. Inventory Committee prepare a concrete plan for the  conduct  of  a
         complete physical inventory which shall include the time  frame  to
         complete inventory count, and prepare the inventory  report  taking
         into consideration  the  present  condition  and  location  of  the
         properties;


      b. OIC-GSO update the property cards and accomplish the form as to the
         required details specified in the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs,  and
         prepare and submit Report on Local Road Network to account for  the
         local roads;

      c. Municipal Accountant prepare the PPE ledger cards  as  required  by
         the manual to serve as tool and reference in the  inventory  taking
         and  provide  for  the  monthly  depreciation   on   all   of   the
         Municipality’s depreciable properties;

      d. Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO coordinate and prepare  supporting
         schedules to facilitate the proper reconciliation of  the  property
         accounts and  establishment  of  actual  existence,  ownership  and
         condition of the properties; and


      e. Municipal  Engineer  prepare  a  schedule  of  local  road  network
         indicating therein the cost of each road network component such  as
         (i) road lot,          (ii) road pavement, (iii) drainage and slope
         protection  structures,   and          (iv)   other   miscellaneous
         structures to be submitted to the OIC-GSO  and  Accounting  Office.
         Moreover, subsequent  road  projects  of  the  Municipality  should
         clearly indicate the separate cost of each road component for  easy
         reference. The Engineering Office is also vested to  determine  the
         life of the local roads taking into consideration the design,  type
         of users, and volume of users of the roads. Such detail should also
         be included in the report as basis for the depreciation of the road
         components to be applied by the Accounting Office.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:


      a.  Municipal Accountant draw Journal Entry Voucher  (JEV)  to  adjust
         the affected accounts for the fair presentation  in  the  financial
         statements and strictly adhere to the provision of  the  Manual  on
         the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I on the recognition of liability; and


      b.  Municipal Engineer ensure the accuracy  and  completeness  of  the
         information provided in the Monthly Monitoring  Report  of  Awarded
         PPAs to be submitted to the Audit  Team  as  prescribed  under  COA
         Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:


     a. Municipal Accountant, BAC and  TWG  submit  the  lacking  necessary
        supporting  documents  required  in  Item  5.7.1  of  the   Revised
        Documentary  Requirements  for   Common   Government   Transactions
        prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012;


     b. Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal  Treasurer  refrain  from
        paying claims with incomplete  documentation  pursuant  to  Section
        4(6) of PD No. 1445; and


     c. Municipal Accountant  comply  with  the  guidelines  set  forth  in
        Section 65 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:


     a. Municipal  Accountant  and  payees  concerned  submit  the  lacking
        supporting documents required under Item 5.13 of COA  Circular  No.
        2012-001 dated June 14,  2012 for audit purposes;


     b. Municipal Accountant  submit  the  certification  that  the  former
        officials or retired employees have  complied  with  the  necessary
        requirements and cleared of the obligation/s or  accountability/ies
        from the Municipal Accounting Office, otherwise,  submit  pertinent
        documents indicating the necessary action/s that the payees  should
        satisfy in order to be cleared, and certified  correct  computation
        of terminal leave benefits for the payees; and refrain from  paying
        claims with incomplete documentation pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD
        No. 1445; and


     c. Municipal Accountant and HRMO-Designate henceforth comply with  the
        documentary requirements for the payment of terminal leave benefits
        prescribed under the circular.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:


      a.  Municipal  Accountant  and  BAC  Secretariat  submit  the  lacking
         documents for audit purposes and  ensure  that  claims  for  future
         transactions be  supported  with  the  necessary  documents  before
         payment;


      b. Officials  and  employees  concerned  provide  explanation  on  the
         failure to observe the  prescribed  dates  in  the  stages  of  the
         contract and  format  of  BSD  and  OSS,  and  ensure  that  future
         transactions must comply with the provisions of the Revised IRR  of
         RA No. 9184;

      c. Municipal Accountant assess  and  demand  the  supplier  liquidated
         damages for its delay in the delivery  of  one  unit  Hammer  Mill-
         Single Stage and record in the books of accounts the Road  Networks
         completed pursuant  to  Item  7.1.1  of  DILG  MC  No.  2018-01  in
         accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008;

      d. Municipal  Agriculturist  secure  and  maintain  the  Feed  Milling
         Equipment to prevent deterioration before the full operation of the
         Small Scale Feed Mill; and

      e. Municipal Engineer ensure the fabrication and installation  of  the
         permanent marker required by Item  No.  5.4.5  of  the  Operational
         Guidelines on the Implementation of the  2018 Performance  Challenge
         Fund.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to  observe  the
   proper recording of fund transfers from the  NGAs  to  the  TF  books  of
   accounts instead of GF in compliance with Sections 309 and 310 of RA  No.
   7160, and review the Due to NGAs account and remit any unutilized  amount
   to the BTr or SA upon completion of the project or activity.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:


      a. Municipal Mayor together with the Sangguniang Bayan  and  Municipal
         officials concerned make the appropriate and  immediate  action  to
         address or resolve the identified issues and concerns  and/or  fast
         track the implementation of programmed developmental projects,  and
         ensure that projects proposed to be undertaken  during  the  budget
         year are implemented or completed in the same year; and


      b. Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to  comply  with  the
         provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 so that the public will  be
         properly informed of the PPAs undertaken by the agency  to  promote
         good  governance  through  transparency  and  accountability,   and
         provide notice to the Audit Team within the prescribed period  that
         the signboards are already posted.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:


        a. Municipal Mayor, as the Head of  the  Procuring  Entity  (HOPE),
           ensure that all procurement undertaken by the Municipality  were
           based on the approved APP; and


        b. Municipal Engineer, other end-user or implementing units of the
           Municipality and the BAC Chairman strictly adhere to  Section  7
           of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in the preparation of PPMP and
           APP.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:


        a. BAC strictly adhere to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of
           RA No. 9184  for  the  procurement  of  infrastructure  projects
           specifically on the conduct of post-qualification,  and  posting
           of the NOA, NTP and  contract  agreement  in  the  PhilGEPs  and
           warranty security;


        b.  Municipal  Accountant  submit  the  pertinent  CAF  for   audit
           purposes, and comply with Section 86 of PD No. 1445 and  Section
           1 of LOI 968 dated December 17, 1979; and


        c. Municipal Accountant and other employees  concerned  submit  the
           lacking necessary supporting documents required under  Item  9.0
           of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:


      a. MDRRMO strictly adhere with the  timeline  of  the  preparation  of
         LDRRMFIP to be integrated in the AIP  pursuant  to  NDRRMC-DBM-DILG
         JMC No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013;


      b. MDC observe timely submission of the AIP to the SB considering  the
         prescribed timeline of approval by the SB  under  DILG-NEDA-DBM-DoF
         JMC No. 1, series of 2016 dated  November  18,  2016,  and  the  SB
         comply with the required date of approval of the AIP as provided in
         the aforesaid JMC;

      c. MBO make corrective action to facilitate necessary  adjustments  to
         reflect the correct PPAs and amounts in the  budget  documents  for
         LDRRMF and notify the SP of such action;

      d. MDC, MBO and SB ensure that all PPAs included in the  Appropriation
         Ordinance are the same and consistent with those listed in the AIP;

      e. Municipal Accountant draw JEV to transfer the amount of ₱166,988.26
         from the TF to the GF to adjust the Trust  Liability-DRRM  account;
         and

      f. MDRRMO, MBO and Municipal Accountant ensure  compliance  with  Item
         5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal SWM Board
 through the MENRO-Designate:


    a.   Enforce the strict implementation of the mandatory  segregation  of
       wastes at source and upon collection, consider the  “No  segregation,
       No collection” policy to instill discipline to the residents  on  the
       proper  segregation  of  wastes,  and  conduct   trainings   on   the
       segregation of wastes;


    b.   Encourage the  other  barangays  to  establish  their  own  MRF  in
       coordination with the Punong Barangays;


    c.   Submit the lacking documents or information required in the  ER  to
       hasten the release of the ECC for the operation of the SLF; and


    d.   Submit the copy of signed MOU to the Audit Team for audit  purposes
       and conform to the conditions cited on the use and safekeeping of the
       mobile shredder machine.

We recommended that the MSB evaluate the PPAs in the SEF Budget  by
   ensuring the compliance with the guidelines relative the  utilization  of
   SEF to priority expenses under Sections 101 and 272 of RA 7160, DepEd-DBM-
   DILG JC No. 1 dated January 19, 2017 and LBC No. 23 dated  September  10,
   1985, and submit the lacking documents to establish the propriety of  the
   subject transactions.  Moreover,  the  Municipal  Accountant  and  Acting
   Municipal Treasurer refrain from making advance payments for any services
   or goods not yet rendered or delivered.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:


     a. Municipal School Board prepare and submit copy of the AIP  and  PPMP
        to the MBO to be subsequently forwarded  by  the  same  to  the  BAC
        Secretariat for consolidation and preparation of indicative APP;


     b. BAC and General Services Officer consider the procurement  of  goods
        or construction materials in  bulk  purchases  to  avail  of  volume
        discount and avoid wastage of  time  for  tedious  paper  works  and
        incurrence of additional  traveling  expenses,  and  adhere  to  the
        existing procurement laws and regulations; and


     c. Municipal officials  and  employees  concerned  submit  satisfactory
        explanation on the noted inconsistencies with the purpose  indicated
        in  the  SEF  Budget  for  the  above-cited  PPAs  and  the   actual
        utilization or implementation of the same.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the  Municipal  Mayor
   through the GAD Focal Person:


     a. Prioritize the attendance of the GFPS Executive Committee, TWG  and
        MET to various GAD-related trainings and seminars to accelerate the
        gender mainstreaming in the Municipality;


     b. Coordinate with the MPDC for the updated data of CBMS that will  be
        used in the maintenance of the GAD Database with  sex-disaggregated
        data and other relevant data that  will  aid  in  identifying  GAD-
        responsive PPAs which is  either  client-focused  or  organization-
        focused;


     c. Comply with the provisions of  PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA  JMC  No.  2013-
        01 dated July 18, 2013, as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No.  2016-
        01 dated January 12, 2016 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March
        18, 2014 relative the observance of timelines  in  the  preparation
        and submission of GPB and GAR. Moreover, submit explanation on  the
        noted inconsistencies in the amounts indicated in the DILG-approved
        GPB and GAR; and


     d. Require the Drafting Committee to prepare the IRR of  the  CY  2012
        GAD  Code  of  the  Municipality  to  effectively   implement   the
        provisions contained therein.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:

     a. Local Administration  devise  plans  and  strategies  to  facilitate
        effective management of market operations which may include:


        i. appointment of a full  pledged  Market  Supervisor  whose  tasks
           solely focus on market operations;
       ii. creation of a Market Development Plan for the  organization  and
           classification of market sections;
      iii. imposition of additional stall fees to lessees who  continuously
           occupy excess spaces;
       iv. issuance of demand letters  to  stallholders  to  encourage  the
           timely payment of rental dues and avoid penalties;
        v. compel stallholders to renew their business permits immediately;
           and
       vi. issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Eviction to lessees  who
           habitually and/or continuously violate the regulations set forth
           by the Market Code.

     b.  Designated  Market  Supervisor  and  Acting   Municipal   Treasurer
        coordinate in the enforcement  of  collection  of  rental  fees  and
        corresponding  surcharges  from  the  delinquent  stallholders,  and
        establish the actual number of stalls occupied by updating  the  map
        or plan of the market  and  prepare  report  of  the  amount  to  be
        collected  per  month  and  submit  the  report  to  the   Municipal
        Accountant for proper recording of receivable; and

     c. Municipal Accountant adopt the accrual basis  instead  of  the  cash
        method of  recording  the  monthly  market  rental  to  reflect  the
        balances  of  the  income  and  receivable  accounts   from   market
        operations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint permanent employees as Head and Staff of the MDRRMO to satisfy the requirements of Section 5.3 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-01 and effectively discharge the attached duties and responsibilities.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the rest of the members of the Joint Committee of the CNA submit valid justifications for items (a) and (b) and the required document in item (c) as required in the afore-cited circulars.
observations.managementAction:
The Management submitted justification for granting CNA Incentives but verification of the Team revealed it was not in accordance with the cost cutting measures.
The observations noted are still not submitted and complied with, to wit: (a) the cost-cutting and systems improvement measures to be undertaken by the Municipality were not clearly identified in the CNA of Victoria Municipal Government and Victoria Employees Association (VICMEA) signed in February 13, 2015; (b) The Status of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations and Balances (SAAOB) as of December 31, 2015 under the General Fund showed that 67.67% was obligated during the year which correspondingly indicated the rate of the Local Government Unit’s (LGU) performance of its targets during the year; and (c) Comparative Statement of Department of Budget and Management (DBM) approved level of operating expenses and actual operating expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Municipal Mayor: a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to institute action for the collection of the accounts or other appropriate remedies to clear the amount in the books; b. Analyze each fund transfer account balance and determined the reason why these were not liquidated and undertake immediate appropriate action thereon; and c. If warranted, request/apply for the write-off of the accounts following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017

observations.managementAction: AAR 2017
observations.reason: The LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to compel the General Services Officer and prepare/submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). Give priority for the creation of an Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of PPE. Direct Head of the General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office to hasten the updating of their records to facilitate the reconciliation of the accounting and property records. Oblige the Municipal Accountant to review the details of properties which are non-functional or unserviceable and prepare the necessary adjustments, if warranted. Require the committee to prepare a methodical and well-planned physical inventory taking, which could be done in a year-round basis, until all the Property, Plant and Equipment of the municipality have been accounted for and listed in the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). Also, all AREs must be renewed regularly pursuant to existing rules and regulations. Thereafter, the RPCPPE must be reconciled with the property record of each class of property at the General Services Office and subsidiary ledgers maintained at the Accounting Office. Any discrepancy should be investigated and appropriate action should be taken thereof. Make representation with the previous accounting personnel to substantiate and reconcile the forwarded balances prior to CY 2012. Work-out and to see to it that Titles of the purchased and donated parcels of land are transferred in the name of the Municipal Government of Victoria to establish absolute ownership thereof. Direct strict compliance with the accounting and reporting guidelines for local road network construction and maintenance system prescribed by COA Circular No. 2015-2008. Instruct the Accountants to draw a JEV to transfer the costs of completed projects from CIP-Infrastructure Assets and Building and Other Structures account to Infrastructure Assets and Building/School building or Other structures account.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 AAR 2012 AAR 2014

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations, and submit immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI form to the Audit Team, copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee on Appropriations and Senate Committee on Finance pursuant to Section 93 of RA 10651.
observations.reason: The LGU did not submit AAPSI.

Henceforth, strictly comply and follow the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR. Require contractors to furnish copies of delivery documents within 24 hours after acceptance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the required Memorandum of Agreement/Trust Agreement of the different projects as well as the Project Briefs and Local Poverty Reduction Action Plan (LPRAP) for the livelihood projects. Submit the lacking supporting documents and justifications as well as rectify other deficiencies noted. Further, ensure projects be implemented upon receipt of the funds for the benefit of the constituents/beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management implement the programs/projects/activities appropriated under the 20% development fund and realign the unimplemented programs/projects/activities which were not in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes. Submit the Memorandum of Agreement with the source agency for the fund transfers and justify the need for the daily maintenance of the projects totaling P1,638,800.00. Also prepare the necessary adjusting entry for erroneous recording of expenses totaling P869,520.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal School Board prepare their budget, give priority to the programs/projects/activities which are the immediate needs of the students and will enhance their learning skills as envisioned by RA 5447. Likewise, refrain from granting cash advances to persons not connected with the municipality, and rectify the deficiencies noted, submit the lacking supporting documents, transact with bona fide suppliers, and discontinue paying previous year transactions which were not obligated. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 100 (a) and (c) and 272 of RA 7160 or RA 5447.
observations.reason: Management did not comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management furnish the audit team with the advice on the review by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan and the Local Finance Committee (LFC) of the Province of Northern Samar on the appropriation ordinances. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of Sec. 327 of RA 7160 or the Local Government Code.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management refrain from disbursing government funds without complete documentations in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 and Section 10 of RA 9184 and COA Circular 2012-001.
observations.reason: Did not submit the lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prepare Monthly Report of Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption and Driver’s Trip Tickets using the form prescribed in COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977.
observations.reason: Some reports were submitted. Implemented only partial.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee be required to submit copies of approved contracts/purchase orders within five (5) days after their execution/issuance and copies of delivery documents within 24 hours after acceptance of delivered goods and services as provided in COA Circulars 2009-001 and 2009-002.
observations.reason: Did not comply with the recommendation; documents not submitted timely.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Bids and Awards Committee submit copies of approved contracts/purchase orders and delivery documents within prescribed timeframes.
observations.reason: The management failed to comply with the recommendation; documents not provided.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require a) all Collecting Officers to turn over their collections intact daily at the close of business day to the Municipal Treasurer; and b) the latter to deposit all the turned over collections to authorized depository bank daily to avoid the possible misuse of cash in their possession.
We also recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to a) closely monitor the issuance of Accountable Forms to the Collecting Officers and instruct them to i) issue ORs in strict numerical sequence and indicate details of check payments in the AFs; and i) stop the transfer/borrowing of AFs; b) avoid the delegation of collection function to Job Orders; and c) indicate the name of the collector remitting collections and the corresponding report no. and amount in the collection portion of her RCDs.
Furthermore, we recommended and Municipal Treasurer agreed to verify and count, not only the cash remitted but also the unissued accountable forms presented and ensure that all collections are remitted intact by all collectors concerned to prevent loss or misuse of government funds.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct: (a) the Treasury Office to update the RPTARs/ Taxpayers’ Index Card and subsequently, prepare and furnish the Accounting Office the list required under Section 20 Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to establish RPT and SET receivables sourcing from the list duly certified by the Treasury Office at the beginning of the year.

We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed to instruct the:
Municipal Accountant (i) to maintain complete PPELC for each class of property to record the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof; and (ii) to make necessary adjustments and recognize financial transactions properly to avoid misstatement of affected accounts;
Municipal Treasurer to prepare PC of all PPE including properties acquired from previous years; and
Inventory Committee to complete the physical verification of all properties of the Municipality and render required reports thereon.
We recommended and Management agreed to present only the portion of the loan that is due to be settled within twelve months as current liability as prescribed in paragraph 80 of PPSAS 1.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to a) direct the Market Administrator to strictly implement the provisions of the Market Code specifically on the execution of the contracts and enforcement of the provisions stipulated therein; and b) make representation to the Sangguniang Bayan for the amendment of the Market Code to include a penalty provision that would enjoin the stallholders to pay their obligations within the designated period.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to a) recognize the revenue in the year it is earned and accrue the current year’s uncollected lease rental at year-end; b) recognize utility expense in the year it is incurred; and c) make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the errors committed in the recognition of financial transactions and events for CY 2018 as follows:
Proposed Adjusting Entry:
For collections relating to CY 2017 rentals:
For the unrecognized receivables relating to CY 2018 rentals:
For the unrecognized payable for electrical usage from October to December 2018:

We reiterated our recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat and the Accounting Office to a) exercise due diligence in preparing documents and processing disbursements and to ensure that required supporting documents are properly accomplished and attached to the disbursement vouchers; and b) submit or settle immediately the deficiencies noted in Annex B.

We recommended and Management agreed that a) Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat Office and the Municipal Engineer to submit the lacking documents and to observe the instructions on contract submission prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-01; and b) Municipal Accountant check the completeness of supporting documents before effecting the payments of obligations in view of the contracts’ deliverables.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Human Resource and Management Office to ensure compliance on setting the minimum limit of monthly take home pay of Municipal employees in accordance with prevailing statute.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the OIC-MENRO to conduct the Information and Education Campaigns the soonest possible time to remind the residents of the existing environmental laws and policies highlighting fines and penalties when violations are committed. We also reiterated our recommendation and the LCE agreed to instruct the solid waste management board to continue to monitor the effective implementation of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations and the Municipality agreed to (a) accomplish properly the GAD Accomplishment Report by indicating the reasons for their variances/deviations between the set targets and actual results; and (b) ensure that all activities included in its GAD Plan and Budget are fully implemented to be able to attain their corresponding GAD objectives. Furthermore, we recommend that the Municipal GFPS adopt and conduct the HGDG Test to assess the gender-responsiveness of each programs and projects before attributing them to the GAD Budget.

We reiterate our prior year’s recommendations and the (a) Municipal LSB agreed to follow and perform the activities in the preparation and approval of the SEF Budget with indicative timelines as prescribed by DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 or the Revised Guidelines on the Use of Special Education Fund (SEF); and (b) Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the Report of SEF Utilization quarterly using the prescribed form for submission to appropriate offices.
We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the BAC and its Secretariat and the Municipal Accountant, to ensure that all required supporting documents are properly accomplished and attached to the disbursement vouchers.

We recommended and the Municipality agreed to (a) hasten the transfer of documents between departments to ensure the timely recording of transactions; and (b) gather all pertinent documents and analyze the subject accounts to establish the accuracy of their balances especially those accounts with negative balances. Furthermore, we reiterate our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Human Resource and Management Officer to (a) remit all the due taxes and premiums within the prescribed period; and (b) reconcile the withheld amounts and actual remittances periodically to ensure that all deductions are remitted intact and on time.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal GSO – Designate to:
a. conduct a complete physical count of the property of the Municipality, and
prepare and submit a properly accomplished RPCPPE not later than
January 31 of the following year;
b. prepare and maintain Property Cards for each category of PPE to support
the balances appearing in the PPE Ledger Cards of the Accounting Office; and
c. conduct a regular reconciliation of Property Cards with the PPE Ledger
Cards maintained by the Accounting Office to ensure the accuracy of the
PPE balances reported in the Statement of Financial Position as of year-
end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal GSO -
Designate to:
a. maintain and update Stock Cards for each supplies/inventory to support
the balance reported in the Supplies Ledger Cards and Financial
Statements of the Accounting Office; and
b. conduct physical count of all its inventories to establish the actual and
existing inventory items and prepare and submit to the Audit Team every
six months, the duly certified and approved RPCI for each type of
inventory.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Accountant to analyze the accounts that are dormant and long
outstanding and consider the applicability of requesting for authority to drop
from the books in line with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 16,
2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Instruct the MGSO – Designate to expedite the preparation and submission
of the list of all insurable property and ensure that all insurable property
are covered by property insurance with the Property Insurance Fund of the
GSIS as required under RA No. 656 and COA Circular No. 92-390 dated
November 17, 1992; and
b. direct the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the annual budget the
amount necessary for the insurance premiums of all insurable property.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
and the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer to ensure that
