payments for garbage hauling and disposal services are completely
documented in compliance with Section 9.1.3.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Chairman
and all other Officials concerned to execute a contract detailing the terms of
reference in compliance with the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer
and other concerned Officials to coordinate with the Land Bank of the
Philippines, Santa Cruz Branch as an initial step to the shift of the Municipality
from manual to automated payroll system to address the risk of loss of funds
and ensure the timely delivery of salaries to all employees of the Municipality.

We recommended that the:


      a. Designated General Services Officer (GSO), Municipal Accountant and
         Municipal   Engineer   continue   to   work   hand   in   hand   in
         tracing/investigating the  discrepancies  between  the  RPCPPE  and
         accounting records and adjust them;


      b. GSO prepare Report on the Physical  Count  of  Local  Road  Network
         (RPCLRN) and maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and Local
         Road Network Property  Ledger  Card  in  accordance  with  the  COA
         Circular No. 2015-008; and


      c. Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal  Accountant  and  the  GSO
         with  the  complete  description  and  cost  segregation  of   road
         components for road projects.

We recommended that the:

      a. Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairman submit the necessary  documents
         for audit purpose; and


      b.  BAC  (i)  submit  the  satisfactory  explanation  on   the   noted
         observations; and (ii) strictly adheres to the  provisions  of  the
         2016 RIRR  of  RA              No.  9184  for  the  above-mentioned
         infrastructure projects and procurement of goods.

We recommended that the (a)  Special  Disbursing  Officer  (SDO)  also
    maintain her own cashbook; and (b) both SDO and DO accomplish the  cashbooks
    in accordance  with  Section  28,  Volume  II  of  the  Manual  on  the  New
    Government Accounting System (MNGAS) to establish proper accountability.

We recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials and staff
    through the Municipal Accountant submit the supporting documents  required
    by Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 for audit purposes.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  BAC   and
    designated General Services Officer (GSO)  to  comply  with  the  period  of
    submission of  executed  contracts  and  their  supporting  documents  under
    Sections 3.1.1 and 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct Municipal Treasurer
to intensify the collection to recover government funds invested in the
program and instruct the Municipal Accountant to institute action for the
collection of the accounts or other appropriate remedies to clear the amount
in the books.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct Municipal Accountant to
determine the reason on why each fund transfer account balance were not
liquidated and undertake immediate appropriate action and if warranted,
request/apply for the write-off of the accounts following the guidelines under
COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor:
a) Create and convene the Inventory and Disposal Committee that shall
conduct the validation of the PPEs physical existence and status, and
submit the necessary report in accordance with the prescribed
procedures and timeline; and
b) Require the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian to
maintain a compete and updated PPELC and PCs respectively for each
class of property and regularly reconcile their records and coordinate
with each other for the provision of depreciation and determination of
impairment losses of these assets.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct accountable officers
to remit and deposit collections daily to the authorized depository bank to
properly safeguard the government funds; prepare Daily Statement of
Collections and Accountable Forms (DSCAF) as required by pertinent
provisions; and transfer the funds to LBP-Allen Branch in order to minimize
the delay of deposit.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the:
a) Treasury Office to update the RPTARs/ Taxpayers’ Index Card and
subsequently, prepare and furnish the Accounting Office the list
required under Section 20 Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for
LGUs; and
b) Municipal Accountant to establish RPT and SET receivables sourcing
from the list duly certified by the Treasury Office.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to direct the:
a) Property Custodian to update, prepare and submit all documents
and reports such as duly approved RIS to evidence the issuance of the
materials from stock and Report of Supplies and Materials Issued etc.
as basis for crediting the non-existing balance from the books;
b) Municipal Accountant to refrain from recording consumed inventory
from the books and reconcile accounting and property and supply
records; and
c) Adjust the non-existing and consumed unaccounted materials
inventory in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) Comply with the provisions of Sections 43 of the Manual of the
NGAS, Volume I on the proper recording of purchases and
issuances of supplies and materials;
b) Restore to inventory accounts the undocumented adjustments; and
c) Maintain the Subsidiary Ledger Card (SLC) for inventories.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor require Municipal Accountant
to record all purchases of inventory items under the perpetual inventory
method in its related inventory account and also the Municipal Social
Welfare Officer and Treasurer to furnish the copy of delivery
receipt/acceptance and inspection report of all received inventory items.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive require Municipal Accountant
to reconcile the Intra-Agency Accounts, “Due from Other Funds and Due to
Other Funds” to promptly identify any discrepancy in the balances of the two
accounts and effect the necessary adjustments, if appropriate, to bring the
affected accounts into an agreement.

We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) develop a more
comprehensive budget priorities based on logical framework of program to
ensure that all components and activities were performed in line with the
programmed design and that it is achieving its objectives

We recommended that Municipal Mayor as the Chairman of the Local
School Board to:
a) Give priority for the repair of classrooms of the Acedillo Elementary
School to provide students of the facilities that are conducive to
learning; and
b) Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the expenditures based on the
appropriated amount as mandated on the approved LSB Resolution.

We recommended for the strict observance of the limitations on the use of
the Special Education Fund as provided for in Section 4 of
DEPED/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 01, series of 2017 in the utilization
of SEF.

We recommended that LDRRMO under the direction of the Local Chief
Executive to prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that the three (3) DRRM position be filled immediately
to begin the institutionalization of the organization of LDDRMO for better
operations and implementation of LDRRM.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned
department heads to optimally utilize the LDRRMF in order to uphold the
rights of the people to life and properties through preparing for disasters,
mitigating its effect, responding and recovering after disasters.

We recommended that LCE enforce proper segregation of solid waste at
source and information and dissemination campaign on proper solid waste
management be conducted so that the constituents will be fully aware of the
benefits derived from the program.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and
Development Officer to prudently monitor and evaluate the implementation
of the development projects to ensure that the 20% Development Fund is
optimally utilized to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development
and environmental outcomes of the Municipal Government.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the department heads
of the implementing offices of funds received from various national
agencies for the timely implementation and completion of projects and
supervise and monitor the completion of all remaining projects without
further delay for the enjoyment of benefits of the targeted beneficiaries
therefrom.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to coordinate each other to effect the
immediate return of the unexpended/excess balances of the same to the
concerned source agencies after the implementation of each project.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Point
Person to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget as well as the GAD
Accomplishment Report and submit to the PPDO for review before
submission to DILG for review and endorsement to ensure that the GAD
activities are aligned to the priorities of the Province and in accordance with
the established GAD framework.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor instruct: (a) the Treasury Office to update the RPTARs/ Taxpayers’ Index Card and subsequently, prepare and furnish the Accounting Office the list required under Section 20 Volume I of the Manual on the  for LGUs; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to establish  and SET receivables sourcing from the list duly certified by the Treasury Office.

We reiterated our recommendations that Management instruct: (a) the Municipal Accountant (i) to maintain complete PPELC for each class of property to record the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof; and (ii) to make necessary adjustments and recognize financial transactions properly to avoid misstatement of affected accounts; (b) the Municipal Treasurer to prepare PC of all PPE including properties acquired from previous years; and (c) the Inventory Committee to complete the physical verification of all properties of the Municipality and render required reports thereon.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC and its Secretariat, the Accounting Office and the Engineering Office to exercise due diligence in preparing documents and processing disbursements and to ensure that required supporting documents are properly accomplished and attached to the disbursement vouchers. Furthermore, we recommended that the deficiencies noted in Annex A be submitted or settled immediately.

We reiterated our recommendations that Management ensure that an employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the minimum allowed by the existing law (GAA provision) and avoid the issuance of certification allowing the acquisition of loans for employees and subsequently rendering their net pay to fall below the minimum. Further, we recommended that payroll deductions shall be limited following the order of preference provided in the GAA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) intensify further the conduct of Information and Education Campaigns to make the residents aware of the existing environmental laws and policies with their corresponding fines and penalties; (b)  instruct the designated enforcers to ensure that the corresponding fines and penalties will be served to violators to reduce the instances of prohibited acts such as littering, open burning, and collection of non-segregated or unsorted waste; and (c) instruct the solid waste management board to continue to monitor the effective implementation of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal GFPS: (a) accomplish properly the GAD Accomplishment Report by including the actual results of the implemented GAD Activities and the reasons for their variances/deviations with the set targets; (b) ensure that all the activities included in its GAD Plan are implemented to be able to attain their corresponding GAD Objectives; and (c) continue to comply with all GAD rules and regulations to promote gender mainstreaming.

We recommended that Management: (a) consider the timelines of project implementation and improve its monitoring functions or supervision for projects being implemented to avoid the incurrence of delays;  (b) direct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions of RA No.9184 and its IRR in the procurement activities; and provide justification on the deficiencies noted and immediately submit the lacking documents enumerated in the Table 2 above and (c) ensure that the Construction Safety and Health Program is embedded in the project’s cost and ultimately in the contract or agreement between the LGU and the contractor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Budget Officer, and the Municipal Accountant comply with the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 and Advisory No. 2016-02 dated November 25, 2016 of the Interim National Peace and Order Council Secretariat of DILG relevant to appropriation, entitlement, release, use and reporting of the confidential funds.

We recommended that management: (a) stop the practice of paying fixed allowance for communication without the submission of proof on the incurrence of expense showing or justifying the actual usage or consumption by the concerned officials and employees and (b) consider the availment of contracts subscriptions with telephone companies offering discounts or services promotions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) require the LSB to follow and perform the activities in the preparation and approval of the SEF Budget with indicative timelines as prescribed by DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 or the Revised Guidelines on the Use of Special Education Fund (SEF); and (b) require the Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare the Report of SEF Utilization quarterly using the prescribed form for submission to appropriate offices; and (ii) make necessary adjustments and recognize financial transactions properly to avoid misstatement of affected accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Human Resource and Management Officer to (a) remit all the due taxes and premiums within the prescribed period; and (b) reconcile the withheld amounts and actual remittance periodically to ensure that all deductions are remitted intact and on time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer – Designate to reconciliation of records and make the necessary adjustments, if there are any; b. Instruct the Municipal General Services Officer – Designate to maintain Property Cards with complete and necessary information to facilitate verification and reconciliation with the Accounting Office PPE Ledger Cards; c. Direct the Municipal General Services Officer – Designate to conduct annual physical inventory of all PPEs to determine any missing/unaccounted property and take/recommend the necessary actions against the accountable; and d. Dispose all unserviceable properties in accordance with the existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: Continuous reconciliation of the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer – Designate to evaluate the discrepancies and make the necessary adjustments, if there are any; The MGSO – Designate to maintain Property Cards. The MGSO – Designate submitted the RPCPPE for CY 2018 on May 30, 2019. The MGSO – Designate to prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) properly signed by authorized officials.
observations.reason: Discrepancies between the records of the Accounting’s Office and MGSO – Designate were not verified due to their late submission of RPCPPE by the MGSO – Designate.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor discontinue the granting of cash advances to the accountable officer in excess of his maximum cash accountability, otherwise instruct the accountable officer to increase his fidelity cash bond sufficient to cover his accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer increased the fidelity cash bond of the Disbursing Officer.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to: a. Set-up RPT Receivable and SET Receivable at the beginning of the year based on duly certified list of taxpayers, and the amount due and collectible for the year provided by the Municipal Treasurer; and b. Ensure the equality of amounts set-up of RPT and SET Receivables.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor furnished the Municipal Accountant a copy of duly certified list of taxpayers. The Municipal Accountant set-up RPT and SET Receivable at the beginning of the year based on the duly certified list of taxpayers. With the implementation of e-PPSAS, discrepancies noted between RPT/SET Receivable and Deferred RPT/SET Income were resolved.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. Make a reconciliation and monitoring of all booked receivables to ensure that only valid receivables are recorded in the books; a.2 Analyze the accounts that are dormant and long outstanding and take the necessary actions to drop them from the books, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 16, 2016; and b. Instruct the OIC – Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) to determine the whereabouts of the loan borrowers for possible collection of the loans granted to them.
observations.managementAction: The OIC – MSWD Officer furnished the Audit Team with a list of dormant receivable. The Accounting Office is in the process of maintaining and updating their records. The MSWD Officer was able to gather copies of death certificates of the deceased loan borrowers.
observations.reason: Updating of records is on-going; some loan claims still need validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Ensure that the implementation of the PPAs included in the 20 per cent Development Fund is in accordance with the timeline indicated in their Annual Investment Plan (AIP) so that maximum benefits will be realized for its constituents; and b. Strictly implement the provisions of Section 5.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: Some PPAs funded under 20 per cent were implemented within the timeline indicated in their AIP. The Municipal Management was able to increase the programs implemented in CY 2018.
observations.reason: Despite the increase in programs implemented, there were still PPAs not implemented within the timeline indicated in the AIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Require the Municipal General Services Officer to start the preparation and maintenance of the LRNPC as required under COA Circular No. 2015-008; and b. The Municipal Accountant to disclose information on the total road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements following the format prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: The MGSO – Designate was not able to provide the details for Road Network System due to the inability of the Municipal Engineer to provide the necessary information. The Municipal Accountant disclosed most of the required information for road system in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.reason: Not complete details for Road Network System were available due to the inability of the Municipal Engineer to provide the description and cost components for road projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development (MSWD) Officials to prepare specific program/project/activity in the AIP; and b. Instruct the MSWD Officials, in coordination with the LCPC members, to monitor the implementation of the PPAs intended to improve the lives of the youth and help reduce, if not eliminate, juvenile delinquency in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The MSWD Officials prepared specific program/project/activity in the AIP. The MSWD Office monitored the implementation of the PPAs funded for LCPC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Require the Local GAD Focal Point and GAD Focal Point Team (LGFPT) to ensure that the Programs, Projects and Activities included in the GAD Plan are gender responsive; and b. Instruct the GAD Focal Point Person and the Budget Officer to reconcile their records to come up with the correct amount of expenditures for Gender and Development related activities.
observations.managementAction: Most of the PPAs included in the GAD Plan already focused on gender issues. Variances noted between the records of the GAD Focal Point Person and Budget Officer were already settled.
observations.reason: There were still PPAs which do not focused on gender issues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Designated General Services Officer (GSO) and Municipal Accountant continue tracing/investigating the discrepancies between the Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and accounting records and adjust them; b. Inventory Committee accomplish the RPCPPE in accordance with the format prescribed in the Manual on New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGU); c. Municipal Accountant prepare a subsidiary record for each component of the road network and Local Road Network Ledger Card; d. Designated GSO maintain Local Road Inventory Road Map and Local Road Property Ledger Card. Moreover, conduct physical count of the road network and prepare Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN) and submit the same to this office not later than January 31 of each year; and e. Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the designated GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction:
The reconciliatio n between the accounting records of PPE and RPCPPE as of December 31, 2018 showed that the discrepancies decreased to ₱15,572,707.4 8 from the prior year’s discrepancies of ₱94,497,416.5 3.
The Municipal Accounting Office prepared:
(i) Subsidiary Records for Road and Road components for every Road Network;
(ii) Local Road Network Ledger Card (LRNLC); and
(iii) Lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component.
The Municipality informed that (i) the adjustments on the PPE accounts will be reflected once the reconciliation of the same has been completed; and (ii) they will create Plantilla Position for the GSO since the designated GSO is the Assistant Municipal Treasurer who is also the Officer-in-Charge (OIC) - Municipal Treasurer. She will be retiring on October 2019, and hire additional technical person to do the analysis of road networks due to voluminous transactions of the Engineering Department.
observations.reason: Continuous efforts were done by the Management to address the discrepancies noted. The Municipal Engineer is gradually providing the Municipal Accountant the details on road descriptions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to (a) adjust the stale checks appearing in their Bank Reconciliatio n Statement to fairly present the cash balance of the Municipality, (b) continue disclosing in the Notes to Financial Statements about the deposits made by the Mining Company to their account amounting to ₱171,825.00 and (c) submit to the COA Audit Team the copy of the passbook for the Cash in Bank balance of ₱183,832.90 appearing as reconciling item.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant prepared (i) Journal Entry Voucher No. 101-208-06-GJ-30 dated May 30, 2018 to adjust the stale checks under general fund amounting to ₱50,668.52. The stale check under trust fund amounting to ₱7,425.00 was issued to Aurelio Escala Plant Nursery; and (ii) disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements about the deposits made by the Mining Company to their account amounting to ₱171,825.00.
The Municipality submitted the copy of passbook for the Cash in Bank balance of ₱183,832.90.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the lacking documents relative the procured lot.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted the Certificate of Title in the name of Local Government of Victoria.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the: a. Municipal Mayor designate Special Disbursing Officer (SDO) to handle the disbursing functions for the Supplemental Feeding Program (SFP); and b. Officer-in-Charge (OIC) - Municipal Treasurer (i) monitor the fidelity bond of every AO for public funds and request for an increase of bond to cover the maximum cash accountability provided under BTr Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009; and (ii) require the DO and SDO to maintain cashbook and properly accomplish it pursuant to Section 28 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGU).
observations.managementAction:
The OIC-Municipal Treasurer was assigned as SDO of SFP of the Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) because her fidelity bond was sufficient to cover the cash accountability.
The cash books maintained by the DO and SDO were not in accordance with Section 28 of MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.reason: For calendar year (CY) 2018, the Municipality still failed to apply for appropriate sufficient fidelity bond for the various accountabilities of the OIC-Municipal Treasurer. However, the Municipality appropriated additional fund for the fidelity bond of OIC-Municipal Treasurer to cover her various CAs accountability. The amount of bond was ₱3,500,000.00 under risk no. 27-190082R effective from January 8, 2019 to January 8, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) (i) submit satisfactory explanation on the observation noted and (ii) strictly adhere to the provisions of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 for the Concreting of Road at Alialy Street, Poblacion, Victoria, Oriental Mindoro; b. Municipal Accountant, Engineer and BAC submit the necessary documents for audit purposes; and c. Municipal Engineer monitor the actions taken by King’s Builders and Development Corporation since the project was still at warranty against structural defects for five years; or in the alternative the Municipal Accountant deduct from the amount due to King’s Builders and Development Corporation the cost deficiency of ₱10,899.12 or to demand payment for such amounts, as the case may be.
observations.managementAction:
The BAC, Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer adhered to the recommendation of COA and will strictly follow the provisions of 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184.
King’s Builder and Development Corporation refunded the amount of ₱10,899.12 under Accountable Form No. 51 with serial no. 9691038 dated July 6, 2018.
observations.reason: The satisfactory explanation of BAC on the observations noted and the required supporting documents are still not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) (i) submit satisfactory explanation on the observation noted and (ii) strictly adheres to the provisions of the RIRR of RA No. 9184; b. Municipal Accountant, Engineer and BAC submit the necessary documents for audit purposes; and c. Municipal Accountant deduct from the amount due to B5 Builders and Supplies the cost deficiency of ₱43,900.15 or to demand payment for such amounts, as the case may be. Moreover, the Technical Evaluation and Inspection Reports by the COA Technical Inspection Audit Specialist (TAS) dated March 8, 2018 was recommended to be strictly adhered by the Municipal Engineer.
observations.managementAction:
The BAC, Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer adhered to the recommendation of COA and will strictly follow the provisions of 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 for Construction of Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office and OPCEN (Phase I) located at the Municipal Compound, Poblacion I, Victoria, Oriental Mindoro.
B5 Builders and Supplies refunded the amount of ₱43,900.15 under Accountable Form No. 51 with serial no. 9691037 dated July 6, 2018.
observations.reason: The satisfactory explanation of BAC on the observations noted and the required supporting documents are still not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Engineer prepare the monthly monitoring Report of the Awarded Infra PPAs and List of Ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted the Monthly Monitoring Report of the Awarded Infra (PPAs) for the period January to June and November to December 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Bids and Awards (BAC) Chairman furnish the Office of the Auditor with the copy of executed contracts, PO and its pertinent supporting documents within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality will hire additional support personnel to BAC Secretariat with appropriate training in procurement process because there were lacking of full time personnel to do the compilation of documents.
observations.reason: The Municipality still failed to submit the executed contracts and POs within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor, Engineer, Budget Officer and Planning and Development Coordinator ensure that the availability of lot and area for the construction of rest room and other problems are properly resolved for the immediate implementatio n of the 20% DF to attain the objective of optimal utilization of the fund pursuant to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 as amended by DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality (i) reprogrammed the projects to other developmental projects in Calendar Years 2018 to 2019 under Supplemental Budget No. 1, Appropriation Ordinance (AO) Nos. 20 and 21 series of 2018 and AO No. 01 series of 2019. The said action was approved by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan; and (ii) committed to adhere on the recommendations of COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Disaster Risk and Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) (i) prepare and submit the LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) in accordance with the format provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to reflect the allocation for QRF in lump sum and disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded, as well as the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; (ii) ensure that all the proposed programs/projects for disaster be implemented during the year to ascertain readiness of the Municipality in case of calamity; and (iii) and in case there is change in the previously proposed projects, see to it that the disaster council is guided by Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG), Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council Joint Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013 wherein the unexpended balance of disaster fund may be released and utilized for new expenditure items upon submission of a revised Annual Investment Plan (AIP) as approved by the local Sanggunian and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) upon recommendation of the council and in accordance with the approved disaster plan; b. Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to prepare and submit monthly the Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month in consonance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; c. Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and the MDRRMO reconcile the balances that appear in the SAAOB and the Report on Utilization of DRRMF; d. Municipal Budget Officer maintain separate RAAO for MOOE and CO of the 70% allocation of LDRRMF and monitor the appropriations of LDRRMF on per Program, Projects and Activities (PPA) basis to facilitate and ensure an accurate basis on certifying availability of appropriations for obligations; e. BAC (i) explain the deficiencies noted above; and (ii) strictly adhere with the pertinent provisions of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184 in the conduct of procurement transactions by procuring goods based on the relevant characteristics and/or performance requirement instead of basing on brand names and procure only those that are necessary, of equally higher quality but with cheaper price; f. Municipal Accountant and BAC submit the supporting documents to substantiate the transactions; and g. Municipal Accountant (i) draw Journal Entry Voucher transfer fund from the Trust Fund to the General Fund to adjust the affected account for the fair presentation in the financial statements; and (ii) maintain subsidiary ledgers for transfer of unutilized calamity fund to trust fund by year of transfer.
observations.managementAction:
The MDRRMO prepared and submitted the following reports: (i) LDRMFIP which reflect the recommendations of COA; and (ii) Monthly the Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF. Monthly reconciliation was regularly conducted by MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant.
The Municipality (i) committed to strictly adhere on the recommendations of COA; and (ii) submitted the documents for the procurement of the disaster supplies. The Municipal Accountant maintained Subsidiary Ledger for the annual monitoring of transfer of LDRRMF to Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Mayor and the concerned official explain the noted observations with probable solutions to address the present conditions in the Municipality, see to it that the criteria cited in Republic Act (RA) No. 9003 for establishment of controlled dumpsites and/or sanitary landfill are complied to mitigate the harmful effects in the environment of solid wastes generated by the locality and further damage to the health of its constituents and ensure that the constituents in the barangays burying and dumping their waste in the backyard are informed of proper waste disposal.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality informed that the (i) approval of 10-year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan (MSWMP) on October 29, 2018 will serve as guide to the Municipality on its solid waste disposal including its 32 barangays; (ii) Department of Environmental and Natural Resources (DENR) MIMAROPA Region acknowledged the receipt of submission of the Municipality’s Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan for the dumpsite located at Barangay San Gelacio, Victoria, Oriental Mindoro and commended for their actions in compliance with the requirements of RA No. 9003. Ocular inspection in Barangay San Gelacio, Victoria, Oriental Mindoro revealed that the practice of burning and burying of waste and using it as a filler to level up the ground for the construction of road were discontinued.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still in the process of obtaining Environmental Compliance Certificate (ECC) from DENR for the construction of Sanitary Landfill (SLF).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) in assistance with the other department head prioritize the revision of the MSWMP in accordance with Section 17 of Republic Act (RA) No. 9003 for submission and approval by the NSWMC.
observations.managementAction: The 10-year MSWMP was approved on October 29, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Local School Board (i) consolidate all requests for procurement; and (ii) prepare and submit the PPMP for supplies and materials and infrastructure projects, consolidated APP, copy furnished the Audit Team; and b. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) (a) strictly adhere with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 in the conduct of procurement transactions by procuring goods based on the relevant characteristics and/or performance requirement instead of basing on brand names and procure only those that are necessary, of equally higher quality but with cheaper price; and (b) explain the observations noted regarding the BAC Resolutions.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal School Board submitted PPMP and APP for CY 2018. The Municipality (i) informed the Secretariat of Local Health Board on the recommendations of COA; and (ii) committed that starting in CY 2019 no transactions will be processed without showing any proof submitting the documents to the Commission.
The BAC Chairman explained that the BAC Resolution issued is good for shopping and small value procurement with provisions on the applicable mode of procurement. In CY 2018, separate BAC Resolutions were prepared for each alternative mode of procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Treasurer and Accountant strictly adhere with Section 4 of Residential Decree (PD) No. 1445, Section 344 of RA No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 2006-002 to ensure the validity and propriety of the transactions; b. Municipal Accountant see to it that the disbursement vouchers are supported with the necessary and properly accomplished; and c. Municipal School Board limit the utilization of the SEF to programs and projects prioritized in Section 272 of RA No. 7160 in order to maximize the benefits that the public school students could derive therefrom.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality committed to strictly adhere with the COA auditors’ recommendations to ensure the validity and propriety of the transactions and to limit the utilization of the SEF to program and projects prioritized in Section 272 of RA No. 7160 in order to maximize the benefits that the public school students could derive therefrom.
The Municipality submitted the copy of Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (ARE) for the amount of ₱88,300.00.
In CY 2018, disbursement vouchers were properly accomplished and required supporting documents such as ARE and AIR were attached. Based on the submitted Utilization Report for CY 2018 disclosed that the utilization of the SEF to programs and projects were in accordance with the Department of Education (DepED), Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) Joint Circular No. 1-2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal officials concerned ensure that the 20% DF were appropriated and disbursed only for the priority projects, programs and activities mentioned in DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality properly planned its 20% DF in CY 2018 and committed to adhere on COA auditors’ recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees: a. Explain why there was excessive claim of traveling expenses, otherwise, refund the amount of ₱192,159.92 for overpayment; b. Submit the necessary documents for the payment of travelling expenses; and c. Henceforth, ensure the compliance with the provisions set forth in Section 4 of EO No. 298 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality submitted the following: (i) Certifications issued by the Local Chief Executive (LCE) that the claims of the municipal officials and employees for hotel accommodation in excess of the allowable hotel room/lodging rate of ₱400.00 is absolutely necessary in accordance with EO No. 298 for the following Municipal officials and employees: Hon. Tanie Malitao, Jr., Hon. Michael Malaluan, Hon. Rowell Ganad, Hon. Imelda Callo, Engr. Mark Mahlon Parial and Ms. Julieta Janeo; (ii) OR for the payment of term membership in the Vice-Mayors League of the Philippines (VMLP); (iii) ORs and Certificate of Appearance for the payment of registration fees for: 2nd quarter 2016 Regular National Executive Board Meeting of the VMLP and 4th quarter 2016 Regular National Executive Board Meeting of the VMLP; and (iv) Certificate of Appearance for Training Workshop on the PFM Assessment Tool, Public Financial Management Assessment Tool (PFMAT) Software and PFM Improvement Plan for Local Government Units (LGUs) on August 15 to 16, 2016.
(Additional supporting documents enumerated in the table are still not submitted as of report date.)
observations.reason: The following documents are still not submitted: (i) Certifications issued by the LCE that the claims of the municipal officials and employees for hotel accommodation in excess of the allowable hotel room/lodging rate of ₱400.00 is absolutely necessary in accordance with EO No. 298 for the following Municipal officials and employees: Hon. Tanie Malitao, Jr., Hon. Michael Malaluan, Hon. Rowell Ganad, Hon. Imelda Callo, Engr. Mark Mahlon Parial and Ms. Julieta Janeo; (ii) OR for the payment of term membership in the Vice-Mayors League of the Philippines (VMLP); (iii) ORs and Certificate of Appearance for the payment of registration fees for: 2nd quarter 2016 Regular National Executive Board Meeting of the VMLP and 4th quarter 2016 Regular National Executive Board Meeting of the VMLP; and (iv) Certificate of Appearance for Training Workshop on the PFM Assessment Tool, Public Financial Management Assessment Tool (PFMAT) Software and PFM Improvement Plan for Local Government Units (LGUs) on August 15 to 16, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipality should strictly adhere to the issuance of checks to financial assistance clients in accordance with the provisions set in Section VI B.3.1 of Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) Administrative Order (AO) No. 5 series of 2011; b. Municipal Accountant submit the necessary supporting documents; and c. Local Chief Executive thru the assistance of the Social Welfare Officer formulate clear-cut policies and well-defined guidelines for the granting of all types of donations/financial assistance, which shall provide among others, the following: (i) a clear definition of the qualified beneficiaries and what kind of assistance or donation is chargeable against a specific fund; (ii) the criteria in determining the amount of donation to be given in every case and the designated person/s that shall be responsible to determine the same; and (iii) complete list of documents to be required for each type of donation/assistance. Moreover, apportionment of funds to more beneficial programs and projects should be carefully considered in order to achieve the desired betterment of the whole constituency.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor issued Executive Order No. 14 series of 2018 dated September 21, 2018 amending on the guidelines on the giving financial assistance by the Mayor’s Office thru Municipal Social and Welfare Development Office (MSWDO) to the unfortunate constituent of Municipality of Victoria.
The Municipality committed to adhere on the recommendations of COA. The Municipal Accountant submit the necessary supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Mr. Remar D. Dela Rosa be appointed as Meat Inspector and that the Municipality explain the noted observations and submit the necessary documents.
observations.managementAction:
Mr. Remar D. Dela Rosa was appointed as Meat Inspector on July 11, 2007. He explained that the slaughterhouse operation for the large cattle starts at 9:00 PM and the slaughtering of hog begins at 2:00 AM daily, thus exposing him to occupational risk, the working hours, night perils, the hazard being exposed to zoonotic diseases like bird flu, tuberculosis, and among others and the exposure to sharp instruments and equipment.
These activities may qualify in the circumstances itemized under Item 3.2.3 Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2016 of Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and Department of Health (DOH) dated July 15, 2016 which provides that the specific work areas in DOH Central Office, DOH-Regional offices, DOH attached agencies, and other health-related establishments where Public Health Workers (PHWs) are exposed to patients with contagious and communicable diseases, or handling infectious specimens for testing, chemicals and other hazardous items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Designated General Services Officer (GSO) prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property to be approved by proper authorities; b. Municipal Mayor create Committee on Appraisal of Property to appraise all the unserviceable properties and request the disposal of the same pursuant to Section 379 of RA No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 92-386; and c. Municipal Accountant explain the non-recording of two motor vehicles.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality informed that IIRUP will be submitted soon to COA.
The Municipal Appraisal Committee informed that they will prioritize the disposal despite voluminous transactions of the Municipality.
The Municipal Accountant explained that reconciliation was made with the designated GSO. The two motor vehicles were already unserviceable before she assumed in her position in CY 2002. No turnover of documents were made and during reconciliation it was discovered that the two motor vehicles were acquired by the previous administration without cost.
observations.reason: The designated GSO informed that they will submit the IIRUP once completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board (MSWMB) in coordination with the Municipal Health Officer and other concerned agencies provide necessary training for the SWM staff to ensure that the solid wastes are handled properly and to minimize the risk of hazards to the SWM personnel and the public. We also recommended that the MSWMB observe the standards and requirements for the collection, transportation and handling of solid wastes required by the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (RA) No. 9003.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality informed that (a) vaccination were given by the Municipal Health Office to SWM staff and protective gears were issued to the collectors of the solid waste; and (b) with the new appointment of permanent Sanitary Inspector, annual training is schedule on the first quarter of Calendar Year (CY) 2019 exclusively for proper handling of different wastes of the Local Government Unit (LGU).
The designated MENRO informed that he conducted weekly informal meeting with the SWM staff for proper handling of waste and the types of wastes to be collected.
observations.reason: The Municipality will conduct formal trainings for the collection, handling and transporting of wastes in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality encourage the barangay to establish MRF that will receive the mixed wastes for final sorting, segregation, composting or recycling before transferring the residual wastes to sanitary landfill. Moreover, we suggested that MSWMB strictly implement and monitor the collection and segregation of wastes at the barangay level as required by the provisions of RA No. 9003 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR).
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality informed that with the new management of Association of Barangay Captains (ABC), establishment of MRF was included in the plan and budget of different barangays.
The Municipality submitted the Certification of the ABC President as to number of MRF established in barangays. The ABC President, certified that only five Barangays have established MRFs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate lawful amount for the other benefits of the public social workers and public social welfare and development workers as stipulated in RA No. 9433.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer certified that she and the Public Social Welfare and Development Workers are already receiving the benefits under the provision of Magna Carta for Public Social Worker since CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze, confirm and ascertain the validity of the long outstanding liabilities in the Trust Fund book and if found with valid claims notify the claimants, for other payables account, if found without valid documentation and/or which have been outstanding for more than two years or more and against which no actual claim has been filed, proper adjustment in the books of accounts can be made and return the amount to the source agencies or to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction:
Dormant accounts were transferred to General Fund as of December 31, 2018.
The Municipality submitted Statement of Funding Sources (Supplemental Budget No. 3) as of September 3, 2018 and Schedule of Sources of Surplus for the period of January 1 to September 30, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that that an updated project reports covering the whole period of operation be rendered by the concerned Municipal and MinSCAT officials/employees. Records, particularly subsidiary ledgers, showing the construction costs of these capital assets, a clear financial report on the results of the project operation and inventory reports should be produced for further audit. Thereafter, any balance of cash should be turned over to the Municipality while the fixed assets should be transferred to MinSCAT where they have been erected. The corresponding accounting entries as these procedures are complied with would logically close the erroneous Investment account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor informed that he will set a meeting with the new administration of MinSCAT thru their General Services Officer (GSO) to resolve the issue and to verify if there are still assets of the ACBS under the MinSCAT compound/properties.
observations.reason: Retrieval of various records pertaining to construction cost and other related disbursements is still on going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the positions required under the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular 2014-1 be filled up immediately by the Municipality to properly institutionalize the organization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office. We recommend that LGU strictly implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 with regard to appropriation and utilization of the 5% of the revenue from the regular sources for disaster preparedness and mitigation to help achieve desirable protection and preservation of the lives and properties of the constituents of the municipality. We recommend that management efficiently utilize the allotted 70% Mitigation Fund. Likewise direct the Municipal Accountant to follow and submit the same in the prescribed format, recording and reporting guidelines.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 AAR 2016 AAR 2015
observations.reason: The LGU submitted a report but not in accordance with the AAR format and was provided by DRRM staff yet to be created; and did not optimally utilize the fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that: a. A list of the Municipality’s programs/projects/activities, inclusive of those for implementation and non-infrastructure projects be submitted and be posted in signboards or black/bulletin boards following the provisions of the cited circular; b. Relevant information regarding the PPAs of the Municipality be posted in signboards or appropriate public places; c. The Audit Team be informed that the appropriate project signboards and/or notices have been posted within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity; and d. Maintain updated Project Status based on the date sourced from the monthly monitoring report prepared by Municipality.
observations.reason: The Mun. Engineer has submitted list of PPAs and other required documents. AAR other required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to render her accounts and submit the required financial transactions together with supporting documents to the Accounting Division, and the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts and reports to COA on or before the 10th day of the succeeding month. Likewise, the accountable officer and/or other officials concerned are reminded of the sanctions imposed under Sec. 2 of PD 1455 in relation to Art. 218 of the Revised Penal Code on the automatic suspension of payment of salaries for failure to render accounts in a period of two months.
observations.reason: A number of financial accounts/transactions were failed to be submitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the supporting documents for some entries in the General Journal and the schedules or list of such supporting documents; prepare the Subsidiary Ledgers correctly and rectify other deficiencies noted. Likewise, include data for the Special Education Fund in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and justify the differences noted.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has submitted some supporting documents. The Municipal Accountant did not include SEF in SCBAA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit) and the Reconciliation between actual amounts on a comparable basis as presented in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and in the Statement of Financial Performance for the Special Education Fund. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) in the preparation of the financial statements.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has presented the Reconciliation between actual amounts on a comparable basis as presented in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and in the Statement of Financial Performance. However, reconciliation for SEF in the face of financial statements was not prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Solid Waste Management Board actively perform the functions required and implement the appropriation and projects/programs/activities in the Annual Investment Plan for the solid waste management program as well as execute the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9003 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.
observations.reason: The SWM Board was created and some actions taken; however full implementation pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002, PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, and require all officials and employees concerned to immediately settle/liquidate their outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: The LGU communicated to the officers to liquidate their outstanding advances; some advances were settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU refrain from hiring private legal consultant without first securing the required documents stated in COA Circular 86-255 as amended by COA Circular No. 95-011, Section 9.1.2 of COA Circular 2012-001 and Section 10 of RA 9184.
observations.reason: The LGU has yet to comply with the recommendation; some documents were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the recipient officials and employees to refund the amount in excess of the authorized amount as prescribed in E.O. No. 80 and DBM BC No. 2014-3.
observations.reason: Employees were required to refund the excess of PEI; a number of recipients refunded; some settlements still pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit bank reconciliation statements for the SEF and Trust Fund and to draw journal entries to record valid reconciling items for all funds in accordance with Sections 3.2 and 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has submitted bank reconciliation statements for the trust fund. Some reconciling items were adjusted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officers and employees to strictly comply with the provisions of E.O. 298, refund the amount received in excess of authorized travel rates for the payment of attendance to conventions, and stop the practice of issuing travel authority for attendance to conventions to which personnel are not affiliated.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013
observations.reason: Employees were required to refund excess cash advance. Only P2,490.00 liquidated for CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor and the concerned department head prioritize the revision of the SWMP in accordance with Section 17 of R.A No. 9003 for submission and approval by the NSWMC.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality will adhere to the COA auditor’s recommendation. Prioritization will be made once the MSWMB will be convened.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal officials concerned ensure that the 20% DF were appropriated and disbursed only for the priority projects, programs and activities mentioned in DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality committed to adhere on COA auditors recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the rest of the members of the Joint Committee of the CNA submit valid justifications for items (a) and (b) and the required document in item (c) as required in the afore-cited circulars. In case of non-compliance to the above mentioned recommendation, the Municipal Mayor, Accountant and Treasurer enforce the refund of the CNA Incentive received by the 107 municipal officials and employees totaling ₱2,096,000.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted justification for granting CNA Incentives but verification of the Team revealed it was not in accordance with the cost cutting measures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board in coordination with the Municipal Health Officer and other concerned agencies provide necessary training for the SWM staff to ensure that the solid wastes are handled properly and to minimize the risk of hazards to the SWM personnel and the public as well. We also recommended that the Municipality’s SWM Board observe the standards and requirements for the collection, transportation and handling of solid wastes required by the provisions of the IRR of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: Not acted upon by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality encourage the barangay to establish materials recovery facility that will receive the mixed wastes for final sorting, segregation, composting or recycling before transferring the residual wastes to sanitary landfill. Moreover, we suggested that Municipality’s SWM Board strictly implement and monitor the collection and segregation of wastes at the barangay level as required by the provisions of RA No. 9003 and its IRR.
observations.managementAction: Not Acted Upon by the Management.
observations.reason: The MENRO is loaded with heavy responsibilities, thus pressure of equally important matters.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal’s Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate lawful amount for the other benefits of the public social workers and public social welfare and development workers as stipulated in RA No. 9433.
observations.managementAction: Not acted upon by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the supporting documents for some entries in the General Journal and the schedules or list of some General Ledgers accounts, use Liquidation Report in the liquidation of prepayments for travelling expenses, prepare the Subsidiary Ledgers correctly and rectify the other deficiencies noted. Likewise, include data for the Special Education Fund in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and justify the differences noted.
observations.reason: Did not submit the reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit) and the Reconciliation between actual amounts on a comparable basis as presented in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and in the Statement of Financial Performance. Henceforth, strictly comply the provisions of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) in the preparation of the financial statements.
observations.reason: The management still did not comply.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management submit the lacking supporting documents; rectify the deficiencies noted; refrain from having transactions with suppliers where pecuniary interest exist; require the contractors/suppliers to issue official receipt in gross amount; and implement its projects within the specified contract time. Henceforth, refrain from payment and undergoing procurement process without complete documentations, and strictly comply the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR.
We also recommend that management submit justification for the early period of action on procurement activities; for the early completion of the Construction of San Lazaro Seawall (Phase 4); for the hiring of a legal consultant without the proper procurement process; and why COA was not informed of the deliveries within 24 hours from acceptance.
Likewise, we recommend that official responsible for accepting deliveries of procured items furnish COA with delivery documents within twenty-four (24) hours after such acceptance.
These recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension, Notice of Disallowance and Notice of Charge as a result of evaluation of the documents and justifications to be submitted by the municipality.
observations.reason: Did not submit the reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management submit the required Memorandum of Agreement/Trust Agreement of the different projects as well as the Project Briefs and Local Poverty Reduction Action Plan (LPRAP) for the livelihood projects. Likewise, submit the lacking supporting documents and justifications as well as rectify the other deficiencies noted. Further, we recommend that projects be implemented upon receipt of the funds for the benefit of the constituents/beneficiaries.
observations.reason: Did not submit the reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management immediately implement the programs/projects/ activities appropriated under the 20% development fund and realign the unimplemented programs/projects/activities which were not in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes. 
We also recommend that management submit the Memorandum of Agreement with the source agency for the fund transfers totaling P1,012,257.45 and justify the need for the daily maintenance of the projects totaling P1,638,800.00.  Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entry for the erroneous recording of expenses totaling P869,520.00.
observations.reason: Did not submit the reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management efficiently utilize the 70% Mitigation Fund of the LDRRMF, rectify the deficiencies noted and submit the lacking supporting documents. Likewise, we recommend that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer prepare the required reports and submit the same in the prescribed format and period; the Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting entries  for the CY 2016 Capital Outlay which was transferred to Trust Fund and strictly observe the prescribed recording and reporting guidelines; and the Property Officer maintain separate registry and prepare the required monthly reports on the receipt and issuance of in-kind donations. These recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Notice of Disallowance as a result of the post audit of the required documents.
observations.reason: The management still did not comply.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal School Board, in preparing their budget, give priority to the programs/projects/activities which are the immediate needs of the students and will enhance their learning skills as envisioned by RA 5447. Likewise, we recommend that management refrain from granting cash advances to persons not connected with the municipality, rectify the deficiencies noted, submit the lacking supporting documents, transact with bonafide suppliers, and discontinue paying previous year transactions which were not obligated. Henceforth, strictly adhere the provisions of Sections 100 (a) and (c) and 272 of Republic Act 7160  and RA 5447.
observations.reason: Did not submit the reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Solid Waste Management Board actively perform the functions required by law. Likewise, we recommend that management provide appropriation and implement the projects/programs/activities in the Annual Investment Plan for the solid waste management program as well as execute the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9003 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We recommend that management furnish the audit team with the advice on the review by the SangguniangPanlalawigan and the Local Finance Committee (LFC) of the Province of Northern Samar on the appropriation ordinances. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of Sec. 327 of RA 7160 or the Local Government Code.
observations.reason: Did not submit the reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create an inventory team that would conduct the periodic physical count of property, plant and equipment, prepare the corresponding report, and submit to the Auditor concerned within the deadline set. Likewise, the Accounting and Property Unit of the LGU should maintain property records to facilitatethe tracing and verification of account balances. Furthermore, the agency should submit list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (“PPA”) during the year and those that are still to be implemented and inform the audit team that appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted, to assist in the verification of the Construction in Progress Account.
observations.reason: Management did not conduct physical inventory and prepare report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU strictly adhere to the specific provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 and Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 on the accounting, reporting and utilization of the DRRMF in-kind donations. Furthermore, we specifically require the submission of reports on the distribution/utilization/issuance and other necessary supporting documents of in-kind donations received from DSWD RO. VIII.
observations.reason: No report was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management refrain from disbursing government funds without complete documentations in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD 1445.
observations.reason: No submission of the lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU refrain from hiring private legal consultant without first securing the required documents stated in COA Circular No. 86-255 as amended by COA Circular No. 95-011, Section 9.1.2 of COA Circular 2012-001 and Section 10 of RA 9184.
observations.reason: Still hired a lawyer with lacking required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management adhere strictly with the provisions of E.O. No. 80 specifically on the authorized amount to be granted for the payment of Productivity Enhancement Incentive. We also recommend that the recipient officials and employees be required to refund the amount in excess of the authorized amount as prescribed in E.O. No. 80 and DBM BC No. 2014-3.
observations.reason: No evidence of refund was submitted but the management informed the audit team that they will start to refund in 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that The Bids and Awards Committee be required to submit copies of approved contracts/purchase orders within five (5) days after their execution/issuance and copies of delivery documents within 24 hours after acceptance of delivered goods and services as provided for in COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2009-002. This recommendation is without prejudice to the result of evaluation.
observations.reason: Management was not able to submit on time.
