observations.managementAction: The MSWD furnished  the Auditor list of dormant receivable.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 5 under Part II of this report. The Team requested to submit additional documents to support the request for write off. Audit

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct: (a) the Treasury Office to update the RPTARs/ Taxpayers’ Index Card and subsequently, prepare and furnish the Accounting Office the list required under Section 20 Volume I of the Manual on the  for LGUs; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to establish  and SET receivables sourcing from the list duly certified by the Treasury Office.

We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed to instruct:
the Municipal Accountant (i) to maintain complete PPELC for each class of property to record the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof; and (ii) to make necessary adjustments and recognize financial transactions properly to avoid misstatement of affected accounts;
the Municipal Treasurer to prepare PC of all PPE including properties acquired from previous years; and
the Inventory Committee to complete the physical verification of all properties of the Municipality and render required reports thereon.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the BAC and its Secretariat, the Accounting Office and the Engineering Office to exercise due diligence in preparing documents and processing disbursements and to ensure that required supporting documents are properly accomplished and attached to the disbursement vouchers. Furthermore, we recommended that the deficiencies noted in Annex A be submitted or settled immediately.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Management agreed to ensure that an employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the minimum allowed by the existing law (GAA provision) and avoid the issuance of certification allowing the acquisition of loans for employees and subsequently rendering their net pay to fall below the minimum. Further, we recommended that payroll deductions shall be limited following the order of preference provided in the GAA.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: (a) intensify further the conduct of Information and Education Campaigns to make the residents aware of the existing environmental laws and policies with their corresponding fines and penalties; (b)  instruct the designated enforcers to ensure that the corresponding fines and penalties will be served to violators to reduce the instances of prohibited acts such as littering, open burning, and collection of non-segregated or unsorted waste; and (c) instruct the solid waste management board to continue to monitor the effective implementation of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan.

We recommended and the Municipality GFPS agreed to: (a) accomplish properly the GAD Accomplishment Report by including the actual results of the implemented GAD Activities and the reasons for their variances/deviations with the set targets; (b) ensure that all the activities included in its GAD Plan are implemented to be able to attain their corresponding GAD Objectives; and (c) continue to comply with all GAD rules and regulations to promote gender mainstreaming.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Budget Officer, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 and Advisory No. 2016-02 dated November 25, 2016 of the Interim National Peace and Order Council Secretariat of DILG relevant to appropriation, entitlement, release, use and reporting of the confidential funds.

We recommended and Management agreed to: (a) stop the practice of paying fixed allowance for communication without the submission of proof on the incurrence of expense showing or justifying the actual usage or consumption by the concerned officials and employees and (b) consider the availment of contracts subscriptions with telephone companies offering discounts or services promotions.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
require the LSB to follow and perform the activities in the preparation and approval of the SEF Budget with indicative timelines as prescribed by DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 or the Revised Guidelines on the Use of Special Education Fund (SEF); and
require the Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare the Report of SEF Utilization quarterly using the prescribed form for submission to appropriate offices; and (ii) make necessary adjustments and recognize financial transactions properly to avoid misstatement of affected accounts.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Human Resource and Management Officer to (a) remit all the due taxes and premiums within the prescribed period; and (b) reconcile the withheld amounts and actual remittance periodically to ensure that all deductions are remitted intact and on time.

We recommended that the:
a. GSO and Municipal Accountant continue tracing/investigating the
discrepancies between the RPCPPE and accounting records and adjust
them;
b. Inventory Committee to accomplish the RPCPPE in accordance with the
format prescribed in the MNGAS for LGU;
c. Municipal Accountant prepare a subsidiary record for each component
of the road network and Local Road Network Ledger Card;
d. GSO maintain Local Road Inventory Road Map and Local Road
Property Ledger Card. Moreover, conduct physical count of the road
network and prepare Report on the Physical Count of Local Road
Network (RPCLRN) and submit the same to this office not later than
January 31 of each year; and
e. Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO -
Designate with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to (a) adjust the
stale checks appearing in their BRS to fairly present the cash balance of the
Municipality, (b) continue disclosing in the Notes to Financial Statements about the
deposits made by the Mining Company to their account amounting to ₱171,825.00
and (c) submit to the COA Audit Team the copy of the passbook for the Cash in
Bank balance of ₱183,832.90 appearing as reconciling item.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the
lacking documents relative the procured lot.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a. Municipal Mayor designate Special Disbursing Officer (SDO) to handle
the disbursing functions for the Supplemental Feeding Program; and
b. OIC-Municipal Treasurer (i) monitor the fidelity bond of every AO for
public funds and request for an increase of bond to cover the maximum
cash accountability provided under BTr Circular No. 02-2009 dated
August 6, 2009; and (ii) require the DO and SDO to maintain cashbook
and properly accomplish it pursuant to Section 28 of the MNGAS for
LGUs.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Engineer to
prepare the monthly monitoring Report of the Awarded Infra PPAs and List of
Ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year in accordance
with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended and Management agreed that the BAC Chairman furnish
the Office of the Auditor with the copy of executed contracts, PO and its pertinent
supporting documents within the prescribed period.

We recommend that Municipal Mayor:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to institute action for the collection of the accounts or other appropriate remedies to clear the amount in the books;
Analyze each fund transfer account balance and determine the reason why these were not liquidated and undertake immediate appropriate action thereon; and
If warranted, request/apply for the write-off of the accounts following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:
Give priority for the creation of an Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of PPE.
Direct Head of the General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office to hasten the updating of their records to facilitate the reconciliation of the accounting and property records.
Oblige the Municipal Accountant to review the details of properties which are non-functional or unserviceable and prepare the necessary adjustments, if warranted.
Require the committee to prepare a methodical and well planned physical inventory taking, which could be done in a year round basis, until all the Property, Plant and Equipment of the municipality have been accounted for and listed in the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). Also, all AREs must be renewed regularly pursuant to existing rules and regulations.
Thereafter, the RPCPPE must be reconciled with the property record of each class of property at the General Services Office and subsidiary ledgers maintained at the Accounting Office.  Any discrepancy should be investigated and appropriate action should be taken thereof.
Make representation with the previous accounting personnel to substantiate and reconciled the forwarded balances prior to CY 2012.
Work out and to see to it that Titles of the purchased and donated parcels of land are transferred in the name of the Municipal Government of San Isidro to establish absolute ownership thereof.
Direct strict compliance with the accounting and reporting guidelines for local road network construction and maintenance system prescribed by .COA Circular No. 2015-008;
Instruct the Accountants to draw a JEV to transfer the costs of completed projects from CIP-Infrastructure Assets and Building and Other Structures account to Infrastructure Assets and Building/School building or Other structures account.

We recommend strict observance to the provision of Section 5.1.5 COA Circular No. 2012-002 requiring submission of the report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the COA auditor of the LGU.
We recommend that the positions required under the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 be filled up immediately by the Municipality to properly institutionalize the organization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office.
We recommend that LGU strictly implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 with regard to appropriation and utilization of the 5% of the revenue from the regular sources for disaster preparedness and mitigation to help achieve desirable protection and preservation of the lives and properties of the constituents of the municipality.
We recommend that management efficiently utilize the allotted 70% Mitigation Fund. Likewise direct the Municipal Accountant to follow and submit the same in the prescribed format, recording and reporting guidelines.

Since fiscal responsibility rests upon the Head of the Agency, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to render her accounts and submit the required financial transactions together with supporting documents to the Accounting Division, and the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts and reports to COA on or before the 10th day of the succeeding month.
Likewise, the accountable officer and/or other officials concerned are reminded of the sanctions imposed under Sec. 2 of PD 1455 in relation to Art. 218 of the Revised Penal Code on the automatic suspension of payment of salaries for failure to render accounts in a period of two months.

We recommend that management formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations, and submit immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI form to the Audit Team, copy furnished the DBM, the House of Committee on Appropriations and Senate Committee on Finance pursuant to Section 93 of R.A. 10651.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services
Officer — Designate to evaluate the discrepancies between their records
and make the necessary adjustments, if there are any;

instruct the Municipal General Services Officer — Designate to maintain
Property Gards with somplete and necessary information to facilitate

34
verification and reconciliation with the Accounting Office PPE Ledger
Cards;

c. direct the Municipal General Services Officer — Designate to conduct
annual physical inventory of all PPEs to determine any
missing/unaccounted properties and take/recommend the necessary
actions against those accountable; and

d. dispose all unserviceable properties in accordance with the above-cited
regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor discontinue the granting of
cash advances to accountable officer in excess of his maximum cash
accountability, otherwise instruct the accountable officer to increase his fidelity
bond sufficient to cover his accountabilities.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the
provisions of Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I to be able to
establish with certainty the RPT/SET Receivables.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to:

a. set-up RPT Receivable and SET Receivable at the beginning of the year
based on duly certified list of taxpayers, the amount due and collectible
for the year provided by the Municipal Treasurer; and

b. ensure the equality of amounts set-up and collected for RPT and SET
Receivables.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:

a. require the Municipal Accountant to:

a.1 make a reconciliation and monitoring of all booked receivables to
ensure that only valid receivables are recorded in the books;

a.2 analyze the accounts that are dormant and long outstanding and take
the necessary actions to drop them from the books, pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 16, 2016; and

b. instruct the Municipal Social Welfare Assistant to determine the
whereabouts of the loan borrowers for possible collection of the loans
granted to them.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. ensure that the implementation of the PPAs included in the 20%
Development Fund is in accordance with the timeline indicated in their
Annual Investment Plan (AIP) so that maximum benefits will be realized
for its constituents; and

b. strictly implement the provisions of Section 5.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No.
2017-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. require the Municipal General Services Officer to start the preparation
and maintenance of LRNPC as required under COA Circular 2015-008
dated November 25, 2015, and

b. the Municipal Accountant to disclose information on the total road
network system in the Notes to Financial Statements following the format
prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint a permanent
employee as Head and Staff of the MDRRMO to satisfy the requirements of
Section 5.3 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-01 and effectively
discharge the attached duties and responsibilities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. direct the MSWD officials to prepare specific program/project/activity in
its AIP to facilitate evaluation on the proper utilization of the allocated
fund; and

b. instruct the MSWD officials, in coordination with the LCPC members, to
monitor the implementation of the PPAs intended to improve the lives of
the youth and help reduce, if not eliminate, juvenile delinquency in the
Municipality.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive ensure the proper grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances by instructing the Municipal Accountant to (a) strictly monitor the cash advances of officials/employees such that no cash advance is granted to those with pending unliquidated cash advances; (b) make sure that said officials/employees settle their accounts within the time set by pertinent rules and regulations; and (c) ensure that fully documented liquidation reports are submitted to the COA office.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer reconcile her cashbook balance with the accounting records to identify the recording errors committed and  take the appropriate adjustments in her records.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct – (a) the Treasury Office to prepare and furnish the Accounting Office the list required under Section 20 Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to base the establishment of RPT and SET receivables on the list submitted by the Treasury Office for an accurate recognition of the said receivables.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Inventory Team to finish the inventory taking of all the properties of the Municipality and prepare and submit the result of the physical inventory that will be used by the designated property officer and the Municipal Accountant as basis for reconciling their records. Also, the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant and the designated Property Officer to assign personnel who can efficiently handle the task on the maintenance of the property ledger cards and property cards per PPE, respectively. In case the concerned employees failed to render the required reports and maintain property records, enforce Section 122 of the P.D. No. 1445 which states that failure on the part of officials concerned to submit required documents and reports shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct DepEd employees to turn-over to the Municipality the issued properties upon transfer to other school or town; and (b) request the DepEd to inform the Municipality for every movement of DepEd personnel assigned in Bamban for the Municipal Treasurer to be able to monitor the proper turn-over of issued properties to the employees who will be moving out from a certain school.

We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 5447, R.A. No. 7160, LOI 1462 and relevant DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto treating on the utilization of the Special Education Fund to avoid disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to submit all the lacking documents in order to enable us to issue and convey appropriate audit action or decision. Further, we recommended the submission of an explanation why the supporting canvass sheets for the purchase of hog grower were not appropriately filled-up showing the prices obtained from the prospective suppliers.

We recommended that the concerned LGU officials comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2009-001 to avoid suspension of the transactions and the penalty prescribed by law under 4.1 of this Circular.

We recommended that the Municipality comply with the requirement on posting of awards for shopping and small-value procurement in the PhilGEPS as required in the above regulation and to show full compliance with the principle on transparency, as mandated by RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the BAC members and the Municipal Engineer to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for their timely implementation and completion; and (b) ensure that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that these projects could serve their purposes to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Municipality: (a) expedite the development of a GAD Database to be able to use the collected data in making more relevant decisions; (b) include in the GAD Plan and Budget the conduct of more capability-building and enhancement trainings, especially in the administration of the HGDG to be able to better identify and prioritize GAD projects and programs; and (c) continue its efforts in gender mainstreaming to be able to institutionalize GAD within the whole community for the benefit of not only a particular gender or sector, but for the benefit and equality of all.
We recommended that management comply with the minimum limit of P3,500.00 for employees’ monthly net take home pay pursuant to Section 52 of GAA 2016.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRM Officer to prepare a monthly Report on the Utilization of DRRMF certified correct by the Accountant, and submit to COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant assist him in preparing the report.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE), as chairman of the MDRRMC, cause the selection and accreditation of CSOs in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the aforementioned M.C.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive a) instruct the Treasury Office to monitor compliance with contract provisions by the lessees; b) see to it that he is not re-entering into a contract with a defaulting lessee; and c) include a penalty provision in the MOA in case of a default to make the lessee pay his obligation on time. We recommended further that the Mayor’s Office comply with the provisions of COA Circular on the submission of contracts.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant require the concerned accounting personnel to properly maintain and update the ledger cards for PPE which shall contain complete description as to acquisition cost and date, quantity and other necessary information/data; b. MGSO and Municipal Accountant (i) work together in tracing the discrepancies between the inventory report and accounting records; (ii) prepare an adjustment to affected records; and (iii) maintain ledger cards and reports pursuant to MNGAS, Volume II; c. Inventory Committee accomplish the Inventory Report in accordance with the MNGAS for LGU and fully conduct the physical inventory of PPE; and d. Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer and Assessor to exert their best efforts to gather the necessary documents to complete the requirements for the processing of the TCT of one parcel of land in favor of the Municipality of Victoria. Coordination with the proper agency such as Register of Deeds shall be made to facilitate the titling and/or transfer of certificate of title in favor of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
With the appointment of Sr. Bookkeeper maintenance of ledger cards will be monitored so as to conform with the audit recommendations. The reconciliation of the PPE is still ongoing. Result of physical inventory will be considered for the adjustments to be made.
All departments are required to submit their inventory indicating the serviceable from unserviceable. These documents will facilitate the physical inventory and documentation to be accomplished by the Inventory Team.
The Municipal Treasurer and Assessor commit to exert their best effort to complete the necessary documents for the processing of the said TCT.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant analyze the recorded Public Infrastructures, prepare the necessary journal entry voucher to record the 25% of the public infrastructures transferred in the registry and submit the Registry of Public Infrastructures for verification; and prepare a subsidiary record for each component of the road network and Local Road Network Ledger Card; b. MGSO maintain Local Road Inventory, Road Map and Local Road Property Ledger Card; and conduct physical count of the road network and prepare Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN) and submit the same to this office not later than January 31 of each year; and c. Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the MGSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction:
The office of the Municipal Accountant is still in the process of analyzing the Public Infrastructure Registries since they have not yet received the complete description and cost segregation of road projects and its components from the Office of the Municipal Engineer. Absence of additional technical person is the reason of the Municipal Engineer for not providing the said documents.
The Municipality committed to provide the necessary documents to the office of the Municipal Accountant and designated GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review and verify the reconciling items identified in the BRS and effect the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balances of the Cash in Bank Account.
observations.managementAction: The necessary adjusting entries were already taken up in the books for the first and second quarter of CY 2017. Quarterly interest income is already reflected in the June, 2017 financial statement. However, some reconciling items still not adjusted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. MDRRMO (i) prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the format provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to reflect the allocation for QRF in lump sum and disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness prepared with details as to projects and activities to be funded, as well as the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; (ii) ensure that all the proposed programs/projects for disaster be implemented during the year to ascertain readiness of the Municipality in case of calamity; and (iii) and in case there is change in the previously proposed projects, see to it that the disaster council is guided by DILG, DBM and National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council Joint Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013 wherein the unexpended balance of disaster fund may be released and utilized for new expenditure items upon submission of a revised AIP as approved by the local Sanggunian and the LCE upon recommendation in the council and in accordance with the approved disaster plan; b. Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to prepare and submit monthly the Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month in consonance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; c. Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and the MDRRMO reconcile the balances that appear in the SAAOB and the Report on Utilization of DRRMF; d. Municipal Budget Officer maintain separate RAAO for MOOE and CO of the 70% allocation of LDRRMF and monitor the appropriations of LDRRMF on per PPA basis to facilitate and ensure an accurate basis on certifying availability of appropriation for obligations; e. BAC (i) explain the deficiencies noted above; and (ii) strictly adhere with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 in the conduct of procurement transactions by procuring goods based on the relevant characteristics and/or performance requirement instead of basing on brand names and procure only those that are necessary, of equally higher quality but with cheaper price; f. Municipal Accountant and BAC submit the supporting documents to substantiate the transactions; and g. Municipal Accountant (i) draw Journal Entry Voucher transfer fund from the Trust Fund to the General Fund to adjust the affected account for the fair presentation in the financial statements; and (ii) maintain subsidiary ledgers for transfer of unutilized calamity fund to trust fund by year of transfer.
observations.managementAction:
The necessary reconciliations is still on-going as per the COA auditors recommendation. The Municipality submitted the documents for the procurement of the disaster supplies.
Reconciliation is still on-going as per the COA auditors recommendation.
The Municipality submitted the documents for the procurement of the disaster supplies. The recommendations will be considered once reconciliation and records of the MDRRMO and budget office will be established so as to properly monitor the disbursement and unutilized balances of the 5% calamity fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Mayor and the concerned official explain the noted observations with probable solutions to address the present conditions in the Municipality, and see to it that the criteria cited in RA 9003 for establishment of controlled dumpsites and/or sanitary landfill are complied to mitigate the harmful effects in the environment of solid wastes generated by the locality and further damage to the health of its constituents and ensure that the constituents in the barangays burying and dumping their waste in the backyard are informed of proper waste disposal.
observations.managementAction: Reorganization of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board will be done and committed to prioritize the discussion of the detailed findings and recommendations of the COA auditors on the meeting of the Board. A qualified personel will be assigned also to closely monitor compliance with the recommended solutions of the Board.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Local School Board (i) consolidate all requests for procurement; and (ii) prepare and submit the PPMP for supplies and materials and infrastructural projects, consolidated APP, copy furnished the Audit Team; and b. Bids and Awards Committee (a) strictly adhere with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 in the conduct of procurement transactions by procuring goods based on the relevant characteristics and/or performance requirement instead of basing on brand names and procure only those that are necessary, of equally higher quality but with cheaper price; and (c) explain the observations noted regarding the BAC Resolutions.
observations.managementAction: The BAC chairman explained that the BAC resolution issued is good for shopping and small value procurement with provisions on the applicable mode of procurement. After the exit conference, the BAC committed to make a separate resolution starting July, 2017 for each alternative mode of procurement to avoid confusion to the readers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Treasurer and Accountant strictly adhere with Section 4 of PD No. 1445, Section 344 of RA No 7160 and COA Circular No. 2006-002 to ensure the validity and propriety of the transactions; b. Municipal Accountant see to it that the disbursement vouchers are supported with the necessary and properly accomplished; and c. Municipal School Board limit the utilization of the SEF to programs and projects prioritized in Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160 in order to maximize the benefits that the public school students could derive therefrom.
observations.managementAction:
Municipality committed to strictly adhere with the COA auditors recommendations to ensure the validity and propriety of the transactions. The Municipality submitted the copy of Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (ARE) for the amount of ₱88,300.00.
We commit to adhere with the COA auditors recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees: a. Explain why there was excessive claim of traveling expenses, otherwise, refund the amount of ₱192,159.92 for overpayment; b. Submit the necessary documents for the payment of travelling expenses; and c. Henceforth, ensure the compliance with the provisions set forth in Section 4 of EO No.298 and COA Circular No. 2012-001. Also, closely monitor the liquidation of CAs and immediately issue demand letter to officials and employees with pending cash advances; and b. Require Ms. Nelia Moong, Mary Ann Bayundang, Maricel Litan and Josephine Ordona to refund the excess CA of ₱60.00 each or totaling ₱240.00 due to erroneous recording of granted cash advance in the liquidation report of ₱3,820.00 instead of ₱3,880.00. d. Require the Municipal Accountant (i) submit all the liquidation reports for the cash advances granted to officers and employees and operating expenses totaling ₱299,454.00 and ₱741,726.55, respectively, to ascertain the amount liquidated; (ii) submit AF No. 51 for the excess CAs granted to officers and employees amounting to ₱13,668.86 if already paid and if not, require them to refund; and (iii) submit and file liquidation reports in a well-organized manner to facilitate the tracing of documents and to avoid misplacing it.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality submitted (i) liquidation reports of the Disbursing Officer amounting to ₱741,726.55 for the liquidation of cash advances granted to various officers and employees; (ii) AF No. 51 as proof of refund for the excess cash advances granted to various officers and employees; (iii) liquidation reports for ₱299,454.00 and ₱741,726.55 and copy of AF No. 51 for the refund made by employees for their respective cash advances including proof of payment made by Nelia Moong, Mary Ann Bayudang, Maricel Litan and Josphine Dimaculangan and Mary Ann Bayudang.
The Municipality committed to have separate files for liquidation reports for easy access of documents by the COA auditors. In the case of liquidation of cash advances beyond the prescribed period, the Municipality assured the COA auditors that close monitoring will be done so as to avoid inconvenience further.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees: a. Explain why there was excessive claim of traveling expenses, otherwise, refund the amount of ₱192,159.92 for overpayment; b. Submit the necessary documents for the payment of travelling expenses; and c. Henceforth, ensure the compliance with the provisions set forth in Section 4 of EO No.298 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the certification s issued by the LCE that the claims of the municipal officials and employees for hotel accommodation in excess of the allowable hotel room/lodging rate of ₱400.00 is absolutely necessary in accordance with Executive Order (EO) No. 298. Copy of supporting documents on the claims of other Municipal Officials are requested to be submitted before the end of August, 2017 for official receipts of conference fees were attached to the claims.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality should strictly adhere to the issuance of checks to financial assistance clients in accordance with the provisions set in Section VI B.3.1 of DSWD AO. No. 5 series of 2011; b. Municipal Accountant submit the necessary supporting documents; and c. Local Chief Executive thru the assistance of the Social Welfare Officer formulate clear-cut policies and well-defined guidelines for the granting of all types of donations/assistance, which shall provide among others, the following: (i) a clear definition of the qualified beneficiaries and what kind of assistance or donation is chargeable against a specific fund; (ii) the criteria in determining the amount of donation to be given in every case and the designated person/s that shall be responsible to determine the same; and (iii) complete list of documents to be required for each type of donation/assistance. Moreover, apportionment of funds to more beneficial programs and projects should be carefully considered in order to achieve the desired betterment of the whole constituency.
observations.managementAction: The office of the Municipal Accountant adopted the recommendations and the Executive Department is in the process of formulating the guidelines. As per recommendation of the COA auditors, the Executive Department is in the process of formulating the said guidelines and apportionment of the funds will be considered to beneficial programs and projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Treasurer’s Office employee in charged with the preparation of Report of Delinquent Market Stallholders provide the Municipal Accountant with amount due and penalties for unpaid market fees to facilitate timely recording of accrued rental income; and b. Municipal Accountant adopt the accrual basis of recording the stall fees pursuant to the Conceptual Framework for General Purpose Financial Reporting and follow PPSAS Nos. 1 and 13.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant adopted the accrual basis of recording of the Waterworks System Fees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the employees concerned refund Hazard Pay due to lack of legal basis and supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Legal basis and justification were submitted to COA. Verification of the Team revealed it was not in accordance with the cost cutting measures.
observations.reason: The hazard pay was not in accordance with Section 3 the DBM-DOH Joint Circular No.1s. 2016 dated July 1, 2016 since those officials and employees are not exposed to the danger, perils to life and physical hardships.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Government Officials and member of the Local Development Council properly plan, prioritize all activities and appraise the socio-economic programs and projects to be implemented during the year, and evaluate viability of the projects as well as the need for any additional fund needed to complete a project programmed in the previous years; and b. Municipal Engineer coordinate, monitor and evaluate the implementation of those projects in order to ensure their completion within the target date in order to preclude the unwarranted delay of the project completion, anxiety of enjoying the benefits therefrom by the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction:
The Project Monitoring and Evaluation Committee (PMEC) commits to regularly monitor the utilization of the 20% development fund and make necessary recommendation to the Local Development Council and Local Chief Executive based on the result of their evaluation.
With the appointment of new engineer in the office of the Municipal Engineer, rest assure that the recommendation of the COA auditors will be considered.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Designated GSO prepare IIRUP for the unserviceable properties to be approved by proper authorities; b. Municipal Mayor create Committee on Appraisal of Property to appraise all the unserviceable properties and request the disposal of the same pursuant to Section 379 of R.A. No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 92-386; and c. Municipal Accountant explain the non-recording of two motor vehicles.
observations.managementAction:
IIRUP report of unserviceable properties particularly motor vehicles has been prepared and ready for approval by the proper authorities.
The Appraisal Team commits to do their function once they will be furnished with the copy of inventory of unserviceable properties.
The office of the Municipal Accountant has no basis in recording the said motor vehicles since no proper document was presented as to the legal basis of its existence, the date and mode of acquisition of the said vehicles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor convene the GAD focal person and require them to develop performance or success indicators for each GAD activity, evaluate the priority projects for GAD during the year and ensure that the proposed GAD projects and activities are implemented at the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality appropriated 9.78% of the Annual Budget which is above the 5% minimum requirement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer to strictly comply with all the provisions of the aforementioned COA Circular by submitting the list of all ongoing and those to be implemented government PPAs to the COA and posting of project signboards and/or public notices within the time specified and likewise informing the Team for timely validation of the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer assigned staff to closely monitor for compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2014. He also requested to the Municipal Mayor for filling up the newly created position of Engineer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze, confirm and ascertain the validity of the long outstanding liabilities in the Trust Fund book and if found with valid claims notify the claimants, for other payables account, if found without valid documentation and/or which have been outstanding for two years or more and against which no actual claim has been filed, proper adjustment in the books of accounts can be made and return the amount to the source agencies or to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation on-going.
observations.reason: Unavailability of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that an updated project reports covering the whole period of operation be rendered by the concerned Municipal and MinSCAT officials/employees. Records, particularly subsidiary ledgers, showing the construction costs of these capital assets, a clear financial report on the results of the project operation and inventory reports should be produced for further audit. Thereafter, any balance of cash should be turned over to the Municipality while the fixed assets should be transferred to MinSCAT where they have been erected. The corresponding accounting entries as these procedures are complied with would logically close the erroneous Investment account.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality coordinated with the MinSCAT officials/employee relative to the records regarding this investment.
Records, particularly subsidiary ledgers, showing the construction cost and other related disbursements is still on-going.
observations.reason: Retrieval of various records pertaining to construction cost and other related disbursements is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of  COA Circular No. 97-002, PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, and require all officials and employees concerned to immediately settle/liquidate their outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: Not all cash advances were liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Health Officer to adhere strictly to Executive Order No. 49 and DOH Administrative Order No. 163 series of 2002, for all requisition and purchase of drugs and medicines.
observations.managementAction: Management Action

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prepare Monthly Report of Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption and to prepare the Driver’s Trip Tickets using the form prescribed in COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension and/or Disallowance as a result of the post-audit of transactions.
observations.reason: No Monthly Report of Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the timely submission of accounts and financial reports in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, Section 70 Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs and Sections 2 and 4 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010.
observations.reason: Late submission of some reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management secure approval from DOF to maintain depository account with Philippine National Bank, otherwise affected depository account may have to be closed pursuant to DOF Order No. 27-05 dated December 9, 2005.
observations.managementAction: Management Action

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.reason: Did not submit all the reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit bank reconciliation statements for the SEF and Trust Fund and to draw journal entries to record valid reconciling items for all funds in accordance with Sections 3.2 and 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.reason: Lacking Bank Reconcilia tion for the Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officers and employees to strictly comply with the provisions of E.O. 298, and refund the amount received in excess of the authorized travel rates as well as the unnecessary travel expenses. Stop the practice of issuing travel authority for attendance to conventions of organizations/leagues to which the personnel is not affiliated in order to preclude unnecessary travel expenses. Likewise, strictly observe the proper classification of accounts as provided for in the NGAS Manual.
observations.reason: Some concerned personnel have refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management comply with the provisions of RA 656.
observations.reason: Failure of the LGU to update valuation of the insurable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the rest of the members of the Joint Committee of the CNA submit valid justifications for items (a) and (b) and the required document in item (c) as required in the afore-cited circulars. In case of non-compliance to the above mentioned recommendation, the Municipal Mayor, Accountant and Treasurer enforce the refund of the CNA Incentive received by the 107 municipal officials and employees totaling ₱2,096,000.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted justification for granting CNA Incentives but verification of the Team revealed it was not in accordance with the cost cutting measures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board in coordination with the Municipal Health Officer and other concerned agencies provide necessary training for the SWM staff to ensure that the solid wastes are handled properly and to minimize the risk of hazards to the SWM personnel and the public as well. We also recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board observe the standards and requirements for the collection, transportation and handling of solid wastes required by the provisions of the IRR of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: Not acted upon by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality encourage the barangay to establish materials recovery facility that will receive the mixed wastes for final sorting, segregation, composting or recycling before transferring the residual wastes to sanitary landfill. Moreover, we suggested that the Municipal’s SWM Board strictly implement and monitor the collection and segregation of wastes at the barangay level as required by the provisions of RA No. 9003 and its IRR.
observations.managementAction: Not acted upon by the Management.
observations.reason: The MENRO is loaded with heavy responsibilities, thus pressure of equally important matters.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal’s Budget Officer to appropriate lawful amount for the other benefits of the public social workers and public welfare and development workers as stipulated in RA No. 9433.
observations.managementAction: Not acted upon by management during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU strictly adhere to the specific provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 and Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 on the accounting, reporting and utilization of the DRRMF in-kind donations. Furthermore, we specifically require the submission of reports on the distribution/utilization/issuance and other necessary supporting documents of in-kind donations received from DSWD RO. VIII.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015
observations.reason: No report submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management refrain from disbursing government funds without complete documentations in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU refrain from hiring private legal consultant without first securing the required documents stated in COA Circular No. 86-255 as amended by COA Circular No. 95-011, Section 9.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 10 of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015
observations.reason: Still hired a lawyer with lacking required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee be required to submit copies of approved contracts/purchase orders within five (5) days after their execution/issuance, and copies of delivery documents within 24 hours after acceptance of delivered goods and services as provided in COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2009-002.
observations.reason: Management was not able to submit on time.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive ensure the proper grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances by instructing the Municipal Accountant to (a) strictly monitor the cash advances of officials/employees such that no cash advance is granted to those with pending unliquidated cash advances; (b) make sure that said officials/employees settle their accounts within the time set by pertinent rules and regulations; and (c) ensure that fully documented liquidation reports are submitted to the COA office.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer reconcile her cashbook balance with the accounting records to identify the recording errors committed and  take the appropriate adjustments in her records.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct – (a) the Treasury Office to prepare and furnish the Accounting Office the list required under Section 20 Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to base the establishment of RPT and SET receivables on the list submitted by the Treasury Office for an accurate recognition of the said receivables.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Inventory Team to finish the inventory taking of all the properties of the Municipality and prepare and submit the result of the physical inventory that will be used by the designated property officer and the Municipal Accountant as basis for reconciling their records. Also, the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant and the designated Property Officer to assign personnel who can efficiently handle the task on the maintenance of the property ledger cards and property cards per PPE, respectively. In case the concerned employees failed to render the required reports and maintain property records, enforce Section 122 of the P.D. No. 1445 which states that failure on the part of officials concerned to submit required documents and reports shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct DepEd employees to turn-over to the Municipality the issued properties upon transfer to other school or town; and (b) request the DepEd to inform the Municipality for every movement of DepEd personnel assigned in Victoria for the Municipal Treasurer to be able to monitor the proper turn-over of issued properties to the employees who will be moving out from a certain school.

We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 5447, R.A. No. 7160, LOI 1462 and relevant DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto treating on the utilization of the Special Education Fund to avoid disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to submit all the lacking documents in order to enable us to issue and convey appropriate audit action or decision. Further, we recommended the submission of an explanation why the supporting canvass sheets for the purchase of hog grower were not appropriately filled-up showing the prices obtained from the prospective suppliers.

We recommended that the concerned LGU officials comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2009-001 to avoid suspension of the transactions and the penalty prescribed by law under 4.1 of this Circular.

We recommended that the Municipality comply with the requirement on posting of awards for shopping and small-value procurement in the PhilGEPS as required in the above regulation and to show full compliance with the principle on transparency, as mandated by RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the BAC members and the Municipal Engineer to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for their timely implementation and completion; and (b) ensure that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that these projects could serve their purposes to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Municipality: (a) expedite the development of a GAD Database to be able to use the collected data in making more relevant decisions; (b) include in the GAD Plan and Budget the conduct of more capability-building and enhancement trainings, especially in the administration of the HGDG to be able to better identify and prioritize GAD projects and programs; and (c) continue its efforts in gender mainstreaming to be able to institutionalize GAD within the whole community for the benefit of not only a particular gender or sector, but for the benefit and equality of all.
We recommended that management comply with the minimum limit of P3,500.00 for employees’ monthly net take home pay pursuant to Section 52 of GAA 2016.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRM Officer to prepare a monthly Report on the Utilization of DRRMF certified correct by the Accountant, and submit to COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant assist him in preparing the report.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE), as chairman of the MDRRMC, cause the selection and accreditation of CSOs in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the aforementioned M.C.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive a) instruct the Treasury Office to monitor compliance with contract provisions by the lessees; b) see to it that he is not re-entering into a contract with a defaulting lessee; and c) include a penalty provision in the MOA in case of a default to make the lessee pay his obligation on time. We recommend further that the Mayor’s Office comply with the provisions of COA Circular on the submission of contracts.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly comply with Revenue Regulation No. 2-28 dated April 7, 1998, as amended by Revenue Regulation Nos. 14-2002 and 10-2008 dated September 9, 2002 and July 8, 2008, respectively, in the withholding and remittance of taxes to avoid the misuse of the same.
We recommended that GSIS premiums deducted be fully remitted intact within the prescribed period provided for under the aforementioned law and be properly monitored to avoid over-remittance.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the supporting
documents for some entries in the General Journal and the schedules or list of some
General Ledgers accounts, use Liquidation Report in the liquidation of
prepayments for travelling expenses, prepare the Subsidiary Ledgers correctly and
rectify the other deficiencies noted. Likewise, include data for the Special Education
Fund in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and justify
the differences noted.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Reconciliation of
Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit) and the
Reconciliation between actual amounts on a comparable basis as presented in the
Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and in the Statement of
Financial Performance. Henceforth, strictly comply the provisions of the Philippine
Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) in the preparation of the financial
statements.

We recommend that management submit the lacking supporting documents;
rectify the deficiencies noted; refrain from having transactions with suppliers where
pecuniary interest exist; require the contractors/suppliers to issue official receipt in
gross amount; and implement its projects within the specified contract time.
Henceforth, refrain from payment and undergoing procurement process without
complete documentations, and strictly comply the provisions of RA 9184 and its
IRR.
We also recommend that management submit justification for the early
period of action on procurement activities; for the early completion of the
Construction of San Lazaro Seawall (Phase 4); for the hiring of a legal consultant
without the proper procurement process; and why COA was not informed of the
deliveries within 24 hours from acceptance.
Likewise, we recommend that official responsible for accepting deliveries of
procured items furnish COA with delivery documents within twenty-four (24) hours
after such acceptance.
These recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension, Notice of Disallowance and Notice of Charge as a result of evaluation of
the documents and justifications to be submitted by the municipality.

We recommend that the management submit the required Memorandum of
Agreement/Trust Agreement of the different projects as well as the Project Briefs
and Local Poverty Reduction Action Plan (LPRAP) for the livelihood projects.
Likewise, submit the lacking supporting documents and justifications as well as
rectify the other deficiencies noted. Further, we recommend that projects be
implemented upon receipt of the funds for the benefit of the
constituents/beneficiaries.

We recommend that management immediately implement the
programs/projects/ activities appropriated under the 20% development
fund and realign the unimplemented programs/projects/activities which
were not in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 to help achieve
desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.
We also recommend that management submit the Memorandum of
Agreement with the source agency for the fund transfers totaling
P1,012,257.45 and justify the need for the daily maintenance of the
projects totaling P1,638,800.00. Likewise, we recommend that the
Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entry for the
erroneous recording of expenses totaling P869,520.00.

We recommend that management efficiently utilize the 70% Mitigation
Fund of the LDRRMF, rectify the deficiencies noted and submit the lacking
supporting documents. Likewise, we recommend that the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction Management Officer prepare the required reports and submit the same
in the prescribed format and period; the Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting
entries for the CY 2016 Capital Outlay which was transferred to Trust Fund and
strictly observe the prescribed recording and reporting guidelines; and the Property
Officer maintain separate registry and prepare the required monthly reports on the
receipt and issuance of in-kind donations. These recommendations are without
prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Notice of Disallowance as a
result of the post audit of the required documents.

We recommend that the Municipal School Board, in preparing their budget,
give priority to the programs/projects/activities which are the immediate needs of
the students and will enhance their learning skills as envisioned by RA 5447.
Likewise, we recommend that management refrain from granting cash advances to
persons not connected with the municipality, rectify the deficiencies noted, submit
the lacking supporting documents, transact with bonafide suppliers, and discontinue
paying previous year transactions which were not obligated. Henceforth, strictly
adhere the provisions of Sections 100 (a) and (c) and 272 of Republic Act 7160 and
RA 5447.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management Board actively perform
the functions required by law. Likewise, we recommend that management provide
appropriation and implement the projects/programs/activities in the Annual
Investment Plan for the solid waste management program as well as execute the 10-
year Solid Waste Management Plan. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of
RA 9003 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We recommend that management furnish the audit team with the advice on
the review by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan and the Local Finance Committee
(LFC) of the Province of Northern Samar on the appropriation ordinances.
Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of Sec. 327 of RA 7160 or the Local
Government Code.

We recommended that: Real properties such as land, land improvements, infrastructur e assets and buildings and other structures totaling ₱49,249,714.98 which comprised about 49% of the total PPE which can be readily located and accounted for be included in the succeeding physical count of the Municipality’s property. The above-mentioned Department Heads investigate and exert efforts in locating the property and documents corresponding to the discrepancies between the accounting and property records; and thereafter prepare the adjusting/correcting entries for the accurate presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Designated General Services and City Accounting and Internal Audit Department exerted efforts in reconciling the discrepancies of ₱87,985,110.51 which significantly decreased by ₱73,108,421.58 in CY 2016 to ₱14,876,688.93.
observations.reason: The Municipality explained that the failure of reconciliation of such with the accounting records was the assigned personnel for PPE was loaded with heavy responsibilities, thus pressure of equally important matters and sufficient time to undertake the process of reconciliation. They further commented that for each change of administration, a new Property/Supply Officer was designated, thus, there is no continuity of plans and actions. Besides, there is no proper turn-over of records, leaving no records on actions undertaken by these Officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw Journal Entry Voucher to transfer fund from the Trust Fund to the General Fund to adjust the affected account for the fair presentation in the financial statements. We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer adopt measures to properly monitor the appropriations to facilitate and ensure an accurate basis in certifying availability of appropriations and allotments for obligations and expenditures on a per PPA basis.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant adjusted the Trust Liability – DRRMF account and transfer the amount of ₱326,525.20 from trust fund to general fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the DRRM Officer explain the charging of expenses totaling ₱8,550.98 and Management refrain from releasing fund without the prescribed supporting documents and strictly follow the guidelines provided in the above-cited circular.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that the actions done by the Municipality were considered as preemptive measures. The Designated MDRRMO assures that prescribed guidelines, rules and regulations will be carefully observed in the disbursement of calamity fund. In CY 2016, QRF was not utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the concerned Department Heads fully implement the mandated development projects. We further recommended that the Municipal Development Council evaluate the Municipality’s Comprehensive Development Plan to check whether the implementation of the proposed projects are still relevant to the current situation and their priority, otherwise, revision and/or upgrading of the development plan should be made for its efficient and effective implementation. More so, the Management to revisit its organizational structure and staffing pattern especially the Municipal Engineering Office to address the issue of lack of manpower and provide them with comfortable working environment for an effective accomplishment of their workloads.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer to strictly comply with all the provisions of the aforementioned COA Circular by submitting the list of all ongoing and those to be implemented government PPAs to the COA and posting of project signboards and/or public notices within the time specified and likewise informing the Team for timely validation of the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer assigned staff to closely monitor for compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2014. He also requested to the Municipal Mayor for filling up the newly created position of Engineer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board revisit the SWM Plan and revise it by incorporating therein all the requirements prescribed in Section 17 of RA No. 9003, and thereafter, submit the same to the NSWMC for approval. We also recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board monitor and review the targets and strategies to ensure efficient management of the Municipality’s SWM program.
observations.reason: The MENRO is only designated and loaded with heavy responsibilities, thus pressure of equally important matters and sufficient time to revise the SWMP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board monitor and evaluate the implementation of the temporary disposal facility in the privately owned lot and see to it that the requirements prescribed in RA No. 9003 was observed, especially securing ECC from the DENR. In case no ECC will be issued, the dumping of wastes in the privately owned lot must be discontinued and the Municipality to look for appropriate dumping site. Adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current temporary disposal facility.
observations.managementAction:
The present Municipal Official explained that they inherited the said practices from previous administration.
The following are their solutions: (a) upgrading of the Municipal Material Recovery Facility; (b) Soon to be used Segregate Dump Truck Machine; (c) Enforce "Basura ko, Ligpit ko" Program and enforce segregation of biodegradable and non-biodegradable program; (d) Convene in urgent the Municipal SWMB for Consultation and Planning; and (e) Temporary use of San Cristobal dumpsite by virtue of the notice to proceed issued by the DENR dated April 29, 2003.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue and institute written policies and guidelines on the hiring of JO services which should include the basis in the hiring of the said personnel. Also, to provide job descriptions to categorically establish duties and responsibilities of the job order personnel.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality approved and adopted the Local Legislative and Executive Development Advisory Council Resolution No. 001-2016 dated August 10, 2016 and Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. 070-2016 dated October 10, 2016 for guidelines on hiring job order personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, who is also the designated General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant assigned staff to determine the complete particulars and cost of the unserviceable properties; prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property for approval of proper authorities; the Committee on Appraisal of Property appraise those property and request the disposal of the same pursuant to COA rules and regulations to prevent further deterioration and to realize income from the sale thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Designated General Services Officer prepared the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for approval of the Municipal Mayor.
observations.reason: Listed unserviceable motor vehicles awaits disposal pending the creation of the Committee on Appraisal of Property.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board revisit the SWM Plan and revise it by incorporating therein all the requirements prescribed in Section 17 of RA No. 9003, and thereafter, submit the same to the NSWMC for approval. We also recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board monitor and review the targets and strategies to ensure efficient management of the Municipality’s SWM program.
observations.reason: The MENRO is only designated and loaded with heavy responsibilities, thus pressure of equally important matters and sufficient time to revise the SWMP. The Solid Waste Management Board is not regularly conducted meeting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze, confirm and ascertain the validity of the long outstanding liabilities in the Trust Fund book and if found with valid claims notify the claimants, for other payables account, if found without valid documentation and/or which have been outstanding for more than two years or more and against which no actual claim has been filed, proper adjustment in the books of accounts can be made and return the amount to the source agencies or to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation on-going.
observations.reason: Unavailability of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GFPS and the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that sufficient amount is appropriated for the formulation of appropriate questionnaire/tools to gather sex-disaggregated data and other necessary information for the setting up of GAD database, and GFPS properly monitor the implementation of GPB and ensure that the same is optimally utilized to address the identified GAD issues and concerns and to promote the welfare of the women in the locality.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2016 out of the ₱10,801,629.20 appropriation for GAD 19.16% or ₱2,069,916.29 were not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that that an updated project reports covering the whole period of operation be rendered by the concerned Municipal and MinSCAT officials/employees. Records, particularly subsidiary ledgers, showing the construction cost of these capital assets, a clear financial report on the results of the project operation and inventory reports should be produced for further audit. Thereafter, any balance of cash should be turned over to the Municipality while the fixed assets should be transferred to MinSCAT where they have been erected. The corresponding accounting entries as these procedures are complied with would logically close the erroneous Investment account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality coordinated with the MinSCAT officials/employee relative to the records regarding this investment. Records, particularly subsidiary ledgers, showing the construction cost of these capital assets, a clear financial report on the results of the project operation and inventory reports should be produced for further audit. Thereafter, any balance of cash should be turned over to the Municipality while the fixed assets should be transferred to MinSCAT where they have been erected. The corresponding accounting entries as these procedures are complied with would logically close the erroneous Investment account.
observations.reason: Retrieval of various records pertaining to construction cost and other related disbursements is still on going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive would conduct the periodic physical inventory and prepare the corresponding report.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015
observations.reason: Management did not create an inventory team that would conduct the periodic physical inventory and prepare the corresponding report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, and require all officials and employees concerned to settle/liquidate their outstanding cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015
observations.reason: Not all cash advances were liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Health Officer to adhere to Executive Order No. 49 and DOH Administrative Order No. 163 series of 2002, for all requisition and purchase of drugs and medicines.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prepare Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and to prepare the Driver’s Trip Tickets using the form prescribed in COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977.
observations.reason: No Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the recipient officials and employees be required to refund the amount in excess of the authorized amount as prescribed in E.O. No. 80 and DBM BC No. 2014-3.
observations.reason: Refunds. Some recipients refunded; management informed the audit team that they will start to refund in 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and Accountant refrain from granting cash advances for pre-determined expenditures which should be paid by check, see to it that all cash advances are liquidated at the end of each year and henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.reason: Some cash advances still unliquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to strictly observe the timely submission of accounts and financial reports in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, Section 70 of Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs and Sections 2 and 4 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010.
observations.reason: Late submission of some reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management secure approval from DOF to maintain depository account with Philippine National Bank, otherwise affected depository account may have to be closed pursuant to DOF Order No. 27-05 dated December 9, 2005.
We recommend that management strictly observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.reason: Did not submit all the reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit bank reconciliation statements for the SEF and Trust Fund and to draw journal entries to record valid reconciling items for all funds in accordance with Sections 3.2 and 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.reason: Late submission of some bank reconciliation for the Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officers and employees to strictly comply with the provisions of E.O. 298, and stop the practice of issuing travel authority for attendance to conventions of organizations/leagues which the personnel is not affiliated in order to preclude unnecessary travel expenses. Likewise, strictly observe the proper classification of accounts as provided in the NGAS Manual.
observations.reason: Some concerned personnel refunded. Stop the practice of issuing travel authority for organizations to which personnel is not affiliated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management comply with the provisions of RA 656.
observations.reason: Failure of the LGU to update valuation of the insurable properties.
